Document map — six instruments, one suite

COI · cautionarySPA · cautionaryIRA · cautionaryVA · cautionaryROFR · cautionaryMRL · cautionary

Chips carry each document's Mode-1 verdict word; configuration qualifiers stay on the document section heads below.

Stack geometry panel · SUITE rows (Stack – Suite Scoring refresh)

SUITE-A — Lower-risk standard financingcautionary

The assembled best-self fill. Sharpened: no licit suite configuration exists — the one-way scoping is irreducible

SUITE-B — Preference stack + dragpotentially extractive

The co-sale waterfall import routes around the drag's protective conditions (the VA protection thins at suite level)

SUITE-C — Redemption / preference liquiditypotentially extractive

Charter redemption + IRA demand machinery: liquidity pressure both directions

SUITE-D — Investor veto governancepotentially extractive

Protective provisions + designated seats + [the exact board as funding condition]; five consent vocabularies

SUITE-E — Shadow governance (info + side letters)potentially extractive

The complete valve: charter waiver + IRA channels-and-waiver + MRL + [observer]/[platform]/M&A-drafts + Side Letters registry

SUITE-F — Founder / employee lock-inpotentially extractive

SV14-01 four limbs + gauntlet + ratchet + at-cost channel + conscription; the 1.45 suite flag engaged

SUITE-G — Dilution burden shiftcautionary

Majors-only pro rata + anti-dilution + pool provisions: participation up, dilution down

SUITE-H — Tranche / SMC / shortfallcautionary

New Oct-2025 architecture: double capital-side milestone gate; two SMC species, each with its protective device

SUITE-I — SAFE / note conversioncautionary

Deemed amendment [on behalf of all holders] + full discharge; the SAFE-compatibility seam

SUITE-J — Commons tensioncautionary

Commons-as-contamination framing in the warranty layer; no enclosure machinery operates

SUITE-K — Full high-risk configurationsevere

B+C+D+E+F assembled: the four-limb leaver machine, the funding trap, the shadow government, the waterfall import

P8 · Check 1 — defined-term drift

TermSitesClassification
Requisite HoldersCOI 2.3.1 inline electors · VA (g) preferred-majority · IRA 1.32 two-phase — one name, three referentsLOAD-BEARING homonym drift
The five-vocabulary aggregate+ SPA 'Requisite Purchasers' · ROFR's unnamed percentage keys — five consent computations for one classload-bearing aggregate
Qualified Key HolderVA 'is' vs ROFR 'means' — definition tails byte-identicalbenign copula drift
CompetitorIRA 1.5 ≡ ROFR 1.7, normalized-equal (20% fund carve-out + [named exception] both sites)verbatim parity
Sanctioned PartyVA = ROFR = IRA byte-identical bodies; the SPA runs its own sanctions reps insteadparity + vocabulary independence
Deemed Liquidation Eventhome in the charter (48 mentions); ROFR/IRA defer expressly, VA within 'Sale of the Company'grounded deference
Transfer Stock · Major Investorsingle-document scopings, stated — the one-way thesis and the master dialdeliberate scoping
Jury waiverVA · ROFR · SPA · IRA — four instances, bodies byte-identical after the outline label; label case splits by vintagewording parity

ReferenceResult

IRA 4.1 → VA (joinder condition)RESOLVED
IRA 1.7 → COI (DLE deference)RESOLVED
IRA 1.42 → SPA (Side Letters import)RESOLVED
IRA 5.x → VA (Sale of the Company)RESOLVED
SPA 2.x ↔ IRA 5.x (QSBS rep/covenant twin)RESOLVED

Zero dangling, stale, or circular references. Check 2 (topology): Key Holder party status varies with direction of load — full parties where bound (VA, ROFR), bracketed where benefited (IRA), absent where the money is (SPA). Board arithmetic: three sites, configuration-conditional rule recorded.

P8 · Check 5 — spine projection (promises traced from the SPA)

Promise (spine locus)VerdictTrace
Pari passu within the class (SPA 6.10)driftedsame-fashion floors real; per-transfer waivers, [sole-discretion release], waiver-by-silence remain
Pro-rata participation (SPA 1.2 / Exh. A)expandedIRA 4.1 delivers to Majors only, with super-allocation
Information boundary (SPA 2.36)expandedchannel widens for the large; the statutory floor is removed beneath the small
Transfer-freedom residue (SPA 2.2(c))driftedKH residue = estate planning + [carve-out (e)] + the at-cost treasury door; investor residue broader
Penalty proportionality (SPA 1.2(c)(iv) + cap)deliveredstrip delivered cross-document; ratio variance is configuration
Exit-value allocation (SPA Exh. B waterfall)expandedROFR imports the waterfall into private co-sales — beyond the spine's DLE scope

InstrumentAmender / thresholdAmendable WITHOUT them

NVCA Model Certificate of Incorporation (Updated Oct 2025)

Risk: cautionary (configuration-pending; PE at standard fill)Sim (disclosed): 1.0122Mode-2 D: 0.038420 scored rows

The corporate layer the suite's contracts assume: waterfall, protective provisions, pay-to-play SMC, and the opportunity waiver (Article 18) that the information valve rides.

MG-NVCA-COI-01OPERATIVE-HEAD, ART-FIRST, ART-SECOND, ART-THIRDcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Identity and the purpose-empty charter

No purposive constraint on enterprise ends: the charter names no mission, no beneficiary class, no commons; whatever the control map produces is what the enterprise is for.

Catechism: purports corporate identity; functions as an unconstrained enterprise shell — no fundus, no destination.Geometry: mission_lock_conflict · control_without_stewardshipStack: Mission integrity stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 11350–12763
… the consent of the stockholders therefor, which resolution setting forth the proposed amendment and restatement is as follows:
RESOLVED, that the Certificate of Incorporation of this corporation be amended and restated in its entirety to read as follows:
FIRST: The name of this corporation is [_______________] (the “Corporation”).
SECOND: The address of the registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware is [_____________], in the City of [__________], County of [__________]. The name of its registered agent at such address is [_____________________].
THIRD: The nature of the business or purposes to be conducted or promoted is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law.
FOURTH: The total number of shares of all classes[FN id=5] of stock which the Corporation shall have the authority to issue is [______]. The Corporation has [two] classes of stock, referred to as Common Stock and Preferred Stock. There are [_____] shares of authorized Common Stock, $[_____] par value per share (“Common Stock”),[FN id=6] and [______] shares of authorized Preferred Stock, $[______] par value per share (“Preferred Stock”), [all] of which are hereby designated as “Series A Preferred Stock”.
The following is a statement of the designations and the powers, preferences and special rights, and the qualifications, limitations or res…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports corporate identity; functions as an unconstrained enterprise shell — no fundus, no destination.

3. Title breakdown

Mission-lock conflictprimary category
Moral hinge
Mission is not merely branding; it must govern capital.
Legitimate form
Asset locks; purpose protections; vetoes against mission drift; commons carveouts; stakeholder accountability.
Abuse form
Exit, IP, data, or investor control rights detach value from mission and community.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Charter identity provisions and the DGCL §102(a)(3) general-purpose clause; amendment-and-restatement lineage recitals.
Economic
No purposive constraint on enterprise ends: the charter names no mission, no beneficiary class, no commons; whatever the control map produces is what the enterprise is for.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the Delaware statutory minimum, universal in the genre; nothing extractive in the words. Cautionary: purpose-emptiness renders every later right end-agnostic — the charter cannot distinguish stewardship from extraction because it names no end to steward; mission-protection absence scored 3.
  • Configuration to watch: Score mission-protection absence at suite level too (P8): does ANY suite instrument name an end the control map must serve? (Expected: no — MRL comes closest and names only the investor's regulatory need.)

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law”

7. Framework / suite notes

Licit-Pole Rubric purpose-primacy item read in reverse; AUTH-CST-CA35 (Verified) — the firm as community of persons has no textual purchase here; v1.4 mission-protection-absence dimension is the scoring vehicle.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
THIRD: The nature of the business or purposes to be conducted or promoted is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law.
Notes
Catechism: purports corporate identity; functions as an unconstrained enterprise shell — no fundus, no destination.
MG-NVCA-COI-02ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Authorized capital and designation (the capital envelope)

The capital envelope and issuance headroom are pure per-deal configuration; authorization sizing is the quiet enabler of every later dilution mechanic (pool, conversions, next rounds).

Catechism: purports capital definition; functions as the configuration space for dilution capacity.Geometry: valuation_control · governance_asymmetryStack: Economics / priority stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 11473–12901
…ows:
RESOLVED, that the Certificate of Incorporation of this corporation be amended and restated in its entirety to read as follows:
FIRST: The name of this corporation is [_______________] (the “Corporation”).
SECOND: The address of the registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware is [_____________], in the City of [__________], County of [__________]. The name of its registered agent at such address is [_____________________].
THIRD: The nature of the business or purposes to be conducted or promoted is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law.
FOURTH: The total number of shares of all classes[FN id=5] of stock which the Corporation shall have the authority to issue is [______]. The Corporation has [two] classes of stock, referred to as Common Stock and Preferred Stock. There are [_____] shares of authorized Common Stock, $[_____] par value per share (“Common Stock”),[FN id=6] and [______] shares of authorized Preferred Stock, $[______] par value per share (“Preferred Stock”), [all] of which are hereby designated as “Series A Preferred Stock”.
The following is a statement of the designations and the powers, preferences and special rights, and the qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof in respect of each class of capital stock of the Corporation. 
A.	COMMON STOCK
1.	General. The voting, dividend and liqu…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports capital definition; functions as the configuration space for dilution capacity.

3. Title breakdown

Valuation controlprimary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
DGCL §102(a)(4) authorized-capital provision; class/series designation.
Economic
The capital envelope and issuance headroom are pure per-deal configuration; authorization sizing is the quiet enabler of every later dilution mechanic (pool, conversions, next rounds).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: authorization is not issuance; standard mechanics. Cautionary: every number is a blank — the envelope's moral load is set per deal, and headroom sized far above need removes a natural refinancing checkpoint.
  • Configuration to watch: At configuration: is authorized headroom sized to the round or to future rounds without further Common consent (with COI-03's bracket included)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “The total number of shares of all classes[FN id=5] of stock which the Corporation shall have the authority to issue is [______].”

7. Framework / suite notes

Framework support: general (authorized-capital sizing); interacts with COI-03's vote override and Section 4.4's Exempted Securities.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
FOURTH: The total number of shares of all classes of stock which the Corporation shall have the authority to issue is [______]. [two] classes: Common and Preferred, all Preferred designated Series A.
Notes
Catechism: purports capital definition; functions as the configuration space for dilution capacity.
MG-NVCA-COI-03ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Common Stock rights and the §242(b)(2) class-vote override

Removes Common's structural veto on expanding the share pie: dilution capacity moves to the combined as-converted majority, which the preferred class can dominate; the one brake Common holds by statute is released by bracket.

Catechism: purports voting mechanics; functions as transfer of the dilution gate from the residual class to the priority Geometry: governance_asymmetry · valuation_controlStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 13840–15222
…nt to this Certificate of Incorporation or pursuant to the General Corporation Law].[FN id=7] [There shall be no cumulative voting.][FN id=8] [The number of authorized shares of Common Stock may be increased or decreased (but not below the number of shares thereof then outstanding) by (in addition to any vote of the holders of one or more series of Preferred Stock that may be required by the terms of this Certificate of Incorporation) the affirmative vote of the holders of shares of capital stock of the Corporation representing a majority of the votes represented by all outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote, irrespective of the provisions of Section 242(b)(2) of the General Corporation Law.][FN id=9]
B.	PREFERRED STOCK
The shares of the Preferred Stock shall have the powers, preferences and special rights set forth in this Part B of this Article Fourth. Unless otherwise indicated, references to “sections” or “Sections” in this Part B of this Article Fourth refer to sections of Part B of this Article Fourth. References to “Preferred Stock” mean the Series A Preferred Stock.
1.	Dividends.[FN id=10]
[Use the following paragraph if the Term Sheet calls for no specific dividend on the Preferred Stock, but an equal sharing if dividends are declared on the Common Stock.]
The Corporation shall not declare, pay or set aside any dividen…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 138978–140384
…der has given notice at the meeting, prior to the voting, of such stockholder’s intention to cumulate such stockholder’s votes. If any stockholder has given proper notice to cumulate votes, all stockholders may cumulate their votes for any candidates who have been properly placed in nomination.” Under cumulative voting, the candidates receiving the highest number of votes, up to the number of directors to be elected, are elected.
[FN id=9 seq=8] Including the final bracketed sentence requires the approval of the holders of Common Stock unless such provision was contained in the original Certificate of Incorporation. Without such a provision, the holders of the Common Stock effectively retain a class protective vote on subsequent equity financings if the number of authorized shares of Common Stock would need to be increased to accommodate the conversion rights of securities issued in such financings. Similar language should be considered vis-à-vis Preferred Stockholders if they are not intended to have a block on future financings. See also footnote 4.
[FN id=10 seq=9] This model charter provides for three bracketed alternative dividend provisions. There are a variety of other alternatives that may be encountered in the marketplace, but the drafter should be able to easily adapt one of the three provisions to provide for whatever dividend terms are applicable to a particular transacti…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports voting mechanics; functions as transfer of the dilution gate from the residual class to the priority class.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Valuation controlsecondary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.

4. Functions

Legal
Common voting rights; opt-out of the DGCL §242(b)(2) separate class vote on changes to authorized Common Stock.
Economic
Removes Common's structural veto on expanding the share pie: dilution capacity moves to the combined as-converted majority, which the preferred class can dominate; the one brake Common holds by statute is released by bracket.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: without the bracket, Common keeps its statutory class vote. Cautionary: with it, founders/employees lose the one charter-level dilution gate they hold as a class; PE in configuration where combined with large authorization headroom and the 4.4 machinery.
  • Configuration to watch: Included or not at configuration? If included: was the trade priced (FN 9's own question), and does the Voting Agreement then bind Common votes on increases too (P8: rights-accumulation)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “irrespective of the provisions of Section 242(b)(2) of the General Corporation Law”
  • “the holders of the Common Stock effectively retain a class protective vote on subsequent equity financings”

7. Framework / suite notes

FN 9 (drafting commentary, in-source) names the trade itself — the second span above is the drafters' own statement of what the bracket removes.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Part A: one vote per Common share; bracketed: no vote on preferred-only charter amendments; bracketed: authorized Common may be increased/decreased by combined majority vote irrespective of DGCL §242(b)(2); bracketed: no cumulative voting.
Notes
Catechism: purports voting mechanics; functions as transfer of the dilution gate from the residual class to the priority class. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-04ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Dividends: parity, preferential, or cumulative accrual (three alternatives)

Alternative 1 keeps dividend parity (societas form). Alternative 2 adds priority but no accrual. Alternative 3 attaches a day-count time-charge to the residual: value accrues to capital by the calendar, whether or not profits exist — and FN 15 wires the accrual into liquidation and redemption payouts.

Catechism: purports dividend preference (societas); alternative 3 functions mutuum-like — the batch's clearest inversion Geometry: carentia_pecuniae · surplus_asymmetryStack: Economics / priority stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 20964–22430
…s sentence of Section 1 shall be calculated based upon the dividend on the class or series of capital stock that would result in the highest dividend for such series of Preferred Stock. The “Original Issue Price” shall mean, with respect to the Series A Preferred Stock, $[insert initial Series A purchase price] per share, subject to appropriate adjustment in the event of any stock dividend, stock split, combination or other similar recapitalization with respect to the applicable Preferred Stock. The “Dividend Amount” shall mean, with respect to any series of Preferred Stock, [8]% of the Original Issue Price of such series of Preferred Stock.
[Use the following paragraph if the Term Sheet calls for a specified cumulative dividend, payable if and when declared by the Board, in addition to shared dividends.]
From and after the date of the issuance of any shares of Preferred Stock, dividends at the rate per annum of $[___] per share[FN id=14] shall accrue on such shares of Preferred Stock (subject to appropriate adjustment in the event of any stock dividend, stock split, combination or other similar recapitalization with respect to the Preferred Stock) (the “Accruing Dividends”). Accruing Dividends shall accrue from day to day, whether or not declared, and shall be cumulative; provided, however, that except as set forth in the following sentence of this Section 1 [or in Section 2.1 [and Section 6.1]][FN id=15], such Accruing Dividends shall be pa…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 21509–22906
…ct to any series of Preferred Stock, [8]% of the Original Issue Price of such series of Preferred Stock.
[Use the following paragraph if the Term Sheet calls for a specified cumulative dividend, payable if and when declared by the Board, in addition to shared dividends.]
From and after the date of the issuance of any shares of Preferred Stock, dividends at the rate per annum of $[___] per share[FN id=14] shall accrue on such shares of Preferred Stock (subject to appropriate adjustment in the event of any stock dividend, stock split, combination or other similar recapitalization with respect to the Preferred Stock) (the “Accruing Dividends”). Accruing Dividends shall accrue from day to day, whether or not declared, and shall be cumulative; provided, however, that except as set forth in the following sentence of this Section 1 [or in Section 2.1 [and Section 6.1]][FN id=15], such Accruing Dividends shall be payable only when, as, and if declared by the Board of Directors and, except as provided in Section 2.1 [and Section 6.1], the Corporation shall be under no obligation to pay such Accruing Dividends. The Corporation shall not declare, pay or set aside any dividends on shares of any other class or series of capital stock of the Corporation (other than dividends on shares of Common Stock payable in shares of Common Stock) unless (in addition to the obtaining of any consents r…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 141313–142772
… be expressed as a percentage of a “base amount,” with the “base amount” defined as the original purchase price plus the amount of previously accrued dividends (it should also be specified whether this “compounding” of the original purchase price is done on an annual, quarterly, or other basis). As an example: “From and after the date of the issuance of any shares of Preferred Stock, dividends at the rate per annum of [8]% of the Original Issue Price (as defined below) of such share, plus the amount of previously accrued dividends, compounded annually, shall accrue on each share then outstanding (the “Accruing Dividends”).”
[FN id=15 seq=14] Insert the bracketed language if the holders of Preferred Stock will receive the benefit of the accruing dividends upon a liquidation event or upon redemption.
[FN id=16 seq=15] The second bracketed alternative may not be sensible with respect to dividends paid on another series of Preferred Stock, particularly one which contains a reciprocal dividend provision, as it may result in an endless series of payments (as any payment with respect to one series would trigger a payment with respect to the other). This provision (and the similar provision in the first alternative dividend paragraph) are likely to be of little practicable import, as most venture capital-backed companies do not have the cash flow to make any dividend payments, and the negative covenants (contained in Section 3.3) prohibit divi…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports dividend preference (societas); alternative 3 functions mutuum-like — the batch's clearest inversion instance.

3. Title breakdown

Carentia pecuniaeprimary category
Moral hinge
Mere lack of money is not enough; absence must not collapse into universal time-value entitlement.
Legitimate form
Only under narrowly justified, verified, non-universal conditions if accepted by a reviewed moral regime.
Abuse form
Every loan becomes interest-bearing by nature; restorative compensation collapses into usurious yield.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Dividend preference provisions; alternative drafting per term-sheet election; anti-circumvention parity formulas across classes.
Economic
Alternative 1 keeps dividend parity (societas form). Alternative 2 adds priority but no accrual. Alternative 3 attaches a day-count time-charge to the residual: value accrues to capital by the calendar, whether or not profits exist — and FN 15 wires the accrual into liquidation and redemption payouts.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: alternative 1; declared-only, non-cumulative alternative 2 in ordinary form. Cautionary: alternative 3's accrual is time-priced return divorced from declared profits. Potentially extractive (configuration): accrual + FN 14 compounding + liquidation/redemption wiring = interest-bearing principal in equity dress.
  • Configuration to watch: Which alternative at configuration; if (3): rate, compounding basis (FN 14), and whether accrual feeds Section 2.1 and 6.1 (FN 15/18 brackets).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “[Use the following paragraph if the Term Sheet calls for a specified cumulative dividend, payable if and when declared by the Board, in addition to sh…”
  • “Accruing Dividends shall accrue from day to day, whether or not declared, and shall be cumulative”
  • “Insert the bracketed language if the holders of Preferred Stock will receive the benefit of the accruing dividends upon a liquidation event or upon re…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Vix Pervenit §3.III (AUTH-BXIV-VP, Verified) marks the boundary: titles must answer real loss/risk/labor — a day-count accrual on equity answers the calendar; catechism inversion (purports societas, functions mutuum-like) per extension-plan §2.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Three alternatives: (1) no preferred dividend, equal-sharing if declared on Common; (2) [8]% of OIP preferential, non-cumulative, when-as-if declared; (3) cumulative Accruing Dividends at $[___]/share/annum, accruing day by day whether or not declared.
Notes
Catechism: purports dividend preference (societas); alternative 3 functions mutuum-like — the batch's clearest inversion instance. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-05ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Liquidation preference: multiple, greater-of, and the participating alternative

Priority return of capital (plus multiple, plus any accrual per COI-04) ahead of the residual class; the participating alternative takes preference AND upside — the double claim.

Catechism: purports preferred equity (societas); functions mutuum-like exactly in proportion to multiple/participation/acGeometry: periculum_sortis · surplus_asymmetryStack: Economics / priority stackConfidence: High

… paid out of the assets of the Corporation available for distribution to its stockholders, and (b) a Deemed Liquidation Event (as defined below), the holders of shares of each series of Preferred Stock then outstanding shall be entitled to be paid out of the consideration payable to stockholders in such Deemed Liquidation Event or out of the Available Proceeds (as defined below), as applicable, on a pari passu basis based on their respective Liquidation Amounts (as defined below) and before any payment shall be made to the holders of Common Stock by reason of their ownership thereof, an amount per share of each such series of Preferred Stock equal to the greater of (i) [__ times] the applicable Original Issue Price, plus any dividends declared but unpaid thereon,[FN id=18] or (ii) such amount per share as would have been payable had all shares of such series of Preferred Stock (and all shares of all other series of Preferred Stock that would receive a larger distribution per share if such series of Preferred Stock were converted into Common Stock) been converted into Common Stock pursuant to Section 4 immediately prior to such liquidation, dissolution, winding up or Deemed Liquidation Event (the amount payable pursuant to this sentence is hereinafter referred to, for each series of Preferred Stock, as applicable, as the “Liquidation Amount”). Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 30096–31642
…distribution if all amounts payable on or with respect to such shares were paid in full.
2.2	Distribution of Remaining Assets. In the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution or winding up of the Corporation, after the payment in full of all amounts required to be paid to the holders of shares of Preferred Stock pursuant to Section 2.1, the remaining assets of the Corporation available for distribution to its stockholders or, in the case of a Deemed Liquidation Event, the consideration not payable to the holders of shares of Preferred Stock pursuant to Section 2.1 or the remaining Available Proceeds, as the case may be, shall be distributed among the holders of the shares of Preferred Stock and Common Stock, pro rata based on the number of shares held by each such holder, treating for this purpose all such securities as if they had been converted to Common Stock pursuant to the terms of this Certificate of Incorporation immediately prior to such liquidation, dissolution or winding up of the Corporation.[FN id=21] The aggregate amount which a holder of a share of Preferred Stock is entitled to receive under Sections 2.1 and 2.2 is hereinafter referred to as the “Liquidation Amount.”
2.3	Deemed Liquidation Events.[FN id=22]
2.3.1	Definition. Each of the following events shall be considered a “Deemed Liquidation Event” unless the holders of at least [specify percentage][FN id=23],[FN id=24] of the outstanding shares of Preferred Stock, voting together as a single class on an as-converted to Common Stoc…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports preferred equity (societas); functions mutuum-like exactly in proportion to multiple/participation/accrual. Quant: exit-waterfall module named, inputs blank, scenarios configuration-pending.

3. Title breakdown

Periculum sortisprimary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Liquidation preference and distribution waterfall for liquidation, dissolution, winding up, and Deemed Liquidation Events; pari passu within preferred.
Economic
Priority return of capital (plus multiple, plus any accrual per COI-04) ahead of the residual class; the participating alternative takes preference AND upside — the double claim.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: 1× non-participating in ordinary form (real capital protection, real conversion choice). Cautionary: blank multiple; greater-of mechanics. Potentially extractive (configuration): multiple >1×, or participating without cap — upside without symmetrical downside (societas leonina direction; AUTH-ROM-SOCIETAS-LEONINA still Draft — no verbatim quotation).
  • Configuration to watch: Multiple filled per deal; participating or NP; if participating, capped? (P0 standing question); waterfall module inputs (stack, pool, caps) once numbers exist.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “equal to the greater of (i) [__ times] the applicable Original Issue Price, plus any dividends declared but unpaid thereon,”
  • “shall be distributed among the holders of the shares of Preferred Stock and Common Stock, pro rata based on the number of shares held by each such hol…”

7. Framework / suite notes

ST II-II q.78 a.2 ad 5 (AUTH-AQU-ST78, Verified): lawful profit-share rides real risk-bearing; a multiple >1× or participation shifts loss to the residual while keeping upside — periculum sortis evacuated in proportion. YCS calibration precedent: 1× NP = commercial norm, cautionary.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Non-participating alternative: preferred paid greater-of([__ times] OIP + declared-unpaid dividends, as-converted amount) before Common; participating alternative: [___ times] OIP + declared-unpaid, THEN pro-rata as-converted share of the remainder.
Notes
Catechism: purports preferred equity (societas); functions mutuum-like exactly in proportion to multiple/participation/accrual. Quant: exit-waterfall module named, inputs blank, scenarios configuration-pending. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-06ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Deemed Liquidation Events: definition, waterfall-forcing, proceeds freeze

Extends the preference to exit-in-substance events (anti-evasion) and converts asset-sale proceeds into a preferred-first fund; the freeze plus the 90/120/150-day clock creates post-sale liquidity pressure with Delaware distribution law as the only brake.

Catechism: purports exit-definition machinery; 2.3.2(b) functions as a conditional put against sale proceeds.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · surplus_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: Medium-high
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 34153–35673
…cense or other disposition is to a wholly owned subsidiary of the Corporation. 
[Notwithstanding anything in Section 2.3.1 to the contrary, in no event shall the sale and issuance by the Corporation of Preferred Stock in a bona fide equity financing of the Corporation, in and of itself, be a Deemed Liquidation Event.][FN id=30] 
2.3.2	Effecting a Deemed Liquidation Event. 
(a)	The Corporation shall not have the power to effect a Deemed Liquidation Event referred to in Section 2.3.1(a)(i) unless the agreement or plan with respect to such transaction, or terms of such transaction (any such agreement, plan or terms, the “Transaction Document”), provide that the consideration payable to the stockholders of the Corporation in such Deemed Liquidation Event shall be allocated to the holders of capital stock of the Corporation in accordance with Sections 2.1 and 2.2.
(b)	[In the event of a Deemed Liquidation Event referred to in Section 2.3.1(a)(ii) or 2.3.1(b), if the Corporation does not effect a dissolution of the Corporation under the General Corporation Law within 90 days after such Deemed Liquidation Event, then (i) the Corporation shall send a written notice to each holder of Preferred Stock no later than the 90th day after the Deemed Liquidation Event advising such holders of their right (and the requirements to be met to secure such right) pursuant to the terms of the following clause (ii) to require the redemption of such shares of Preferred Stock, and (ii) if the Requisite Holders so request …
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 37152–38624
…to the preceding sentence, if the Available Proceeds are not sufficient to redeem all outstanding shares of Preferred Stock, the Corporation shall redeem a pro rata portion of each holder’s shares of Preferred Stock to the fullest extent of such Available Proceeds, based on the respective amounts which would otherwise be payable in respect of the shares to be redeemed if the Available Proceeds were sufficient to redeem all such shares, and shall redeem the remaining shares as soon as it may lawfully do so under Delaware law governing distributions to stockholders. Prior to the distribution or redemption provided for in this Section 2.3.2(b), the Corporation shall not expend or dissipate the Available Proceeds for any purpose, except to discharge expenses incurred in connection with such Deemed Liquidation Event. In connection with a distribution or redemption provided for in Section 2.3.2, the Corporation shall send written notice of the redemption (the “Redemption Notice”) to each holder of record of Preferred Stock. Each Redemption Notice shall state:
(i)	the number of shares of Preferred Stock held by the holder that the Corporation shall redeem on the date specified in the Redemption Notice;
(ii)	the redemption date and the price per share at which the shares of Preferred Stock are being redeemed;
(iii)	for holders of shares in certificated form, that the holder is to surrender to the Corporation, in the manner and at the place designated, his,…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports exit-definition machinery; 2.3.2(b) functions as a conditional put against sale proceeds.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Deemed-liquidation definition and enforcement mechanics; escrow/contingent-consideration allocation (2.3.4); board good-faith valuation of non-cash consideration (2.3.3).
Economic
Extends the preference to exit-in-substance events (anti-evasion) and converts asset-sale proceeds into a preferred-first fund; the freeze plus the 90/120/150-day clock creates post-sale liquidity pressure with Delaware distribution law as the only brake.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: 2.3.2(a)'s waterfall-forcing; the Requisite-Holders opt-out enables preference waiver (FN 23/24). Cautionary: the 2.3.2(b) machinery; board-only valuation of non-cash consideration (FN 31 offers the appraisal alternative). Potentially extractive (configuration): 2.3.2(b) included + short runway = forced wind-down pressure after any major asset transaction.
  • Configuration to watch: 2.3.2(b) bracket in or out; escrow deemed Initial or Additional Consideration (2.3.4 bracket); Preferred-Director approval bracket in 2.3.3?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “provide that the consideration payable to the stockholders of the Corporation in such Deemed Liquidation Event shall be allocated to the holders of ca…”
  • “the Corporation shall not expend or dissipate the Available Proceeds for any purpose, except to discharge expenses incurred in connection with such De…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Double-edged: 2.3.2(a) is allocation honesty (the agreed waterfall cannot be bypassed in the deal document — protective of ALL stockholders' bargain); 2.3.2(b) is the redemption-pressure edge. FN 30 (new Oct 2025) clarifies a pure equity financing is not a DLE — new-surface item for the drift disclosure.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
DLE = mergers/asset sales unless Requisite Holders opt out; 2.3.2(a) bars the Corporation from a merger DLE unless the transaction document allocates consideration per Sections 2.1-2.2; 2.3.2(b) (bracketed): if no dissolution within 90 days of an asset-sale DLE, preferred may force redemption from Available Proceeds on the 150th day; anti-dissipation freeze until then.
Notes
Catechism: purports exit-definition machinery; 2.3.2(b) functions as a conditional put against sale proceeds. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-07ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Voting and the board-election architecture

The board map is the control constitution: who elects whom, in what number, decides which class's judgment governs — every number a per-deal blank; Requisite-Directors gates the 3.3.9 carve-outs and multiple valuation calls.

Catechism: purports governance allocation (societas governance); functions per configuration.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 41677–43219
…convertible (as provided in Section 4 below) as of the record date for determining stockholders entitled to vote on such matter. Except as provided by law or by the other provisions of this Certificate of Incorporation, holders of Preferred Stock shall vote together with the holders of Common Stock as a single class and on an as-converted to Common Stock basis.
3.2	Election of Directors. 
(a)	(i) At all times when at least [__] shares of Preferred Stock remain outstanding (subject to appropriate adjustment in the event of any stock dividend, stock split, combination, or other similar recapitalization with respect to the Preferred Stock), (x) the holders of record of the shares of Preferred Stock, exclusively and voting together as a separate class on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, shall be entitled to elect [__] director[s] of the Corporation (the “Preferred Director[s]”); and (y) the holders of record of the shares of Common Stock, exclusively and voting together as a separate class, shall be entitled to elect [__] directors of the Corporation; and (ii) the holders of record of the shares of Common Stock and of any other class or series of voting stock (including the Preferred Stock), exclusively and voting together as a single class on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, shall be entitled to elect the balance of the total number of directors of the Corporation (the “At-Large Directors”). [Notwithstanding the foregoing, for administrative convenience, the initial Preferred Director[s] may also be appointe…

…the vote or written consent in lieu of a meeting of the stockholders entitled to elect the At-Large Directors, or (B) the vote or written consent in lieu of a meeting of [all/a majority of the] remaining director(s) [elected by the stockholders entitled to elect the At-Large Directors].[FN id=36]  
(d)	At any meeting held for the purpose of electing a director, the presence in person or by proxy of the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of the class or series of capital stock entitled to elect such director shall constitute a quorum for the purpose of electing such director.
(e)	For purposes of this Certificate of Incorporation, “Requisite Directors” means the Board of Directors [including [a majority of] the Preferred Directors then seated].[FN id=37]

At any time when at least [____] shares of Preferred Stock [(subject to appropriate adjustment in the event of any stock dividend, stock split, combination or other similar recapitalization with respect to the Preferred Stock)] are outstanding, the Corporation shall not, either directly or indirectly by amendment, merger, consolidation, domestication, transfer of the Corporation, continuance, reorganization, recapitalization, reclassification, waiver, statutory conversion, or otherwise, effect[FN id=38] any of the following acts or transactions without (in addition to any other vote requ…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports governance allocation (societas governance); functions per configuration.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Class voting rights and board-composition provisions; director removal and vacancy rules; consent-threshold definition used across the charter.
Economic
The board map is the control constitution: who elects whom, in what number, decides which class's judgment governs — every number a per-deal blank; Requisite-Directors gates the 3.3.9 carve-outs and multiple valuation calls.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: separate-class representation is real risk-sharing in governance when balanced. Cautionary: all numbers blank; the Requisite-Directors bracket can make preferred directors a gating sub-quorum inside the board itself.
  • Configuration to watch: Board numbers at configuration (preferred vs common vs at-large); Requisite-Directors bracket in/out; initial-appointment shortcut used?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the holders of record of the shares of Preferred Stock, exclusively and voting together as a separate class on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, …”
  • ““Requisite Directors” means the Board of Directors [including [a majority of] the Preferred Directors then seated]”

7. Framework / suite notes

Board arithmetic must reconcile with the Voting Agreement's designation covenants — P8 check 2 (topology) carries it; FN 37 (thresholds customizable) and FN 33 (quorum interaction, DGCL §141(b)) are the configuration seams.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
As-converted voting generally (3.1); separate-class election: preferred elect [__] Preferred Director(s), Common elect [__], balance elected jointly (At-Large); removal only by the electing class; vacancy mechanics; bracketed initial-appointment shortcut; “Requisite Directors” defined as the Board [including [a majority of] the Preferred Directors then seated].
Notes
Catechism: purports governance allocation (societas governance); functions per configuration. Forensics: stage 5 (with COI-08).
MG-NVCA-COI-08ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Protective provisions: structural vetoes and the operational set

Structural vetoes protect the priced bargain; the operational set governs the company's ordinary managerial metabolism — budget, hiring, borrowing, contracting — turning consent into shadow management wherever the brackets fill in.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
operational_veto_intensity
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports protective consent (defensive); the operational set functions as locatio conductio of management to capital — control without stewardship in proportion to brackets filled.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Class consent rights (negative covenants at charter level); remedies hardening via the void-ab-initio bracket (post-Fletcher, FN 39).
Economic
Structural vetoes protect the priced bargain; the operational set governs the company's ordinary managerial metabolism — budget, hiring, borrowing, contracting — turning consent into shadow management wherever the brackets fill in.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: structural vetoes 3.3.1-3.3.8 guard the bargain the parties priced (defensive form). Cautionary: the operational set as drafted-with-brackets. Potentially extractive (configuration): 3.3.9 (e)/(f) — hiring and budget — plus (a)/(j) in combination hand the managerial function to the consent-holder; with the void-ab-initio bracket, breach risk chills ordinary management.
  • Configuration to watch: Which 3.3.9 items survive configuration; Requisite-Directors carve-out retained (the drafted mitigator)? Void-ab-initio bracket in/out? Flag operational_veto_intensity attached (confirmed in Analyzer Flags tab, weight 1.45).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “and any such act or transaction that has not been approved by such consent or vote prior to such act or transaction being effected shall be null and v…”
  • “hire, terminate, or change the compensation of, approve or amend any stock or option awards to, or enter into any non-employment related transaction w…”
  • “approve the annual budget or any [material] amendment to the annual budget”

7. Framework / suite notes

Severe signal root: control without stewardship — dominion detached from the end it should serve (charter names no end, COI-01). SV14-04 (KPI/reporting as operational control) is tested at SUITE level with the IRA's information machinery — carried to P8 check 4. FN 45 (revised Oct 2025): the Requisite-Directors exception drafted as the compromise; FN 38: predicate board approval does not violate the provision; FN 40: 2023 removal of “or consent to any of the foregoing.”

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
3.3 preamble: listed acts require Requisite Holders' consent, direct or indirect, [null-and-void-ab-initio bracket]; 3.3.1-3.3.8 structural vetoes (liquidation/DLE, charter/bylaws amendment, senior/pari passu stock, share counts, redemptions/dividends, subsidiaries, board size); 3.3.9 bracketed operational set [(a) debt, (b) equity plans, (c) loans, (d) guarantees, (e) executive hiring/compensation, (f) annual budget, (g) 1%-holder grants, (h) committee composition, (i) tokens, (j) large contracts, (k) IP/acquihire transfers, (l) leases] unless Requisite Directors approve.
Notes
Catechism: purports protective consent (defensive); the operational set functions as locatio conductio of management to capital — control without stewardship in proportion to brackets filled. Forensics: stage 5 (subclause per veto).
MG-NVCA-COI-09ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Optional conversion and mechanics (with the 5A suspension weld)

The exit-to-Common option is the preferred holder's own risk-rejoining choice — the societas door kept open; the suspension bracket closes that door exactly when the pay-to-play penalty would bite, so a holder cannot convert to dodge the 5A discipline.

Catechism: purports option machinery (emptio venditio of the conversion right's exercise); functions as the risk-rejoininGeometry: periculum_sortis · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

…d by dividing the applicable Original Issue Price[FN id=47] by the applicable Conversion Price (as defined below) in effect at the time of conversion. The “Conversion Price” applicable to the Preferred Stock as of the Original Issue Date shall be equal to $_______ [insert original purchase price of Series A Preferred Stock] per share of Series A Preferred Stock. Such initial Conversion Price for a series of Preferred Stock, and the rate at which shares of Preferred Stock may be converted into shares of Common Stock, shall be subject to adjustment as provided in this Section 4. [Notwithstanding the foregoing and notwithstanding Section 4.3.1, the optional right to convert shares of Preferred Stock into shares of Common Stock pursuant to the first sentence of this Section 4.1.1 shall be suspended, and no optional conversion may be effective, from and after the date the Corporation delivers (pursuant to Section 5A.1 below) notice of a Qualified Financing (as defined below) until immediately after the earlier of (x) consummation of the Qualified Financing and the associated Special Mandatory Conversion or (y) termination of the Qualified Financing; provided, however, that the foregoing limitation on the right to optionally convert shares into Common Stock pursuant to this Section 4.1.1 shall not affect the calculation of the number of shares deemed issuable upon conversion of such shares for purposes of (A) voting rights under this Cer…

…on Time (i) issue and deliver to such holder of Preferred Stock, or to his, her or its nominees, a certificate or certificates (whether electronic or physical) for the number of full shares of Common Stock issuable upon such conversion in accordance with the provisions hereof and a certificate for the number (if any) of the shares of Preferred Stock represented by the surrendered certificate that were not converted into Common Stock[FN id=50], and (ii) pay all declared but unpaid dividends on the shares of Preferred Stock converted.
4.3.2	Reservation of Shares. The Corporation shall at all times when the Preferred Stock shall be outstanding, reserve and keep available out of its authorized but unissued capital stock, for the purpose of effecting the conversion of the Preferred Stock, such number of its duly authorized shares of Common Stock as shall from time to time be sufficient to effect the conversion of all outstanding Preferred Stock; and if at any time the number of authorized but unissued shares of Common Stock shall not be sufficient to effect the conversion of all then outstanding shares of the Preferred Stock, the Corporation shall take such corporate action as may be necessary to increase its authorized but unissued shares of Common Stock to such number of shares as shall be sufficient for such purposes, including, without limitation, engaging in best efforts to obtain the requisite stockholder approval of a…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports option machinery (emptio venditio of the conversion right's exercise); functions as the risk-rejoining door.

3. Title breakdown

Periculum sortisprimary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Conversion right and mechanics; DGCL share-reservation covenant; bracketed suspension coordinating with Section 5A's pay-to-play.
Economic
The exit-to-Common option is the preferred holder's own risk-rejoining choice — the societas door kept open; the suspension bracket closes that door exactly when the pay-to-play penalty would bite, so a holder cannot convert to dodge the 5A discipline.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the conversion right itself and the reservation covenant (promise-keeping mechanics). Cautionary: the suspension bracket is configuration; timing rules (4.1.2) put a real deadline on the convert-or-take-preference choice.
  • Configuration to watch: Suspension bracket in/out at configuration (only meaningful with 5A included); certificated-share mechanics mostly legacy.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the optional right to convert shares of Preferred Stock into shares of Common Stock pursuant to the first sentence of this Section 4.1.1 shall be susp…”
  • “reserve and keep available out of its authorized but unissued capital stock, for the purpose of effecting the conversion of the Preferred Stock”

7. Framework / suite notes

Conversion is the mechanism by which preferred CAN rejoin full residual risk (q.78 a.2 ad 5 direction — AUTH-AQU-ST78, Verified); the suspension bracket is 5A coordination, not extraction (it binds the capital side).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Each preferred share convertible at holder's option into Common at OIP/Conversion Price (4.1.1); conversion rights terminate on redemption notice or liquidation payout dates (4.1.2); mechanics, share reservation, effect, taxes (4.3.x); bracketed: optional conversion SUSPENDED between notice and consummation of a Qualified Financing.
Notes
Catechism: purports option machinery (emptio venditio of the conversion right's exercise); functions as the risk-rejoining door. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-COI-10ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Anti-dilution: broad-based weighted average vs full ratchet

Down-round repricing shifts dilution loss from preferred to Common; the weighted-average formula shares that loss in proportion to round size, the ratchet transfers it entirely regardless of size — Common absorbs the valuation error of a prior round.

CP2 = CP1* (A + B) / (A + C).

4.4.4	Adjustment of Conversion Price Upon Issuance of Additional Shares of Common Stock. In the event the Corporation at any time after the Original Issue Date [and prior to [Date]][FN id=58] issues Additional Shares of Common Stock (including Additional Shares of Common Stock deemed to be issued pursuant to Section 4.4.3), without consideration or for a consideration per share less than the Conversion Price of a series of Preferred Stock in effect immediately prior to such issuance or deemed issuance, then the Conversion Price of such series of Preferred Stock shall be reduced, concurrently with such issuance or deemed issuance, to the consideration per share received by the Corporation for such issue or deemed issue of the Additional Shares of Common Stock; provided that if such issuance or deemed issuance was without consideration, then the Corporation shall be deemed to have received an aggregate of [$0.001] of consideration for all such Additional Shares of Common Stock issued or deemed to be issued.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
harsh_anti_dilution
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports price-adjustment machinery; the ratchet functions as retroactive repricing of a completed sale (emptio venditio revisited against the seller).

3. Title breakdown

Valuation controlprimary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Conversion-price adjustment on dilutive issuances; definitional machinery for options/convertibles; carve-out schedule.
Economic
Down-round repricing shifts dilution loss from preferred to Common; the weighted-average formula shares that loss in proportion to round size, the ratchet transfers it entirely regardless of size — Common absorbs the valuation error of a prior round.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: parity adjustments only via 4.5-4.8 (COI-11). Cautionary: broad-based weighted average as drafted. Potentially extractive (configuration): full ratchet — the residual class insures capital against its own pricing judgment; aggravated when combined with cumulative dividends (COI-04) and a multiple (COI-05).
  • Configuration to watch: Which 4.4.4 at configuration; ratchet time-limit bracket; Exempted-Securities gates (Requisite-Directors approval vs fixed caps) — who controls the escape hatches?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “CP2 = CP1* (A + B) / (A + C).”
  • “the Conversion Price of such series of Preferred Stock shall be reduced, concurrently with such issuance or deemed issuance, to the consideration per …”
  • “[Use the following Section 4.4.4 if the terms sheet calls for a broad-based weighted average anti-dilution provision]”

7. Framework / suite notes

v1.3/v1.4 calibration: weighted-average = commercial norm (cautionary); full ratchet was the NVCA-era PE marker. The Exempted Securities brackets (approval vs share-cap variants) decide how much ordinary operation escapes repricing.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Alternative 4.4.4s: broad-based weighted-average (CP2 = CP1*(A+B)/(A+C)) vs full ratchet (Conversion Price drops to the new issue price outright, optionally time-limited); deemed-issue machinery 4.4.3; Exempted Securities carve-outs (a)(i)-(ix), several gated on Requisite-Directors approval or share caps.
Notes
Catechism: purports price-adjustment machinery; the ratchet functions as retroactive repricing of a completed sale (emptio venditio revisited against the seller). Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-11ART-FOURTHlicitprotective

Parity adjustments, certificates, and record-date notices (4.5-4.10)

Keeps the agreed exchange ratio true through corporate arithmetic — protects the priced bargain symmetrically rather than shifting surplus; the certificates and notices are enforceable transparency.

4.9	Certificate as to Adjustments. Upon the occurrence of each adjustment or readjustment of the Conversion Price of a series of Preferred Stock pursuant to this Section 4, the Corporation at its expense shall, as promptly as reasonably practicable but in any event not later than ten days thereafter, compute such adjustment or readjustment in accordance with the terms hereof and furnish to each holder of such series of Preferred Stock a certificate setting forth such adjustment or readjustment (including the kind and amount of securities, cash or other property into which such series of Preferred Stock is convertible) and showing in detail the facts upon which such adjustment or readjustment is based. 1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
licit
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
protective
Positive form
POS-08 — Transparency as equity — Terms, costs, and waterfalls disclosed so exchange equality is checkable.

Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports adjustment mechanics; functions as promise-keeping arithmetic.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Valuation controlsecondary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.

4. Functions

Legal
Standard anti-manipulation arithmetic preserving the conversion bargain through recapitalizations; information/certification duties.
Economic
Keeps the agreed exchange ratio true through corporate arithmetic — protects the priced bargain symmetrically rather than shifting surplus; the certificates and notices are enforceable transparency.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: parity-preserving arithmetic and enforceable disclosure duties; nothing here shifts surplus or control. Cautionary: none of substance (notice periods bracketed at [10] days).
  • Configuration to watch: None material; notice-period blanks are minor configuration.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “furnish to each holder of such series of Preferred Stock a certificate setting forth such adjustment or readjustment”
  • “Such notice shall be sent at least [10] days prior to the record date or effective date for the event specified in such notice.”

7. Framework / suite notes

AUTH-CST-OPQ21 (Verified): transparency as equity infrastructure — the 4.9/4.10 duties are its operative form at charter level.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Proportional Conversion-Price adjustment on splits/combinations (4.5), stock dividends (4.6), other distributions (4.7), mergers/reorganizations (4.8); Corporation must certify adjustments within 10 days on request (4.9); 10-day advance notice of record dates and major events (4.10).
Notes
Catechism: purports adjustment mechanics; functions as promise-keeping arithmetic. No forensics.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports automatic conversion covenant; functions as the sunset of priority.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Periculum sortissecondary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.

4. Functions

Legal
Automatic conversion trigger provisions; procedural mechanics (5.2).
Economic
The agreed self-dissolution of the preference architecture at a qualified public exit: priority claims end when liquidity and pricing become public — the structural counterweight to everything in Sections 1-3.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the self-dissolution principle; Requisite-Holders election route. Cautionary: threshold blanks (price floor, proceeds floor) decide whether the off-ramp is real or notional; the bracketed Direct Listing limb (new surface vs 2020) adds a board-discretion market-cap gate.
  • Configuration to watch: Threshold values at configuration; Direct Listing limb in/out; who bears if thresholds set unreachably high (preference becomes perpetual)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “in a firm-commitment underwritten”
  • “the date and time, or upon the occurrence of an event, specified by vote or written consent of the Requisite Holders.”

7. Framework / suite notes

The mandatory-conversion event is where the instrument's mutuum-like features are contractually scheduled to die — the honesty of the architecture depends on the thresholds being reachable (blanks).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
All preferred auto-converts at the earlier of: (a) a firm-commitment underwritten IPO [at $[__]/share] with $[__] [gross] proceeds and an approved exchange listing (Qualified IPO) [or a Qualified Direct Listing with board-determined market-cap floor]; (b) the date/event specified by Requisite Holders' vote.
Notes
Catechism: purports automatic conversion covenant; functions as the sunset of priority. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-COI-13ART-FOURTHcautionaryprotective

Special mandatory conversion (pay-to-play)

Investor-vs-investor burden-sharing enforcement: capital that declines to keep sharing the enterprise's ongoing risk loses its priority — the one mechanism in the charter that makes CONTINUED risk-bearing a condition of keeping preference.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
protective
Positive form
POS-01 — Risk truly shared — Capital stands to lose principal with no insulating stack; profit-share matches exposure.
Materiality M
0.288 (b0.6 binding-conditional (bracketed alternative + per-deal trigger definitions); s0.8 survives while preferred outstanding; l0.6 answers free-riding within the financing stack partially (squeeze configuration cuts the other way))
Enforceability test
Automatic and self-executing once triggered — but the whole Section is a [Use if] alternative: binding-conditional.
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports conversion penalty; functions as enforced risk-solidarity among capital — protective exemplarity with the squeeze configuration flagged.

3. Title breakdown

Periculum sortisprimary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Forced-conversion penalty on non-participation in qualified financings; definitional machinery (5A.3).
Economic
Investor-vs-investor burden-sharing enforcement: capital that declines to keep sharing the enterprise's ongoing risk loses its priority — the one mechanism in the charter that makes CONTINUED risk-bearing a condition of keeping preference.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the burden-sharing principle; the Applicable Portion variant (proportional, not cliff). Cautionary: the Corporation controls timing/notice (10-day window); Board sets Offered Securities and cutbacks; smaller funds without reserves bear it hardest (directionality: risk-to-member). Potentially extractive (configuration): as a squeeze tool — a controlled Board setting an unnecessary Qualified Financing at a low price to force out minority preferred.
  • Configuration to watch: Included at configuration? Whole-share or Applicable Portion penalty? Qualified Financing floor set by proceeds or by any-dilutive-issuance (the wider trigger widens the squeeze surface)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “be converted into shares of Common Stock at the applicable Conversion Price in effect immediately prior to the consummation of such Qualified Financin…”
  • “[Use the following Section 5A if the Term Sheet calls for a pay-to-play provision where the penalty is conversion into Common Stock.]”

7. Framework / suite notes

The adversarial corpus's nearest approach to a periculum-sortis-maintenance device: preference is conditioned on continuing to fund risk (q.78 a.2 ad 5 direction, AUTH-AQU-ST78 Verified). Genre-scoped renunciation test (G3 ruling): within this genre a pay-to-play is a live counterfactual (most charters omit it), so protective credit is genuine, not definitional.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[Use if the Term Sheet calls for a pay-to-play with conversion penalty.] A holder failing to purchase its Pro Rata Amount in a Qualified Financing is automatically converted to Common — [each share] or [the Applicable Portion] (proportional shortfall variant); affiliates aggregated; Qualified Financing definable by proceeds floor or by any dilutive issuance; Requisite Holders may exempt a transaction.
Notes
Catechism: purports conversion penalty; functions as enforced risk-solidarity among capital — protective exemplarity with the squeeze configuration flagged. No forensics beyond stage-5 note.
MG-NVCA-COI-14ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Redemption: the non-redeemable default and the redemption engine

The put converts equity into a maturing claim: principal back on demand-date, priority over every corporate purpose, and a penalty interest curve on delay — the balance-sheet behavior of debt with none of debt's disclosure.

Catechism: purports redeemable preferred (societas with exit); functions as mutuum-on-demand with poena conventionalis onGeometry: carentia_pecuniae · liquidity_pressureStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 102570–104002
… gross proceeds to the Corporation, [including/excluding] proceeds previously received by Corporation for debt that is being cancelled or converted in connection with such issuance] [or result in the reduction of the Conversion Price of any series of Preferred Stock pursuant to the terms of this Certificate of Incorporation (without giving effect to the operation of Section 4.4.2]], unless the [Requisite Holders] elect, by written notice sent to the Corporation at least [__] days prior to the consummation of the Qualified Financing, that such transaction not be treated as a Qualified Financing for purposes of this Section 5A. 
6.	Redemption. [Other than as set forth in Section 2.3.2(b), the Preferred Stock is not redeemable at the option of the holder or the Corporation.][FN id=76],[FN id=77] 
6.1	[General.
