…ion Notice, and thereupon the Redemption Price for such shares shall be payable to the order of the person whose name appears on such certificate or certificates as the owner thereof. In the event less than all of the shares of Preferred Stock represented by a certificate are redeemed, a new certificate, instrument, or book entry representing the unredeemed shares of Preferred Stock shall promptly be issued to such holder.
6.4	Interest. If any shares of Preferred Stock are not redeemed for any reason on any Redemption Date, all such unredeemed shares shall remain outstanding and entitled to all the rights and preferences provided herein, and the Corporation shall pay interest on the Redemption Price applicable to such unredeemed shares at an aggregate per annum rate equal to [12]% (increased by 1% each month following the Redemption Date until the Redemption Price, and any interest thereon, is paid in full), with such interest to accrue daily in arrears and be compounded [annually]; provided, however, that in no event shall such interest exceed the maximum permitted rate of interest under applicable law (the “Maximum Permitted Rate”), provided, however, that the Corporation shall take all such actions as may be necessary, including, without limitation, making any applicable governmental filings, to cause the Maximum Permitted Rate to be the highest possible rate. In the event any provision hereof would result in the rate of interest payable hereunder being in excess of the Maximum Permitted Rate, the amount of interest required to be paid hereu…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
redemption_rights
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports redeemable preferred (societas with exit); functions as mutuum-on-demand with poena conventionalis on arrears. SV14/NEC-SV check at stage 6: with engine + accrual + operational vetoes, the liquidity-pressure severe configuration is constructible — the stage-6 scenario carries it.

3. Title breakdown

Carentia pecuniaeprimary category
Moral hinge
Mere lack of money is not enough; absence must not collapse into universal time-value entitlement.
Legitimate form
Only under narrowly justified, verified, non-universal conditions if accepted by a reviewed moral regime.
Abuse form
Every loan becomes interest-bearing by nature; restorative compensation collapses into usurious yield.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Redemption right (holder put) with installment mechanics, interest on arrears, and legal-capital limits; notice and certificate machinery.
Economic
The put converts equity into a maturing claim: principal back on demand-date, priority over every corporate purpose, and a penalty interest curve on delay — the balance-sheet behavior of debt with none of debt's disclosure.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the non-redeemable default sentence — as drafted-default this charter renounces the put. Cautionary: the engine as bracketed alternative; Excluded-Shares opt-out and legal-capital limits are real brakes. Potentially extractive (configuration): the engine included with FMV floor + short date + 12%+1%/mo escalator — a demand loan against the company's whole asset base, extinguishing enterprise purpose at the moment of exercise.
  • Configuration to watch: Engine in or out (the single largest configuration decision in the charter); if in: date, price formula (OIP vs greater-of-FMV), installment count, interest rate/escalator; does the accrual (COI-04 alt 3) feed the Redemption Price (FN 78)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “[Other than as set forth in Section 2.3.2(b), the Preferred Stock is not redeemable at the option of the holder or the Corporation.]”
  • “the Corporation shall apply all of its assets to any such redemption, and to no other corporate purpose, except to the extent prohibited by Delaware l…”
  • “the Corporation shall pay interest on the Redemption Price applicable to such unredeemed shares at an aggregate per annum rate equal to [12]% (increas…”

7. Framework / suite notes

The catechism inversion at maximum contrast: purports societas, functions mutuum on election — principal recovery (redemption at OIP), time-charge (12% escalating), and priority over enterprise purpose ('no other corporate purpose'). The 6.4 escalator is poena conventionalis in form: a conventional penalty priced on delay, growing monthly. Cf. DSFP-01's opposite pole (redemption out of surplus only, dignity ordering) — the NEC suite's D4.4 machinery is the drafted refutation of this clause.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Default: [Other than 2.3.2(b), the Preferred Stock is not redeemable]. Alternative engine (6.1-6.5): from a date, Requisite Holders may demand redemption of ALL preferred at [greater of OIP + declared-unpaid dividends [or FMV]] in three annual installments; on request the Corporation applies ALL assets to redemption and no other corporate purpose (except as Delaware law prohibits); unredeemed shares accrue [12]% interest, +1% per month until paid; holders may opt out (Excluded Shares).
Notes
Catechism: purports redeemable preferred (societas with exit); functions as mutuum-on-demand with poena conventionalis on arrears. SV14/NEC-SV check at stage 6: with engine + accrual + operational vetoes, the liquidity-pressure severe configuration is constructible — the stage-6 scenario carries it. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-15ART-FOURTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Redeemed-share retirement, class waiver, notices (Sections 7-9)

The waiver clause makes the Requisite-Holders threshold the effective owner of every preferred protection: whatever majority can amend can also waive for everyone — minority preferred protections are exactly as strong as the threshold is high.

Catechism: purports housekeeping; the waiver functions as intra-class government.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Named-investor / side-right stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 111229–112735
… subsidiaries shall be automatically and immediately cancelled and retired and shall not be reissued, sold or transferred. Neither the Corporation nor any of its subsidiaries may exercise any voting or other rights granted to the holders of Preferred Stock following redemption, conversion or acquisition. The Corporation may thereafter take such appropriate action (without the need for stockholder action) as may be necessary to reduce the authorized number of shares of Preferred Stock accordingly.
8.	Waiver. Except as otherwise set forth herein, (a) any of the rights, powers, preferences and other terms of the Preferred Stock set forth herein may be waived on behalf of all holders of Preferred Stock by the affirmative written consent or vote of the holders that would otherwise be required to amend such right, powers, preferences, and other terms and (b) at any time more than one series of Preferred Stock is issued and outstanding, any of the rights, powers, preferences and other terms of any series of Preferred Stock set forth herein may be waived on behalf of all holders of such series of Preferred Stock by the affirmative written consent or vote of the holders of such series that would otherwise be required to amend such right, power, preference, or other term.
9.	Notices. Any notice required or permitted by the provisions of this Article Fourth to be given to a holder of shares of Preferred Stock shall be mailed, postage prepaid, to the post office address last shown on the recor…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports housekeeping; the waiver functions as intra-class government.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Share retirement; class-level waiver provision keyed to amendment thresholds; notice formalities.
Economic
The waiver clause makes the Requisite-Holders threshold the effective owner of every preferred protection: whatever majority can amend can also waive for everyone — minority preferred protections are exactly as strong as the threshold is high.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: retirement mechanics; waiver-parity-with-amendment is coherent design. Cautionary: majority-of-class waiver binds dissenting minorities on every preference; combined with affiliate aggregation, a lead investor at threshold controls the class's rights.
  • Configuration to watch: Requisite-Holders percentage at configuration (the single number governing DLE opt-outs, protective provisions, waivers, 5A exemptions); any series-level carve-outs?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “may be waived on behalf of all holders of Preferred Stock by the affirmative written consent or vote of the holders that would otherwise be required t…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Intra-class agency: the same Requisite-Holders machinery that protects preferred against the company governs preferred against each other (cf. COI-06's DLE opt-out, COI-13's exemption vote) — the P8 defined-term check tracks the threshold across all consent sites.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Redeemed/acquired preferred auto-cancels and cannot be reissued (unless Board + Requisite Holders approve); any preferred right may be waived for ALL holders by the same vote required to amend it; electronic notice mechanics.
Notes
Catechism: purports housekeeping; the waiver functions as intra-class government. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-COI-16ART-FIFTH, ART-SIXTH, ART-SEVENTH, ART-EIGHTHlicitneutral (exemplarity)

Governance housekeeping: bylaw power, board size, ballots, meetings

Load-bearing only through what the Bylaws then do: board size and bylaw control are the levers these articles delegate — and the Bylaws are NOT part of the model suite, so the suite cannot verify what arrives through this door.

Catechism: purports procedural allocation; functions per the absent Bylaws. Grouped treatment (4 articles, one row).Geometry: governance_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…ight, power, preference, or other term.
9.	Notices. Any notice required or permitted by the provisions of this Article Fourth to be given to a holder of shares of Preferred Stock shall be mailed, postage prepaid, to the post office address last shown on the records of the Corporation, or given by electronic transmission in compliance with the provisions of the General Corporation Law, and shall be deemed sent upon such mailing or electronic transmission.
FIFTH: Subject to any additional vote required by this Certificate of Incorporation or the Bylaws of the Corporation, in furtherance and not in limitation of the powers conferred by statute, the Board of Directors is expressly authorized to make, repeal, alter, amend and rescind any or all of the Bylaws of the Corporation.
SIXTH: Subject to any additional vote required by this Certificate of Incorporation, the number of directors of the Corporation shall be determined in the manner set forth in the Bylaws of the Corporation. 
SEVENTH: Elections of directors need not be by written ballot unless the Bylaws of the Corporation shall so provide. 
EIGHTH: Meetings of stockholders may be held within or outside of the State of Delaware, as the Bylaws of the Corporation may provide. The books of the Corporation may be kept (subject to any provision of applicable law) outside of the State of Delaware at such place or places or in such manner or manners as may be design…

… post office address last shown on the records of the Corporation, or given by electronic transmission in compliance with the provisions of the General Corporation Law, and shall be deemed sent upon such mailing or electronic transmission.
FIFTH: Subject to any additional vote required by this Certificate of Incorporation or the Bylaws of the Corporation, in furtherance and not in limitation of the powers conferred by statute, the Board of Directors is expressly authorized to make, repeal, alter, amend and rescind any or all of the Bylaws of the Corporation.
SIXTH: Subject to any additional vote required by this Certificate of Incorporation, the number of directors of the Corporation shall be determined in the manner set forth in the Bylaws of the Corporation. 
SEVENTH: Elections of directors need not be by written ballot unless the Bylaws of the Corporation shall so provide. 
EIGHTH: Meetings of stockholders may be held within or outside of the State of Delaware, as the Bylaws of the Corporation may provide. The books of the Corporation may be kept (subject to any provision of applicable law) outside of the State of Delaware at such place or places or in such manner or manners as may be designated from time to time by the Board of Directors or in the Bylaws of the Corporation.

Exemplarity (Mode 2)

2. Catechism / title

purports procedural allocation; functions per the absent Bylaws. Grouped treatment (4 articles, one row).

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Standard DGCL allocations of bylaw and board-sizing authority; procedural formalities.
Economic
Load-bearing only through what the Bylaws then do: board size and bylaw control are the levers these articles delegate — and the Bylaws are NOT part of the model suite, so the suite cannot verify what arrives through this door.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: ordinary-form allocations, both subject to the protective provisions (3.3.8 board-size veto reaches back in). Cautionary: none in the text itself; the delegation target is outside the suite.
  • Configuration to watch: P8: record the Bylaws-absence as a coverage hole in the suite map (the 2020-era baseline had the same hole).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the Board of Directors is expressly authorized to make, repeal, alter, amend and rescind any or all of the Bylaws of the Corporation”
  • “the number of directors of the Corporation shall be determined in the manner set forth in the Bylaws of the Corporation”

7. Framework / suite notes

Framework support: general (standard Delaware allocations). Suite-topology note for P8: the Bylaws are the one governance instrument the model set references but does not contain — board-size arithmetic (COI-07 ↔ VA) resolves partly outside the suite's four corners.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Board expressly authorized to make/amend/repeal Bylaws (FIFTH, subject to any additional charter/bylaw vote); director count set per Bylaws (SIXTH); no written-ballot requirement (SEVENTH); meetings/books may sit outside Delaware (EIGHTH).
Notes
Catechism: purports procedural allocation; functions per the absent Bylaws. Grouped treatment (4 articles, one row).
MG-NVCA-COI-17ART-NINTH, ART-TENTH, ALT-TENTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Exculpation and indemnification (short form + Exhibit A long form)

Fiduciary-breach costs move from the persons who govern to the company purse (and its insurer): accountability for negligent stewardship is priced away from the stewards — standard across the genre, and precisely calibrated to the DGCL's outer limit ('to the fullest extent permitted').

Catechism: purports liability allocation; functions as damnum shifted from steward to enterprise. Grouped with the ExhibiGeometry: governance_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Founder personal exposure stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 113387–114876
…ll be determined in the manner set forth in the Bylaws of the Corporation. 
SEVENTH: Elections of directors need not be by written ballot unless the Bylaws of the Corporation shall so provide. 
EIGHTH: Meetings of stockholders may be held within or outside of the State of Delaware, as the Bylaws of the Corporation may provide. The books of the Corporation may be kept (subject to any provision of applicable law) outside of the State of Delaware at such place or places or in such manner or manners as may be designated from time to time by the Board of Directors or in the Bylaws of the Corporation.
NINTH: To the fullest extent permitted by law, a director [or officer] of the Corporation shall not be personally liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director [or officer]. If the General Corporation Law or any other law of the State of Delaware is amended after approval by the stockholders of this Article Ninth to authorize corporate action further eliminating or limiting the personal liability of directors [or officers], then the liability of a director [or officer] of the Corporation shall be eliminated or limited to the fullest extent permitted by the General Corporation Law as so amended.
Any amendment, repeal or elimination of the foregoing provisions of this Article Ninth by the stockholders of the Corporation shall not adversely affect any right or protection of a director [or officer] of the Corporati…

…f an undertaking by the Indemnified Person to repay all amounts advanced if it should be ultimately determined that the Indemnified Person is not entitled to be indemnified under this Article Tenth or otherwise.
3.	Claims by Directors and Officers. If a claim for indemnification or advancement of expenses under this Article Tenth is not paid in full within 30 days after a written claim therefor by the Indemnified Person has been received by the Corporation, the Indemnified Person may file suit to recover the unpaid amount of such claim and, if successful in whole or in part, shall be entitled to be paid the expense of prosecuting such claim. In any such action the Corporation shall have the burden of proving that the Indemnified Person is not entitled to the requested indemnification or advancement of expenses under applicable law.
4.	Indemnification of Employees and Agents. The Corporation may indemnify and advance expenses to any person who was or is made or is threatened to be made or is otherwise involved in any Proceeding by reason of the fact that such person, or a person for whom such person is the legal representative, is or was an employee or agent of the Corporation or, while an employee or agent of the Corporation, is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer, employee or agent of another corporation or of a partnership, joint venture, limited liability company, trust, enterprise or nonprofit entity, including service with respe…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports liability allocation; functions as damnum shifted from steward to enterprise. Grouped with the Exhibit A alternative (one row, alternatives noted).

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
DGCL §102(b)(7) exculpation (2022-amendment officer bracket); §145 indemnification architecture; advancement with undertaking; prospective-only repeal.
Economic
Fiduciary-breach costs move from the persons who govern to the company purse (and its insurer): accountability for negligent stewardship is priced away from the stewards — standard across the genre, and precisely calibrated to the DGCL's outer limit ('to the fullest extent permitted').

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: statutory-form exculpation; advancement undertakings; insurance. Cautionary: fullest-extent + auto-expansion drafting; the officer bracket (post-2022 DGCL) extends the shield to management; burden-reversal in Exhibit A §3 makes the purse presumptively open. Potentially extractive: not on this text alone — accountability erosion compounds only with the control stack.
  • Configuration to watch: Officer bracket in/out; short TENTH vs Exhibit A at configuration; D&O insurance assumed (ALT-TENTH 8) — who pays the premium is a real cost allocation.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “a director [or officer] of the Corporation shall not be personally liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fi…”
  • “In any such action the Corporation shall have the burden of proving that the Indemnified Person is not entitled to the requested indemnification or ad…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Severe signal root 'information without accountability' in weakened form: the duty survives (equity, loyalty carve-outs by statute) but its damages remedy against the person is removed; the company (all stockholders, incl. Common) funds the shield for directors a class elected (COI-07).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Director [or officer] personal liability for fiduciary-breach damages eliminated to the DGCL ceiling, auto-expanding with future amendments, repeal prospective-only (NINTH); indemnification/advancement authorized beyond §145 (TENTH); Exhibit A alternative: mandatory indemnification + advancement for directors/officers with burden of proof on the Corporation, permissive for employees/agents, insurance authority, non-exclusivity (ALT-TENTH 1-9).
Notes
Catechism: purports liability allocation; functions as damnum shifted from steward to enterprise. Grouped with the Exhibit A alternative (one row, alternatives noted).
MG-NVCA-COI-18ART-ELEVENTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Corporate-opportunity renunciation (Covered Persons), entrenched

Investors sit inside the company's information flow (board seats, inspection, MRL consultation) while owing it nothing they learn there beyond the narrow qua-director channel: deal flow discovered through the portfolio may be taken elsewhere freely — information without reciprocal accountability, constitutionally locked FOR capital.

Catechism: purports conflict management; functions as a one-way information valve with constitutional lock.Geometry: information_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 115568–117064
…se, in excess of the indemnification and advancement otherwise permitted by Section 145 of the General Corporation Law.
Any amendment, repeal, modification or elimination of the foregoing provisions of this Article Tenth shall not (a) adversely affect any right or protection of any director, officer or other agent of the Corporation existing at the time of such amendment, repeal, modification or elimination; or (b) increase the liability of any director, officer or agent of the Corporation with respect to any acts or omissions of such director, officer or agent occurring prior to such amendment, repeal, modification or elimination.
ELEVENTH: The Corporation renounces, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any interest or expectancy of the Corporation in, or in being offered an opportunity to participate in, any Excluded Opportunity. An “Excluded Opportunity” is any matter, transaction or interest that is presented to, or acquired, created or developed by, or which otherwise comes into the possession of (i) any director of the Corporation who is not an employee of the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries, or (ii) any holder of Preferred Stock or any partner, member, director, stockholder, employee, affiliate or agent of any such holder, other than someone who is an officer or employee of the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries (collectively, the persons referred to in clauses (i) and (ii) are “Covered Persons”), unless such matter, transaction or interest is present…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)COI · chars 117094–118543
… developed by, or otherwise comes into the possession of, a Covered Person expressly and solely in such Covered Person’s capacity as a director of the Corporation while such Covered Person is performing services in such capacity. Any repeal or modification of this Article Eleventh will only be prospective and will not affect the rights under this Article Eleventh in effect at the time of the occurrence of any actions or omissions to act giving rise to liability. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained elsewhere in this Certificate of Incorporation, in addition to any other vote required by law or this Certificate of Incorporation, the affirmative vote of the Requisite Holders will be required to amend or repeal, or to adopt any provisions inconsistent with this Article Eleventh.[FN id=89]
TWELFTH: [Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery in the State of Delaware shall be the sole and exclusive forum for any stockholder (including a beneficial owner) to bring (i) any derivative action or proceeding brought on behalf of the Corporation, (ii) any action asserting a claim of breach of fiduciary duty owed by any director, officer or other employee of the Corporation to the Corporation or the Corporation’s stockholders, (iii) any action asserting a claim against the Corporation, its directors, officers or employees arising pursuant to any provision of the…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports conflict management; functions as a one-way information valve with constitutional lock.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
DGCL §122(17) corporate-opportunity waiver; constitutional entrenchment of the waiver behind a class consent.
Economic
Investors sit inside the company's information flow (board seats, inspection, MRL consultation) while owing it nothing they learn there beyond the narrow qua-director channel: deal flow discovered through the portfolio may be taken elsewhere freely — information without reciprocal accountability, constitutionally locked FOR capital.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: conflict-management rationale for multi-portfolio fiduciaries; qua-director carve-in preserved. Cautionary: network breadth (agents, affiliates); prospective-only repeal; the asymmetric entrenchment — mission has no entrenched article anywhere in this charter, the opportunity waiver does. Potentially extractive (configuration/suite): combined with IRA information rights + MRL consultation access, the one-way information valve is complete (P8 check 4).
  • Configuration to watch: Any competitor-facing narrowing at configuration (cf. IRA 'Competitor' definition)? Note for P8: this article + IRA §3 + MRL = the information-without-accountability suite cell.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “The Corporation renounces, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any interest or expectancy of the Corporation in, or in being offered an opportunit…”
  • “the affirmative vote of the Requisite Holders will be required to amend or repeal, or to adopt any provisions inconsistent with this Article Eleventh”

7. Framework / suite notes

Severe signal root 'information without accountability' — the direct case: advantage from position without reciprocal responsibility (good faith in exchange). Commercially standard for fund investors (fiduciary-conflict management across portfolios is real); the moral question is the breadth (whole networks) and the one-way entrenchment (the ONLY article entrenched behind the class vote is the one protecting the class).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Corporation renounces, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any interest or expectancy in Excluded Opportunities — matters reaching any non-employee director OR any Preferred Stock holder and its whole network (partners, members, directors, stockholders, employees, affiliates, agents), unless received expressly and solely qua director; repeal prospective-only; amendment requires Requisite Holders' vote.
Notes
Catechism: purports conflict management; functions as a one-way information valve with constitutional lock. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-COI-19ART-TWELFTH, ART-THIRTEENTH, ART-FOURTEENTHcautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Forum selection, fail-open severability, California §500 waiver

Boilerplate with distributional load at the margins: the forum clause prices enforcement (Chancery expertise, but one venue, no jury) — heaviest for small common holders; fail-open severability preserves the remaining machinery maximally when any piece falls; the §500 waiver removes a California legal-capital brake specifically for employee-share repurchases — smoothing the leaver machinery the suite operates through the ROFR and repurchase carve-outs (3.3.6(iii)).

Catechism: purports boilerplate; the §500 waiver functions as a leaver-machinery lubricant. Grouped (3 articles, one row)Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Founder labor / equity stackConfidence: Medium-high

TWELFTH: [Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery in the State of Delaware shall be the sole and exclusive forum for any stockholder (including a beneficial owner) to bring (i) any derivative action or proceeding brought on behalf of the Corporation, (ii) any action asserting a claim of breach of fiduciary duty owed by any director, officer or other employee of the Corporation to the Corporation or the Corporation’s stockholders, (iii) any action asserting a claim against the Corporation, its directors, officers or employees arising pursuant to any provision of the General Corporation Law or the Corporation’s certificate of incorporation or bylaws or (iv) any action asserting a claim against the Corporation, its directors, officers or employees governed by the internal af…

…ficate of Incorporation from employees, officers, directors or consultants of the Corporation in connection with a termination of employment or services pursuant to agreements or arrangements approved by the Board of Directors (in addition to any other consent required under this Certificate of Incorporation), such repurchase may be made without regard to any “preferential dividends arrears amount” or “preferential rights amount” (as those terms are defined in Section 500 of the California Corporations Code). Accordingly, for purposes of making any calculation under California Corporations Code Section 500 in connection with such repurchase, the amount of any “preferential dividends arrears amount” or “preferential rights amount” (as those terms are defined therein) shall be deemed to be zero.][FN id=91]

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation has been executed by a duly authorized officer of this corporation on ____________…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports boilerplate; the §500 waiver functions as a leaver-machinery lubricant. Grouped (3 articles, one row).

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Forum-selection clause; severability clause; California Corporations Code §500 distribution-test waiver for compensatory repurchases.
Economic
Boilerplate with distributional load at the margins: the forum clause prices enforcement (Chancery expertise, but one venue, no jury) — heaviest for small common holders; fail-open severability preserves the remaining machinery maximally when any piece falls; the §500 waiver removes a California legal-capital brake specifically for employee-share repurchases — smoothing the leaver machinery the suite operates through the ROFR and repurchase carve-outs (3.3.6(iii)).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: Chancery expertise rationale; severability is universal boilerplate. Cautionary: single-forum cost asymmetry; fail-open preservation of the extraction machinery; the §500 waiver removes a statutory employee-protective brake (bracketed — configuration).
  • Configuration to watch: Forum bracket in/out; §500 waiver relevant only for quasi-CA corporations — flag at configuration; note the severability-polarity contrast for the suite memo.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the Court of Chancery in the State of Delaware shall be the sole and exclusive forum for any stockholder”
  • “the amount of any “preferential dividends arrears amount” or “preferential rights amount” (as those terms are defined therein) shall be deemed to be z…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Fail-open vs fail-closed severability is a design signature: the NEC suite drafts fail-closed (protections' failure stops the machine); this charter drafts fail-open (the machine survives its parts). The §500 waiver interacts with the founder labor/equity stack — repurchases at original price (3.3.6(iii)) proceed without the CA arrears test.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Chancery-exclusive forum for stockholder/internal-affairs actions (bracketed, with indispensable-party and jurisdiction outs) (TWELFTH); maximal severability — every provision survives to the fullest extent if another falls (THIRTEENTH); for quasi-California corporations, employee-repurchase calculations deem preferential-dividend/rights arrears to be zero (FOURTEENTH, bracketed).
Notes
Catechism: purports boilerplate; the §500 waiver functions as a leaver-machinery lubricant. Grouped (3 articles, one row).

Accordingly, for purposes of making any calculation under California Corporations Code Section 500 in connection with such repurchase, the amount of any “preferential dividends arrears amount” or “preferential rights amount” (as those terms are defined therein) shall be deemed to be zero.][FN id=91]

COI — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Economics / priority stack
Synthesis
Priority chain: accrual->preference->DLE payout->redemption price (04/05/06/14, FN18/FN78 wiring)
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-02cautionary
Stack
Governance / operational control stack
Synthesis
Vote-override + board map + vetoes + class waiver; Requisite-Holders threshold governs every consent site; 3.3.9(e)/(f) reach management
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-03cautionary
Stack
Information / KPI stack
Synthesis
Transparency in (11) vs opportunity valve out (18); suite completion at IRA S3 + MRL (P8 cell)
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-04cautionary
Stack
Liquidity / exit / financing stack
Synthesis
Exit clock-works: DLE freeze, conversion locks, sunset thresholds, pay-to-play, engine
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-05cautionary
Stack
Founder labor / equity stack
Synthesis
Thin at charter level by design (S500 waiver + 3.3.6(iii) only); assembles across ROFR/VA/SPA - P8
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-06cautionary
Stack
Founder personal exposure stack
Synthesis
Exculpation/indemnification shields founder-directors too; company purse funds the shield
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-07cautionary
Stack
Mission integrity stack
Synthesis
Purpose-emptiness + asymmetric entrenchment (the only locked article protects capital's information freedom)
MG-NVCA-COI-SYN-08licit
Stack
Mitigator stack
Synthesis
Parity arithmetic, sunset, pay-to-play, non-redeemable default, Excluded Shares, waterfall-forcing, RD carve-out, greater-of choice

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-COI-A — Lower-risk standard financinglicit
Configuration
Alt-1 dividends; 1x NP; no 2.3.2(b); balanced board + RD carve-out + minimal 3.3.9; broad-based WA; no engine; no 5A; no S242 override; reachable QIPO thresholds
NVCA-COI-B — Standard-plus investor roundpotentially extractive
Configuration
Alt-2/3 dividends; 1-2x [participating capped]; 2.3.2(b) in; several 3.3.9 incl. (e) or (f); WA; override in; engine out
NVCA-COI-C — Full extraction configurationsevere
Configuration
Alt-3 compounding; >1x participating uncapped; full ratchet; engine with FMV + 12%+1%/mo; full 3.3.9 no carve-out; void-ab-initio; override; 5A cliff; unreachable sunset. SV14-07 fires at scenario level. Constructible entirely within printed brackets.

…are defined in the Certificate of Incorporation) or (2) the relationship among the parties after the closing, such as registration rights, rights of first refusal and co-sale and voting arrangements (these matters often implicate persons other than just the Company and the investors in this round of financing and are usually embodied in separate agreements to which those others persons are parties, or in some cases in the Certificate of Incorporation). The main items of negotiation in the Stock Purchase Agreement are the representations and warranties that the Company must make to the investors and the closing conditions for the transaction.

MG-NVCA-SPA-03SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Tranche machinery: milestones, elective/shortfall closings (1.2(c)(i)-(iii),(v))

The milestone call sits on the capital side twice over: the board determination requires the Preferred Directors' own approval, and the Requisite Purchasers may simply waive the milestones and call the tranche early at their discretion — the company's funding path is evidence-gated one way and discretion-gated the other.

2. Catechism / title

purports conditional subscription; functions per who holds the milestone pen.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Milestone-tranched financing architecture (new Oct-2025 model surface).
Economic
The milestone call sits on the capital side twice over: the board determination requires the Preferred Directors' own approval, and the Requisite Purchasers may simply waive the milestones and call the tranche early at their discretion — the company's funding path is evidence-gated one way and discretion-gated the other.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: staged deployment against milestones is real risk discipline. Cautionary: the double capital-side gate; Milestones live in Annex 1 (pure configuration). Potentially extractive (configuration): judgment-laden milestones + PD-majority approval = the funders adjudicate their own funding obligation.
  • Configuration to watch: Milestone objectivity at configuration (Annex 1); [Requisite Purchasers] limb in the determination; Outside Date length.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the written waiver of the achievement of the Milestones by the Requisite Purchasers, which waiver may be given or withheld by such Purchasers in their…”
  • “the determination by [(x)] the Board of Directors, which determination shall include the approval by [a majority] of the Preferred Directors”

7. Framework / suite notes

The N42 comparator lands: doc 42's tranches are evidence-led with a reasoned-explanation duty (IA-04); the NVCA tranche condition is approved by the funders' own directors with no explanation duty and a discretion-waiver route — the two suites' answers to the same problem, side by side (suite memo exhibit).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Tranche Closing obligatory if the Tranche Closing Condition occurs by the Outside Date: EITHER board determination of Milestone achievement — including [a majority of] Preferred Directors' approval [and Requisite Purchasers] — OR written waiver of the Milestones by Requisite Purchasers in their SOLE DISCRETION; Elective Closings let any purchaser pre-buy; Shortfall Closings reallocate defaulted shares; terminates at Outside Date/IPO/DLE.
Notes
Catechism: purports conditional subscription; functions per who holds the milestone pen. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-SPA-04SEC-1cautionaryprotective

SPA-side Special Mandatory Conversion (1.2(c)(iv))

Poena conventionalis among the capital class: default on the tranche promise costs ~90% of the stake's conversion value plus the rights apparatus — and the penalty is expressly the ONLY remedy, capping it at its conventional size.

Catechism: purports conversion penalty; functions as conventional penalty with a cap.Geometry: poena_conventionalis · periculum_sortisStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

… required number of Tranche Closing Shares at the Tranche Closing (including for calculation purposes all Tranche Closing Shares purchased at any Elective Closing)  (any such Purchaser, a “Defaulting Purchaser”), then each Share[FN id=12] held by such Defaulting Purchaser as of immediately prior to the Tranche Closing (the “Subject Preferred Stock”) shall automatically, and without any further action on the part of such Defaulting Purchaser, the Company or any other Person, be converted into fully paid and nonassessable shares of the Company’s common stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Common Stock”) at the rate of [one-tenth][FN id=13] of a share of Common Stock for each one share of the Subject Preferred Stock, all pursuant to, and as further provided in, Article Fourth, Part B, Section [5A.1] of the Restated Certificate (a “Special Mandatory Conversion”)[FN id=14].

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
protective
Positive form
POS-02 — Restorative compensation only — Charges track documented actual loss, never opportunity assumed.
Materiality M
0.48 (b1.0 operative; s0.8 survives the default event it governs; l0.6 answers penalty-stacking risk within the member stack partially)
Enforceability test
Sole-and-exclusive-remedy sentence: operative, self-executing cap on the penalty.
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports conversion penalty; functions as conventional penalty with a cap.

3. Title breakdown

Poena conventionalisprimary category
Moral hinge
Delay may be penalized only as wrongful delay, not as the price of time.
Legitimate form
Realistic due date; culpable delay; proportionate amount; lender prefers timely repayment; no engineered default.
Abuse form
Penalty calibrated to function as disguised interest, payable regardless of fault or actual harm.
Periculum sortissecondary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.

4. Functions

Legal
Investor-default penalty: punitive-ratio conversion plus rights stripping.
Economic
Poena conventionalis among the capital class: default on the tranche promise costs ~90% of the stake's conversion value plus the rights apparatus — and the penalty is expressly the ONLY remedy, capping it at its conventional size.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: enforced risk-solidarity (the tranche promise is real money others rely on); the exclusive-remedy cap. Cautionary: the [one-tenth] ratio is punitive configuration (poena beyond restoration); collateral rights-stripping reaches instruments outside this contract.
  • Configuration to watch: Ratio at configuration ([one-tenth] vs 1:1); waiver mechanics (Requisite Purchasers may waive for all).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “of a share of Common Stock for each one share of the Subject Preferred Stock”
  • “shall be the sole and exclusive remedy under this Agreement for the Company or any other Purchaser against any Defaulting Purchaser”

7. Framework / suite notes

The COI-13 pay-to-play's SPA twin, harsher ([one-tenth] ratio vs 1:1) and aimed at tranche default; directionality risk-to-member. The sole-exclusive-remedy sentence is a genuine proportionality device: the penalty cannot be stacked with damages.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
A Defaulting Purchaser's preferred auto-converts at [ONE-TENTH] of a Common share per preferred share; collateral terminations: board-designation/observer rights (below thresholds), and — for the converted stock — IRA registration/preemptive rights and ROFR/co-sale rights; sole-and-exclusive-remedy clause; transferee conscription (transfers only with tranche obligations assumed).
Notes
Catechism: purports conversion penalty; functions as conventional penalty with a cap. Forensics: stage 5 note.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports conversion housekeeping; the deemed amendment functions as majority rewriting.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Valuation controlsecondary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.

4. Functions

Legal
Bridge-instrument cleanup at the priced round.
Economic
The deemed-amendment clause reaches instruments and possibly HOLDERS beyond the signing purchasers ([on behalf of all holders] bracket): a majority-executed agreement rewrites the terms of outstanding securities — the SAFE-stacking reconciliation the YCS run flagged, resolved here by force of the new round's paper.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: clean discharge at conversion. Cautionary: the [all holders] bracket's reach; fixed conversion price set in this document.
  • Configuration to watch: Bracket scope; SAFE amendment-clause compatibility per instrument.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “hereby are and will be deemed for all purposes to have been amended and modified by virtue hereof to the full extent necessary to permit and facilitat…”
  • “issued in full and complete discharge and satisfaction of all obligations of the Company”

7. Framework / suite notes

YCS calibration cross-link: the Pro Rata Side Letter and SAFE conversion mechanics meet exactly here; whether a SAFE's own amendment provision permits majority amendment is the enforceability seam (FN 19/20 territory).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Purchasers' SAFEs/notes auto-convert at the Initial Closing per Exhibit A at a fixed conversion price; issued in FULL DISCHARGE of all obligations; Convertible Securities deemed AMENDED [on behalf of themselves and all holders] to the extent necessary; [tax withholding machinery].
Notes
Catechism: purports conversion housekeeping; the deemed amendment functions as majority rewriting. Forensics-light.

Defined terms (1.4)

The SPA names the suite (Transaction Agreements) and defines its fourth consent-holder class (Requisite Purchasers) — a threshold DISTINCT from the charter's Requisite Holders and the VA/ROFR investor keys: four majorities, four vocabularies, one class.

(T)	“Side Letters” means the letter agreements listed in Section 2.10(a)(iii) of the Disclosure Schedule.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category

Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Definitional architecture; the suite's roster clause.
Economic
The SPA names the suite (Transaction Agreements) and defines its fourth consent-holder class (Requisite Purchasers) — a threshold DISTINCT from the charter's Requisite Holders and the VA/ROFR investor keys: four majorities, four vocabularies, one class.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: clear drafting. Cautionary: threshold multiplicity is drift surface; Knowledge qualifiers calibrate every Section-2 rep.
  • Configuration to watch: Threshold parity at configuration.

  • ““Requisite Purchasers” means Purchasers holding [a majority] of the Shares issued pursuant to this Agreement.”

P8 check 1's richest cell: Requisite Holders (COI) / Requisite Holders (VA, redefined) / Investor % (ROFR 6.8) / Requisite Purchasers (SPA) — same people, four definitions; Side Letters defined by Disclosure-Schedule list (the shadow layer's registry).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Knowledge/Knowledge Parties (Officers, reasonable-investigation standard; patent knowledge actual-only); Requisite Purchasers ([a majority] of Shares); Tranche definitions; Transaction Agreements (the suite roster); Milestones = Annex 1; MAE; Side Letters (Disclosure-Schedule-listed).
Notes

2. Catechism / title

purports disclosure; 2.2(c)-(d) function as the labor-lock warranty.

3. Title breakdown

Stipendium laborisprimary category
Moral hinge
Labor may be paid; capital should not hide yield inside labor language.
Legitimate form
Actual labor or administration; reasonable cost; transparency; no surplus extraction; mission-serving stewardship.
Abuse form
Management, monitoring, facility, compliance, or advisory fees exceeding plausible labor or administrative cost.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Company representations — the deal's information floor and the leaver machinery's warranty layer.
Economic
2.2(c)-(d) is where the suite WARRANTS the labor lock: every share of common — the founders' and employees' — is represented to be transfer-restricted, standoff-bound, vesting-scheduled without acceleration, and repriced never. The reps make the leaver machinery a closing condition of the money.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: capitalization transparency is the deal's honest floor. Cautionary: the warranted lock-in; 'customary' as the vesting norm-setter. The prior-employer IP litigation rep (2.7) polices labor's provenance.
  • Configuration to watch: 2.2(d) Disclosure-Schedule exceptions (who escaped the lock); vesting bracket ([monthly]).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “All outstanding shares of Common Stock and all shares of Common Stock underlying outstanding options are subject to (i) a right of first refusal in fa…”
  • “as to employees, [monthly] over four years with a one-year cliff”
  • “none of the Company’s stock purchase agreements or stock option documents contains a provision for acceleration of vesting”

7. Framework / suite notes

SV14-01's CONTROL LIMB found: the vesting/no-acceleration/ROFR-standoff warranty (here) + the at-cost buyback channel (ROFR 3.1(b)) + QKH voting lapse (VA) = the full cross-document stack, now documented in all three limbs — P8 check 4's primary exhibit.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Standard org/authorization/issuance/consents/litigation reps, plus 2.2: full cap table; Stock Plan reservation; 2.2(c) ALL Common and options subject to (i) Company ROFR on transfer and (ii) 180-day IPO standoff; 2.2(d) all service-provider stock on [monthly]/4-year/1-year-cliff vesting, NO acceleration provisions anywhere, options never repriced; no other purchase/redemption obligations; waivers of purchase rights obtained.
Notes
Catechism: purports disclosure; 2.2(c)-(d) function as the labor-lock warranty. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-SPA-08SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

IP and commons reps (2.8)

The commons appears as pure hazard: the OSS rep warrants the company has NOT let copyleft obligations attach to its code — openness is a contamination to be certified against, the exact inversion of the licit pole's commons-gate fidelity; the assignment rep reaches personal work done on any amount of company time or equipment.

neutral (exemplarity)

2. Catechism / title

purports IP diligence; functions as commons-quarantine warranty.

3. Title breakdown

Digital commons stewardshipprimary category
Moral hinge
Commons value requires governance, not merely ownership.
Legitimate form
OSS/data/AI carveouts; mission locks; user/community protections; no forced enclosure through warranties or exit.
Abuse form
Investor rights or IP covenants that convert commons labor into private exit value.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
IP representations including the commons-interaction layer.
Economic
The commons appears as pure hazard: the OSS rep warrants the company has NOT let copyleft obligations attach to its code — openness is a contamination to be certified against, the exact inversion of the licit pole's commons-gate fidelity; the assignment rep reaches personal work done on any amount of company time or equipment.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: infringement hygiene, trade-secret care, provenance policing. Cautionary: the assignment breadth (no personal-projects carve-out); commons-as-contamination framing.
  • Configuration to watch: Assignment-breadth exceptions in Disclosure Schedule; (k) bracket in/out.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “The Company has not embedded, used, linked or distributed any open source, software, technologies or other materials”
  • “were developed on any amount of the Company’s time or with the use of any of the Company’s equipment, supplies, facilities or information”

7. Framework / suite notes

SUITE-J's home (commons-as-risk framing); contrast N28/N44: personal-projects carve-outs and relicense-closed bans vs any-amount-of-time assignment and copyleft-free warranties. The [Generative AI] rep is new Oct-2025 surface. SV14-05 (commons enclosure) input: framing present, enclosure machinery not in this document.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
IP sufficiency/non-infringement (knowledge-qualified [for patents/trademarks]); no material encumbrances outside Standard Outbound/Inbound Agreements; trade-secret measures; (h) employee/consultant assignment of ALL IP conceived during employment that relates to the business OR was developed on any amount of Company time/resources; (i) OPEN SOURCE rep: no copyleft use requiring Company Code disclosure, license-for-derivatives, consideration restrictions, or rights grants; (j) no government/university funding claims; (k) [Generative AI rep].
Notes
Catechism: purports IP diligence; functions as commons-quarantine warranty. Forensics: stage 5.

Stipendium laborisprimary category
Moral hinge
Labor may be paid; capital should not hide yield inside labor language.
Legitimate form
Actual labor or administration; reasonable cost; transparency; no surplus extraction; mission-serving stewardship.
Abuse form
Management, monitoring, facility, compliance, or advisory fees exceeding plausible labor or administrative cost.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Labor-layer representations.
Economic
The employment-side warranty of the leaver machinery: everyone can be terminated at will, nothing is owed on exit, and everyone senior who left has released all claims — the company warrants that labor's position is precisely as fragile as the standard stack assumes.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: wage-compliance and release hygiene. Cautionary: the at-will/no-severance warranty as investor protection — the money is conditioned on labor's terminability.
  • Configuration to watch: Scheduled exceptions (2.16(c)); [union rep] bracket.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “The employment of each employee of the Company is terminable at the will of the Company”
  • “upon termination of the employment of any such employees, no severance or other payments will become due”

7. Framework / suite notes

With 2.2(d) (no acceleration) and ROFR 3.1(b) (at-cost buyback): terminate at will -> vesting stops -> repurchase at cost — the three-step leaver sequence, each step warranted or exempted in a different suite document. P8 check 4/5 exhibit; N28's exit-door design is the drafted anti-type.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
No conflicting employee obligations; wage/withholding compliance; AT-WILL employment warranted; NO severance due on termination (exceptions scheduled); no inconsistent equity promises; departed senior employees signed full releases; ERISA plans scheduled; [no union]; [officer bad-actor history rep].
Notes
Catechism: purports employment diligence; functions as the leaver sequence's employment warranty. Forensics: with 07.
MG-NVCA-SPA-11SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)

Exemplarity (Mode 2)

Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports conditions; functions as suite assembly.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Conditions precedent both directions.
Economic
The SPA is the suite's assembler: the money closes only if the ENTIRE architecture — charter, IRA, ROFR, VA, [MRL], [the exact board] — is executed and seated. [4.6] hard-wires the control map as a funding condition; the waiver key is the purchasers' alone.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: conditioning money on the agreed architecture is the bargain made visible. Cautionary: the one-way waiver key; [4.6] making board seats a funding condition.
  • Configuration to watch: [4.6] bracket in/out; minimum-raise bracket.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “unless otherwise waived by Purchasers purchasing [at least [___]%/a majority] of the Shares in such Closing, in their sole discretion”
  • “the authorized size of the Board of Directors shall be [______], and the Board of Directors shall be comprised of”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 3's spine confirmation: the Transaction Agreements definition (1.4) + these conditions make the SPA the suite's structural keystone — every document is a closing deliverable of the money.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Purchasers' obligations conditioned on: true reps; performance; compliance certificate; qualifications; [counsel opinion]; [BOARD COMPOSITION: authorized size and named members]; Indemnification Agreements; execution of IRA, ROFR, and VA by all named parties; Restated Certificate filed; secretary's certificate; [minimum raise]; [MRL]; [SBA forms] — waivable by [%/majority] purchasers in SOLE DISCRETION. Company's conditions: mirror-thin (7).
Notes
Catechism: purports conditions; functions as suite assembly. Forensics-light.
MG-NVCA-SPA-16SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Miscellaneous: survival, fees, amendment, termination, conflicts (6.1-6.18)

Three margins tell the story: the company funds both sides' lawyers (6.8) while its own counsel discloses concurrent investor relationships (6.18); investor knowledge of a rep's falsity does not impair the claim (6.1); and the company must acknowledge the money implies nothing about future money (6.17).

Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)SPA · chars 105296–106761
…ted by any investigation or knowledge of the subject matter thereof made by or on behalf of the Purchasers or the Company.[FN id=95]
6.2	Successors and Assigns
. The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the respective successors and assigns of the parties. Nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to confer upon any party other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and assigns any rights, remedies, obligations or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement, except as expressly provided in this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, no Shares may be transferred prior to the consummation or termination of the obligation to consummate the Tranche Closing unless all rights and obligations hereunder (including without limitation the obligations to consummate the Tranche Closing) are assigned to and assumed by the transferee.
6.3	Governing Law
.[FN id=96] This Agreement shall be governed by the internal law of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of law principles that would result in the application of any law other than the law of the State of Delaware.
6.4	Counterparts
. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Counterparts may be delivered via electronic mail (including pdf or any electron…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports boilerplate; margins price the relationship.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Miscellaneous with the suite's sharpest margins.
Economic
Three margins tell the story: the company funds both sides' lawyers (6.8) while its own counsel discloses concurrent investor relationships (6.18); investor knowledge of a rep's falsity does not impair the claim (6.1); and the company must acknowledge the money implies nothing about future money (6.17).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: same-fashion rule; insolvency termination honest. Cautionary: no-sandbagging survival; fee shifting; conflicts-waiver normalization.
  • Configuration to watch: 6.8 cap blank; 6.18 bracket; arbitration/court election.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “shall survive the execution and delivery of this Agreement and the Closing and shall in no way be affected by any investigation or knowledge of the su…”
  • “the Company shall pay the reasonable fees and expenses of [_______], the counsel for [name of lead Purchaser”
  • “no Shares may be transferred prior to the consummation or termination of the obligation to consummate the Tranche Closing unless all rights and obliga…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Fees-both-sides (6.8) is the transaction-cost asymmetry the fee/cost vocabulary was built for; jury waiver = fourth verbatim instance (P8 parity datum); same-fashion rule (6.10) is the genuine equal-treatment floor.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Reps SURVIVE closing unaffected by any investigation or knowledge (no-sandbagging, 6.1); transfer conscription pre-Tranche (6.2); COMPANY PAYS lead purchaser's counsel to $[__] (6.8); [prevailing-party fees]; amendment = Company + [%] of Shares with same-fashion rule (6.10); fail-open severability; purchaser termination on DLE/IPO/insolvency (6.15); [no-additional-financing acknowledgment (6.17)]; [conflicts waiver: company counsel also represents Purchasers (6.18)]; JAMS/court + jury waiver.
Notes
Catechism: purports boilerplate; margins price the relationship. Forensics-light (6.1/6.8/6.18).

SPA — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Liquidity / exit / financing stack
Synthesis
Tranche architecture: milestone pen capital-side twice over (PD approval + sole-discretion waiver); one-way elective acceleration; SMC enforces the tranche promise
MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-02cautionary
Stack
Founder labor / equity stack
Synthesis
Warranty limb: 2.2(c)-(d) + 2.16 warrant the leaver stack as a condition of the money; SV14-01 control limb documented; assembly at P8
MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-03cautionary
Stack
Governance / operational control stack
Synthesis
Suite assembler: Transaction Agreements roster + conditions 4.x seat the exact board and require the full architecture; fourth consent vocabulary; one-way waiver key
MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-04cautionary
Stack
Information / KPI stack
Synthesis
36-vs-15 representation asymmetry; 2.36 keystone; no-sandbagging survival (6.1); disclosure flows capital-ward only
MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-05cautionary
Stack
Named-investor / side-right stack
Synthesis
Company pays lead counsel (6.8); [conflicts waiver 6.18]; Side Letters registry; [Additional-Purchaser acceptability gate]
MG-NVCA-SPA-SYN-06licit
Stack
Mitigator stack
Synthesis
POS-02 exclusive-remedy cap (the one operative protection - for a defaulting investor); same-fashion rule; several-not-joint; two-way geopolitical perimeter; honest insolvency termination

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-SPA-A — Milestone-objective configurationcautionary
Configuration
Objective Annex 1 milestones; [Requisite Purchasers] limb out of the determination; SMC at/near 1:1; [4.6] matches negotiated map; 6.8 capped low; [6.18] out
NVCA-SPA-B — Standard-filled roundpotentially extractive
Configuration
Judgment-laden milestones + PD-majority approval + sole-discretion waiver; [one-tenth] ratio; no-sandbagging enforced; fees/conflicts as drafted. = the v3.15 baseline's reading
NVCA-SPA-C — Funding-trap configurationsevere
Configuration
B + milestone pen used to withhold the tranche while the warranted labor lock and assembled architecture hold; SMC punitive ratio enforced; SV14-01 all three limbs active at suite level

NVCA Model Investors' Rights Agreement (Updated October 2025)

Risk: cautionary (configuration-pending; PE at standard fill)Sim (disclosed): 1.0663Mode-2 D: 0.053318 scored rows

The investor class's constitution: §220 waived for all while tiered channels serve the large; ROFO; four-tier amendment government; the labor pair — restraints for people, immunity for funds (SV14-01 durability limb).

…d among the Company and such Existing Investors (the “Prior Agreement”); 
WHEREAS, the undersigned Existing Investors are holders of a sufficient number of the securities of the Company as are required to amend the Prior Agreement, and desire to amend and restate the Prior Agreement in its entirety and to accept the rights created pursuant to this Agreement in lieu of the rights granted to them under the Prior Agreement; and]
WHEREAS, the Company and [certain of] the Investors are parties to that certain Series [_] Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement of even date herewith by and among the Company and such Investors (the “Purchase Agreement”), under which certain of the Company’s and such Investors’ obligations are conditioned upon the execution and delivery of this Agreement by the undersigned parties.
NOW, THEREFORE, the parties agree as follows:
1.	Definitions
. For purposes of this Agreement:
1.1	“Affiliate” means, with respect to any specified Person, any other Person who, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such Person, including, without limitation, any general partner, managing member, officer, director or trustee of such Person, or any venture capital fund or other investment fund now or hereafter existing that is controlled by one or more general partners, managing members or investment advisers of, or shares the same management company or investment advis…

MG-NVCA-IRA-02SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Definitions: the tiered-class architecture (1.1-1.42)

The document's government is set here: rights run to Major Investors (blank threshold), consents to Requisite Holders (FIFTH distinct consent vocabulary in the suite), and the Key Holders are written into the parties and out of the benefits - in the definitions, before any operative clause runs (the ROFR-02 pattern repeated).

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports lexicon; functions as the rights-class boundary.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Definitional architecture: who counts, at what size, for which rights.
Economic
The document's government is set here: rights run to Major Investors (blank threshold), consents to Requisite Holders (FIFTH distinct consent vocabulary in the suite), and the Key Holders are written into the parties and out of the benefits - in the definitions, before any operative clause runs (the ROFR-02 pattern repeated).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: clear lexicon. Cautionary: threshold blank = the class boundary is configuration; KH carve-outs = one-way scoping.
  • Configuration to watch: Major Investor threshold (FN 27: often = smallest VC's holdings); KH-exclusion bracket set.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • ““Major Investor” means any Investor that, individually or together with such Investor’s Affiliates, holds at least [______] shares of Registrable Secu…”
  • “excluding any Common Stock issued upon conversion of the Preferred Stock pursuant to the Special Mandatory Conversion”
  • ““Requisite Holders” means (a) prior to the IPO, the Investors holding [a majority/at least [___]%] of the then outstanding shares of Preferred Stock”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 1: fifth vocabulary (COI Requisite Holders / VA redefinition / ROFR Investor % / SPA Requisite Purchasers / IRA Requisite Holders-redefined). P8 check 5: 1.30's SMC exclusion is the IRA-side delivery of the SPA/COI conversion penalty - cross-document promise coherence CONFIRMED in text. Competitor def near-verbatim with ROFR 1.7 (parity datum).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Major Investor = [______]-share threshold (1.26); Requisite Holders redefined AGAIN (majority of Preferred pre-IPO / of Registrable Securities post-IPO, Sanctioned shares disregarded) (1.32); Registrable Securities EXCLUDES SMC-converted stock and carves Key Holder Registrable Securities out of demand/2.10/[3.1, 3.2, 4.1, first sentence of 6.6] (1.30); Competitor def with 20% fund carve-out + [named exception] (1.5); Side Letters imported from the SPA (1.42).
Notes
Catechism: purports lexicon; functions as the rights-class boundary. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-IRA-03SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Demand registration (2.1)

The class can compel the company into the public markets on its own timing (majority + dollar floor); the company's counterweights are real but bounded (deferral certificate, counting rules). The [Foreign Person] exclusion is CFIUS-driven (FN 13: demand rights read as 'control').

Catechism: purports demand right; functions as class-held exit timing.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

…ders other than the Initiating Holders; and (y) as soon as practicable, and in any event within 60 days after the date such request is given by the Initiating Holders, file a Form S-1 registration statement under the Securities Act covering all Registrable Securities that the Initiating Holders requested to be registered and any additional Registrable Securities requested to be included in such registration by any other Holders, as specified by notice given by each such Holder to the Company within 20 days of the date the Demand Notice is given, and in each case, subject to the limitations of Sections 2.1(c) and 2.3[; provided, however, that this right to request the filing of a Form S-1 registration statement shall in no event be made available to any Holder that is a Foreign Person].[FN id=13]
(B)	Form S-3 Demand. If at any time when it is eligible to use a Form S-3 registration statement, the Company receives a request from Holders of the Registrable Securities then outstanding that the Company file a Form S-3 registration statement with respect to outstanding Registrable Securities of such Holders having an anticipated aggregate offering price, net of Selling Expenses, of at least $5,000,000, then the Company shall (i) within ten days after the date such request is given, give a Demand Notice to all Holders other than the Initiating Holders; and (ii) as soon as practicable, and in any event within 45 days after the d…
