1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports demand right; functions as class-held exit timing.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Holder-initiated exit machinery with company breathing valves.
Economic
The class can compel the company into the public markets on its own timing (majority + dollar floor); the company's counterweights are real but bounded (deferral certificate, counting rules). The [Foreign Person] exclusion is CFIUS-driven (FN 13: demand rights read as 'control').

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: bargained exit path with real company valves. Cautionary: timing control sits with the class; blanks calibrate.
  • Configuration to watch: S-1 gate date; deferral window length; demand counts.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “this right to request the filing of a Form S-1 registration statement shall in no event be made available to any Holder that is a Foreign Person”
  • “the Company may not invoke this right more than [once] in any 12-month period”

7. Framework / suite notes

Demand rights are the registered-promise machinery P7 tracks in registered suites; here unregistered (P7 n/a) but P8 check 4 counts them in the holder-class rights accumulation.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
S-1 demand: majority of Registrable Securities, >$[20,000,000] offering, gated to [3-5 yrs] or 180 days post-IPO; S-3 demand: >$5,000,000, [two]/12-month cap; CEO deferral certificate ([30-120] days, [once]/12 months, no self-registration during deferral); effected-counting rules; [no S-1 demand for Foreign Persons].
Notes
Catechism: purports demand right; functions as class-held exit timing. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-04SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Piggyback registration + underwriter cutbacks (2.2-2.3)

The cutback ladder is the waterfall in registration form: company first (its own offering), then investors, then everyone else - and [within the Holders, Key Holder shares exit the queue first]. Priority is stacked twice against the common-and-founder layer.

Catechism: purports allocation mechanics; functions as exit-priority ladder.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)IRA · chars 25343–26834
… Initiating Holders in writing that marketing factors require a limitation on the number of shares to be underwritten, then the Initiating Holders shall so advise all Holders of Registrable Securities that otherwise would be underwritten pursuant hereto, and the number of Registrable Securities that may be included in the underwriting shall be allocated among such Holders of Registrable Securities, including the Initiating Holders, in proportion (as nearly as practicable) to the number of Registrable Securities owned by each Holder or in such other proportion as shall mutually be agreed to by all such selling Holders; provided, however, that the number of Registrable Securities held by the Holders to be included in such underwriting shall not be reduced unless all other securities are first entirely excluded from the underwriting. To facilitate the allocation of shares in accordance with the above provisions, the Company or the underwriters may round the number of shares allocated to any Holder to the nearest 100 shares.
(B)	In connection with any offering involving an underwriting of shares of the Company’s capital stock pursuant to Section 2.2, the Company shall not be required to include any of the Holders’ Registrable Securities in such underwriting unless the Holders accept the terms of the underwriting as agreed upon between the Company and its underwriters, and then only in such quantity as the underwriters in their sole discretion determine will not jeopardiz…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)IRA · chars 28217–29599
…unless all other securities (other than securities to be sold by the Company) are first entirely excluded from the offering, [or] (ii) the number of Registrable Securities included in the offering be reduced below [20-30]% of the total number of securities included in such offering, unless such offering is the IPO, in which case the selling Holders may be excluded further if the underwriters make the determination described above and no other stockholder’s securities are included in such offering [or (iii) notwithstanding (ii) above, any Registrable Securities which are not Key Holder Registrable Securities be excluded from such underwriting unless all Key Holder Registrable Securities are first excluded from such offering].[FN id=16] For purposes of the provision in this Section 2.3(b) concerning apportionment, for any selling Holder that is a partnership, limited liability company, or corporation, the partners, members, retired partners, retired members, stockholders, and Affiliates of such Holder, or the estates and Immediate Family Members of any such partners, retired partners, members, and retired members and any trusts for the benefit of any of the foregoing Persons, shall be deemed to be a single “selling Holder,” and any pro rata reduction with respect to such “selling Holder” shall be based upon the aggregate number of Registrable Securities owned by…

2. Catechism / title

purports allocation mechanics; functions as exit-priority ladder.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Priority queue for public-market access.
Economic
The cutback ladder is the waterfall in registration form: company first (its own offering), then investors, then everyone else - and [within the Holders, Key Holder shares exit the queue first]. Priority is stacked twice against the common-and-founder layer.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: underwriting mechanics. Cautionary: the double-stacked queue.
  • Configuration to watch: [(iii)] KH-first-out bracket; [20-30]% floor.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the number of Registrable Securities held by the Holders to be included in such underwriting shall not be reduced unless all other securities are firs…”
  • “unless all Key Holder Registrable Securities are first excluded from such offering”

7. Framework / suite notes

FN 6 confirms the design: KH registration rights, where granted at all, ride subordinate. P8 check 4 accumulation datum.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Company-registration piggyback; underwriting cutback hierarchy: Holders' Registrable Securities reduced only after ALL other securities excluded; [20-30]% floor except IPO; [KH Registrable Securities excluded before investor RS]; Holder underwriting reps limited + several-not-joint + net-proceeds cap.
Notes
Catechism: purports allocation mechanics; functions as exit-priority ladder. Forensics-light.
MG-NVCA-IRA-05SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Company obligations, expenses, no-injunction (2.4-2.7)

Fees-both-sides again (the company funds the class's counsel per registration - the SPA 6.8 pattern as standing covenant), and the remedy geometry is one-way: the class can compel registration, the company cannot be enjoined by a dissenting Holder - dissent within the class is disarmed in the company's favor, but the machinery it protects is the class's.

Catechism: purports mechanics; prices the machinery onto the company.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Economics / priority stackConfidence: High

…e request with reasonable promptness after learning of such information then the Holders shall not be required to pay any of such expenses and shall not forfeit their right to one registration pursuant to Sections 2.1(a) or 2.1(b). All Selling Expenses relating to Registrable Securities registered pursuant to this Section 2 [(other than fees and disbursements of counsel to any Holder, other than the Selling Holder Counsel, which shall be borne solely by the Holder engaging such counsel)] shall be borne and paid by the Holders pro rata on the basis of the number of Registrable Securities registered on their behalf.
2.7	Delay of Registration
. No Holder shall have any right to obtain or seek an injunction restraining or otherwise delaying any registration pursuant to this Agreement as the result of any controversy that might arise with respect to the interpretation or implementation of this Section 2.
2.8	Indemnification
.[FN id=20] If any Registrable Securities are included in a registration statement under this Section 2 [or in connection with a Direct Listing, as applicable]:
(A)	To the extent permitted by law, the Company will indemnify and hold harmless each selling Holder, and the partners, members, officers, directors, and stockholders of each such Holder; legal counsel and accountants for each such Holder; any underwriter (as defined in the Securities Act) for each such Holder; and ea…

…ecurities as is reasonably required to effect the registration of such Holder’s Registrable Securities.
2.6	Expenses of Registration
. All expenses (other than Selling Expenses) incurred in connection with registrations, filings, or qualifications pursuant to Section 2, including all registration, filing, and qualification fees; printers’ and accounting fees; fees and disbursements of counsel for the Company; and the reasonable fees and disbursements[, not to exceed $[50,000] [per registration],] of one counsel for the selling Holders selected by Holders of a majority of the Registrable Securities to be registered (“Selling Holder Counsel”), shall be borne and paid by the Company; provided, however, that the Company shall not be required to pay for any expenses of any registration proceeding begun pursuant to Section 2.1 if the registration request is subsequently withdrawn at the request of the Holders of a majority of the Registrable Securities to be registered (in which case all selling Holders shall bear such expenses pro rata based upon the number of Registrable Securities that were to be included in the withdrawn registration), unless the Holders of a majority of the Registrable Securities agree to forfeit their right to one registration pursuant to Sections 2.1(a) or 2.1(b), as the case may be; provided furthe…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports mechanics; prices the machinery onto the company.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
The cost and remedy allocation of the registration machinery.
Economic
Fees-both-sides again (the company funds the class's counsel per registration - the SPA 6.8 pattern as standing covenant), and the remedy geometry is one-way: the class can compel registration, the company cannot be enjoined by a dissenting Holder - dissent within the class is disarmed in the company's favor, but the machinery it protects is the class's.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: expense clarity; MAC escape is honest. Cautionary: cost direction; 2.4(h) opens the books to sellers' agents.
  • Configuration to watch: Counsel cap blank; audit bracket at 3.1(a).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “No Holder shall have any right to obtain or seek an injunction restraining or otherwise delaying any registration”
  • “shall be borne and paid by the Company”

7. Framework / suite notes

Transaction-cost asymmetry vocabulary (SPA 6.8, IRA 2.6, [IRA 5.x Investor Counsel]) - three sites, P8 check 4.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Full registration-mechanics duties incl. diligence access for selling Holders' agents (2.4(h)); insider-trading policy must permit director 10b5-1 plans; Holder information condition (2.5); Company pays ALL registration expenses + Selling Holder Counsel [$50,000/registration], withdrawal-forfeit rules with MAC escape (2.6); Holders may NEVER enjoin a registration (2.7).
Notes
Catechism: purports mechanics; prices the machinery onto the company. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-06SEC-2cautionaryprotective

Registration indemnification (2.8)

Asymmetric by design: the company's exposure is unbounded, the Holder's is fenced to its own statements and capped at what it took out - risk follows authorship and the deep pocket. The cap is the document's one self-executing liability protection.

Catechism: purports mutual indemnity; functions as asymmetric allocation with a member-side cap.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · periculum_sortisStack: Economics / priority stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)IRA · chars 40342–41834
…the sale of Registrable Securities to the Person asserting the claim]; and each such selling Holder will pay to the Company and each other aforementioned Person any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred thereby in connection with investigating or defending any claim or proceeding from which Damages may result, as such expenses are incurred; provided, however, that the indemnity agreement contained in this Section 2.8(b) shall not apply to amounts paid in settlement of any such claim or proceeding if such settlement is effected without the consent of the Holder, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; and provided further that in no event shall the aggregate amounts payable by any Holder by way of indemnity or contribution under Sections 2.8(b) and 2.8(d) exceed the proceeds from the offering received by such Holder (net of any Selling Expenses paid by such Holder), except in the case of fraud or willful misconduct by such Holder.
(C)	Promptly after receipt by an indemnified party under this Section 2.8 of notice of the commencement of any action (including any governmental action) for which a party may be entitled to indemnification hereunder, such indemnified party will, if a claim in respect thereof is to be made against any indemnifying party under this Section 2.8, give the indemnifying party notice of the commencement thereof. The indemnifying party shall have the right to participate in such action and, to the extent the indemnifying party so des…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
protective
Positive form
POS-02 — Restorative compensation only — Charges track documented actual loss, never opportunity assumed.
Materiality M
0.48 (b1.0 operative unbracketed; s0.8 survives offering completion and Agreement termination (2.8(f)); l0.6 answers unlimited-liability risk within the registration stack partially (fraud/willful carve-out; company side unbounded))
Enforceability test
Holder liability cap (2.8(b)+(d) combined): operative, self-executing, survives via 2.8(f).
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports mutual indemnity; functions as asymmetric allocation with a member-side cap.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Periculum sortissecondary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.

4. Functions

Legal
Securities-liability allocation for registrations.
Economic
Asymmetric by design: the company's exposure is unbounded, the Holder's is fenced to its own statements and capped at what it took out - risk follows authorship and the deep pocket. The cap is the document's one self-executing liability protection.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: authorship-based allocation. Cautionary: uncapped company side as configured norm.
  • Configuration to watch: Settlement-consent brackets; corrected-writing brackets.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “in no event shall the aggregate amounts payable by any Holder by way of indemnity or contribution under Sections 2.8(b) and 2.8(d) exceed the proceeds…”

7. Framework / suite notes

POS-02 type (liability cap) - the SPA SMC cap's registration-layer sibling: same protective geometry (cap what a member can lose), different subject.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Company indemnifies Holders/underwriters/controlling persons for Damages, uncapped, expenses-as-incurred; each Holder indemnifies severally, ONLY for its own furnished information, CAPPED at net offering proceeds except fraud/willful misconduct (2.8(b), mirrored at contribution 2.8(d)); procedures; underwriting-agreement supremacy; obligations SURVIVE (2.8(f)).
Notes
Catechism: purports mutual indemnity; functions as asymmetric allocation with a member-side cap. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-07SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Limitation on subsequent registration rights (2.10)

The class locks the door behind itself: future capital may join the queue only behind or beside, never ahead - rights-accumulation control held by the incumbent class (FN 21 warns against single-investor blocking rights).

Catechism: purports future-rights hygiene; functions as incumbent-class entrenchment.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…ct to such reporting requirements), or that it qualifies as a registrant whose securities may be resold pursuant to Form S-3 (at any time after the Company so qualifies); and (ii) such other information as may be reasonably requested in availing any Holder of any rule or regulation of the SEC that permits the selling of any such securities without registration (at any time after the Company has become subject to the reporting requirements under the Exchange Act) or pursuant to Form S-3 (at any time after the Company so qualifies to use such form).
2.10	Limitations on Subsequent Registration Rights
. From and after the date of this Agreement, the Company shall not, without the prior written consent of the Requisite Holders, enter into any agreement with any holder or prospective holder of any securities of the Company that would (i) [provide to such holder or prospective holder the right to include securities in any registration on other than either a pro rata basis with respect to the Registrable Securities or on a subordinate basis after all Holders have had the opportunity to include in the registration and offering all shares of Registrable Securities that they wish to so include]/[allow such holder or prospective holder to include such securities in any registration unless, under the terms of such agreement, such holder or prospective holder may include such securities in any such registration only to the extent that the inclusion of such securities …

2. Catechism / title

purports future-rights hygiene; functions as incumbent-class entrenchment.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Class-protective exclusivity over the registration machinery.
Economic
The class locks the door behind itself: future capital may join the queue only behind or beside, never ahead - rights-accumulation control held by the incumbent class (FN 21 warns against single-investor blocking rights).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: protects the bargain against dilution by side deal. Cautionary: entrenchment as configured; consent key = Requisite Holders (vocabulary #5).
  • Configuration to watch: Which alternative bracket; interaction with Side Letters.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the Company shall not, without the prior written consent of the Requisite Holders, enter into any agreement with any holder or prospective holder of a…”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 4: the accumulation clause proper - the suite's rights architecture is made self-entrenching here.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
No new registration rights to anyone without Requisite Holders' consent unless subordinate/pro-rata [alternative formulations]; no new demand rights at all; 6.9 joinders exempt.
Notes
Catechism: purports future-rights hygiene; functions as incumbent-class entrenchment. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-08SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Investor market stand-off (2.11)

The suite's second standoff: investors accept the same 180-day cage founders warranted in SPA 2.2(c) - but the investor version carries a PARITY CONDITION (bound only if insiders are equally bound) and [a pro-rata waiver rule]; the founder version (ROFR 5) carries a most-restrictive-terms ratchet instead. Same cage, different linings.

Catechism: purports market hygiene; functions as conditioned mutual cage.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

The underwriters in connection with such registration are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Section 2.11 and shall have the right, power and authority to enforce the provisions hereof as though they were a party hereto. Each Holder further agrees to execute such agreements as may be reasonably requested by the underwriters in connection with such registration …

Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports market hygiene; functions as conditioned mutual cage.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
The investor-side lock-up.
Economic
The suite's second standoff: investors accept the same 180-day cage founders warranted in SPA 2.2(c) - but the investor version carries a PARITY CONDITION (bound only if insiders are equally bound) and [a pro-rata waiver rule]; the founder version (ROFR 5) carries a most-restrictive-terms ratchet instead. Same cage, different linings.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: underwriting necessity, mutual in principle; the parity condition is genuine equal-treatment text. Cautionary: [sole-discretion release bracket] reopens selectivity.
  • Configuration to watch: [1-3]% threshold; IPO-only bracket; waiver-release blank.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “shall be applicable to the Holders only if all officers and directors of the Company are subject to the same restrictions”
  • “The underwriters in connection with such registration are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Section 2.11”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 4/5 exhibit: lock-up parity RESOLVED - both classes bound, asymmetry lives in the conditions (parity floor + pro-rata waiver for investors; ratchet for Key Holders). FN 25: waiver brackets exist for small-employee liquidity.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Holder lock-up: 180 days IPO [90 days other registrations] [IPO-only bracket]; broad transfer/hedge ban; PARITY CONDITION: applies only if all officers/directors equally bound + company efforts on >[1-3]% stockholders; underwriters are third-party beneficiaries; [discretionary waivers must be pro rata, except sole-discretion release up to [___] shares].
Notes
Catechism: purports market hygiene; functions as conditioned mutual cage. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-IRA-09SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Transfer restrictions, legends, termination/suspension (2.12-2.13)

Mostly securities-law hygiene rather than exit-gauntlet (contrast ROFR): the restrictions protect the exemption, not the class; the sanctions suspension is the geopolitical perimeter operating on rights already granted.

[Any discretionary waiver or termination of the restrictions of any or all of such agreements by the Company or the underwriters shall apply pro rata to all Company stockholders that are subject to such agreements, based on the number of shares subject to such agreements[, except that, notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company and the underwriters may, in their sole discretion, waive or terminate these restrictions with respect to up to [___] shares of the Common Stock].][FN id=25]
2.12	Restrictions on Transfer
.[FN id=26]
(A)	The Preferred Stock and the Registrable Securities shall not be sold, pledged, or otherwise transferred, and the Company shall not recognize and shall issue stop-transfer instructions to its transfer agent with respect to any such sale, pledge, or transfer, except upon the conditions specified in this Agreement, which conditions are intended to ensure compliance with the provisions of the Securities Act and all other applicable U.S. laws and regulations. A transferring Holder will cause any proposed purchaser, pledgee, or transferee of the Preferred Stock and the Registrable Securities held by such Holder to agree to take and hold such securities subject to the provisions and upon the conditions specified in this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company shall not require any transferee of shares pursuant to an effective registrati…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports compliance; functions as compliance.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Compliance-driven transfer machinery + rights sunset.
Economic
Mostly securities-law hygiene rather than exit-gauntlet (contrast ROFR): the restrictions protect the exemption, not the class; the sanctions suspension is the geopolitical perimeter operating on rights already granted.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: exemption hygiene; honest sunsets. Cautionary: sole-discretion waiver key at 6.6(a) incl. waiver-by-silence.
  • Configuration to watch: Termination bracket set; anniversary blank.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the Company shall not recognize and shall issue stop-transfer instructions to its transfer agent”
  • “shall be suspended during any time as such Holder is a Sanctioned Party”

7. Framework / suite notes

Genre note for the suite memo: the IRA's transfer restrictions are law-facing where the ROFR's are class-facing - same vocabulary, opposite direction.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Securities-law transfer conditions: stop-transfer instructions, two-legend notation, opinion-of-counsel gate (waivable by Company in SOLE DISCRETION, and silence = deemed waiver per 6.6(a)); transferee conscription; registration rights terminate at [DLE with cash/comparable-rights proviso], [1% + full Rule 144 liquidity], [3rd-5th] IPO anniversary; rights SUSPENDED while a Holder is a Sanctioned Party.
Notes
Catechism: purports compliance; functions as compliance. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-10SEC-3cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Information and inspection rights (3.1-3.2)

The suite's information plumbing: everything flows to the Major Investors on a calendar, budget discipline is board-approved and investor-visible, and the company's protections are carve-outs (trade secrets, privilege) rather than reciprocity. With the MRL and COI 18 this is the machinery the information_mrl_shadow_governance flag was calibrated on - but the flag's sharpest edge here is 3.7, not 3.1.

Catechism: purports reporting; functions as tiered information government.Geometry: information_asymmetry · governance_asymmetryStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High

(E)	The right of any Holder to request registration or inclusion of Registrable Securities in any registration pursuant to Section 2.1 or 2.2 shall be suspended during any time as such Holder is a Sanctioned Party.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports reporting; functions as tiered information government.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
The contractual information channel to the major class.
Economic
The suite's information plumbing: everything flows to the Major Investors on a calendar, budget discipline is board-approved and investor-visible, and the company's protections are carve-outs (trade secrets, privilege) rather than reciprocity. With the MRL and COI 18 this is the machinery the information_mrl_shadow_governance flag was calibrated on - but the flag's sharpest edge here is 3.7, not 3.1.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: monitoring rights against real money; carve-outs are honest. Cautionary: class-tiered access; Competitor gate is board-judgment.
  • Configuration to watch: Major Investor threshold (shared with 1.26); audit bracket; monthly bracket.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “provided that such Major Investor is not a Competitor”
  • “by uploading the information to a portfolio management platform”
  • “a comparison between (x) the actual amounts as of and for such fiscal year and (y) the comparable amounts for the prior year and as included in the Ap…”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 information-valve map, limb 2 of 4: COI 18 (waivable statutory floor) / IRA 3.1-3.2 (contract channel to Majors) / IRA 3.7 (statutory waiver + crumb) / MRL (fund-side VCOC channel). Budget comparison = KPI surface.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Major Investors [not Competitors] receive: audited annuals within 180 days [with budget-vs-actual comparison], quarterlies in 45, [monthlies in 30], quarterly cap-table statements, the Approved Annual Budget; board-approved Budget process; [portfolio-platform upload on request]; inspection at investor expense with trade-secret/privilege carve-outs; pre-registration blackout valve.
Notes
Catechism: purports reporting; functions as tiered information government. No forensics.

4. Functions

Legal
Named-investor board presence without a seat.
Economic
The shadow-seat: full board information flow and meeting presence, none of the fiduciary duty of a director - the named-investor stack's information limb (VA seats, IRA observer, Side Letters).

[Named investor above [____] shares gets a nonvoting board observer with all board materials [promptly]; confidentiality condition; exclusion valves: privilege, trade secrets, competitive harm, conflicts [, Competitor status].]

. MG-NVCA-IRA-13SEC-3cautionaryprotective

Waiver of statutory information rights + the crumb (3.7)

The information valve's keystone: the statutory right every stockholder holds AS a stockholder is waived by all Investors, while the contractual replacement (3.1-3.2) runs only to Major Investors - information moves from law to contract and from all to the large. What the small investor gets back is a quarterly cap table. The company gains litigation peace (220 demands foreclosed); the major class gains relative position; the burden lands inside the investor class itself.

Catechism: purports waiver housekeeping; functions as re-basing of information from ownership to size.Geometry: information_asymmetry · governance_asymmetryStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High

.[FN id=39]
(A)	Each Investor hereby acknowledges and agrees that until the consummation of the [earlier of the ]IPO[ or Direct Listing, as applicable], such Investor shall hereby be deemed to have unconditionally and irrevocably, to the fullest extent permitted by law, on behalf of such Investor and all beneficial owners of the shares of Common Stock or Preferred Stock owned by such Investor (a “Beneficial Owner”), waived, and does hereby so waive, any rights such Investor or a Beneficial Owner might otherwise have had under Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law (or under similar rights under other applicable law) to inspect for any proper purpose and to make copies and extracts from the Company’s stock ledger, a list of its stockholders and its other books and records or the books and records of any subsidiary. This waiver applies only in such Investor’s capacity as a stockholder and does not affect any other information and inspection rights such Investor may expressly have pursuant to Sections 3.1, 3.2, or 3.7(b) of this Agreement. 2. Catechism / title

purports waiver housekeeping; functions as re-basing of information from ownership to size.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Statutory-floor removal with a contractual remainder.
Economic
The information valve's keystone: the statutory right every stockholder holds AS a stockholder is waived by all Investors, while the contractual replacement (3.1-3.2) runs only to Major Investors - information moves from law to contract and from all to the large. What the small investor gets back is a quarterly cap table. The company gains litigation peace (220 demands foreclosed); the major class gains relative position; the burden lands inside the investor class itself.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: 220 litigation is a real cost; the 3.7(b) duty is real. Cautionary: the tier boundary; irrevocability; Beneficial-Owner reach. PE as configured: with a high Major threshold, small checks are dark.
  • Configuration to watch: Major threshold (the same blank now controls information class membership); crumb frequency.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “waived, and does hereby so waive, any rights such Investor or a Beneficial Owner might otherwise have had under Section 220 of the Delaware General Co…”
  • “Within 15 days after request by any Investor that is not a Major Investor (but no more frequently than once per each quarter”

7. Framework / suite notes

COI 18's contractual twin - the charter waives 220 for stockholders at the corporate layer, the IRA re-waives it for Investors at the contract layer, and both substitute class-tiered channels: the information-valve architecture COMPLETE (P8 check 4 exhibit; flag calibration site).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
EVERY Investor irrevocably waives DGCL 220 books-and-records inspection (for itself AND its Beneficial Owners) until IPO [or Direct Listing], with a warranted-counsel-review recital; non-Major Investors may request (quarterly at most) a cap-table statement, delivered within 15 days (3.7(b)).
Notes
Catechism: purports waiver housekeeping; functions as re-basing of information from ownership to size. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-IRA-14SEC-4cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Right of first offer on new issuances (4.1-4.2)

Every future financing is pre-mortgaged to the incumbent majors (with super-allocation letting the fully-exercising absorb the passive), and the [use-it-or-lose-it] bracket disciplines the class itself; the company's counterweights are the windows, the no-better-terms resale lane, and the charter's Exempted list.

Catechism: purports pro rata courtesy; functions as pre-mortgaged financing path.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

…res of capital stock outstanding at the end of the period, the Common Stock issuable upon conversion or exercise of any outstanding securities convertible or exercisable for Common Stock and the exchange ratio or exercise price applicable thereto, and the number of shares of issued stock options and stock options not yet issued but reserved for issuance, if any, all in sufficient detail as to permit the relevant Investors to calculate its respective percentage equity ownership in the Company.
4.	Rights to Future Stock Issuances.
4.1	Right of First Offer
. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Section 4.1 and applicable securities laws, if the Company proposes to offer or sell any New Securities, the Company shall first offer such New Securities to each Major Investor.[FN id=40] A Major Investor shall be entitled to apportion the right of first offer hereby granted to it in such proportions as it deems appropriate, among (i) itself, (ii) its Affiliates and (iii) its beneficial interest holders, such as limited partners, members or any other Person having “beneficial ownership,” as such term is defined in Rule 13d-3 promulgated under the Exchange Act, of such Major Investor (“Investor Beneficial Owners”); provided that each such Affiliate or Investor Beneficial Owner (x) is not a Competitor [or FOIA Party], unless such party’s purchase of New Securities is otherwise consented to by the Board of Directors, …

Following any such termination, such Investor shall no longer be deemed a “Major Investor” for any purpose of this Section 4.1]

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports pro rata courtesy; functions as pre-mortgaged financing path.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Preemptive machinery for the major class.
Economic
Every future financing is pre-mortgaged to the incumbent majors (with super-allocation letting the fully-exercising absorb the passive), and the [use-it-or-lose-it] bracket disciplines the class itself; the company's counterweights are the windows, the no-better-terms resale lane, and the charter's Exempted list.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: anti-dilution participation against real money; honest exceptions. Cautionary: financing-path encumbrance; joinder conscription into IRA+VA extends the suite's reach through the class's own LP networks.
  • Configuration to watch: Threshold reuse; [use-it-or-lose-it] bracket; [post-issuance alternative].

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “if the Company proposes to offer or sell any New Securities, the Company shall first offer such New Securities to each Major Investor”
  • “shall terminate with respect to any Major Investor who fails to purchase, in any transaction subject to this Section 4.1”

7. Framework / suite notes

6.6(b)(i)'s Participating-Investor proviso (FN 65) is this section's fairness patch: waived rounds must offer proportional participation - intra-class equal-treatment, the ROFR (c)(i) pattern at suite scale.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Major Investors get pro rata first offer on ALL New Securities + oversubscription of the unsubscribed; apportionable to Affiliates and Investor Beneficial Owners (LPs) if not Competitor/[FOIA]/Sanctioned and joined to IRA+VA; 20-day windows; company may sell the remainder on no-better terms in [90-120] days, else re-offer; exceptions: Exempted Securities (charter), IPO shares[, Purchase Agreement preferred]; [use-it-or-lose-it: Major status lost for 4.1 on any pass]; [post-issuance notice alternative].
Notes
Catechism: purports pro rata courtesy; functions as pre-mortgaged financing path. No forensics.
MG-NVCA-IRA-15SEC-5cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

The labor pair: employee agreements + right to conduct activities (5.2, 5.6)

The suite's sharpest inversion pair in one section: labor must be put under [non-competes] as a standing covenant to the capital, the leaver machinery is board-locked against softening (no alteration of restricted stock agreements - the SPA 2.2(d) warranty made durable), [severance is capped by covenant], while the funds are contractually IMMUNIZED to fund the company's competitors. The employee may not compete; the owner may.

Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)IRA · chars 90424–91946
…and related proprietary information of many enterprises, some of which may compete directly or indirectly with the Company’s business (as currently conducted or as currently propose to be conducted). Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude or in any way restrict the Professional Investment Organization from evaluating or purchasing securities, including publicly traded securities, of a particular enterprise, or investing or participating in any particular enterprise whether or not such enterprise has products or services that compete with those of the Company; and the Company hereby agrees that, to the extent permitted under applicable law, no Professional Investment Organization shall be liable to the Company for any claim arising out of, or based upon, (i) the investment by such Professional Investment Organization in any entity competitive with the Company, or (ii) actions taken by any partner, officer, employee or other representative of such Professional Investment Organization to assist any such competitive company, whether or not such action was taken as a member of the board of directors of such competitive company or otherwise, and whether or not such action has a detrimental effect on the Company; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not contravene the confidentiality obligations in Section 3.5 or otherwise in this Agreement or relieve any director or officer of the Company from any liability associated with such person’s fiduciary duties to the Company.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)

Confidence

2. Catechism / title

5.6 purports acknowledgment of fund business models; the pair functions as asymmetric competition rights (societas inversion: the partner may defect, the worker may not).

3. Title breakdown

Stipendium laborisprimary category
Moral hinge
Labor may be paid; capital should not hide yield inside labor language.
Legitimate form
Actual labor or administration; reasonable cost; transparency; no surplus extraction; mission-serving stewardship.
Abuse form
Management, monitoring, facility, compliance, or advisory fees exceeding plausible labor or administrative cost.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Employment-layer standing covenants + the fund-side non-compete immunity.
Economic
The suite's sharpest inversion pair in one section: labor must be put under [non-competes] as a standing covenant to the capital, the leaver machinery is board-locked against softening (no alteration of restricted stock agreements - the SPA 2.2(d) warranty made durable), [severance is capped by covenant], while the funds are contractually IMMUNIZED to fund the company's competitors. The employee may not compete; the owner may.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: trade-secret hygiene; board approval as escape hatch. Cautionary-to-PE as configured: the pair's direction; [$200,000] severance ceiling as investor protection.
  • Configuration to watch: Non-compete bracket (jurisdiction-dependent); severance cap blank; board-approval practice.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “non-competition and non-solicitation agreement”
  • “shall not amend, modify, terminate, waive, or otherwise alter, in whole or in part, any of the above-referenced agreements or any restricted stock agr…”
  • “no Professional Investment Organization shall be liable to the Company for any claim arising out of, or based upon, (i) the investment by such Profess…”

7. Framework / suite notes

SV14-01 durability limb: 5.2(b) converts the SPA's point-in-time no-acceleration warranty into a STANDING no-softening covenant - the lock now has a lock. FN 46's 'good governance constructs' framing noted. N28 comparator: the drafted suite's restraints are severance-cushioned and mission-scoped; here restraint is maximal and the cushion is capped by covenant.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
5.2: every employee/consultant with confidential access must sign NDA + proprietary-rights assignment [+ non-compete and non-solicit to the extent permissible]; company may NOT alter ANY such agreement or any restricted stock agreement without board approval [; no severance plan over $[200,000] per individual]. 5.6: Professional Investment Organizations expressly free to invest in and assist competitors, with liability waived except confidentiality/fiduciary carve-outs.
Notes
Catechism: 5.6 purports acknowledgment of fund business models; the pair functions as asymmetric competition rights (societas inversion: the partner may defect, the worker may not). Forensics: stage 5.

… without entry into such agreement and shall, at the same time, in good faith work expeditiously to enable Investor Counsel and its clients to negotiate and enter into the appropriate agreement(s) without undue burden to the clients of Investor Counsel.]
5.5	[Indemnification Matters
. The Company hereby acknowledges that one or more of the directors affiliated with one or more Investors (“Investor Directors”) may have certain rights to indemnification, advancement of expenses and/or insurance provided by one or more of the Investors and certain of their Affiliates (collectively, the “Investor Indemnitors”). The Company hereby agrees (a) that it is the indemnitor of first resort (i.e., its obligations to any such Investor Director are primary and any obligation of the Investor Indemnitors to advance expenses or to provide indemnification for the same expenses or liabilities incurred by such Investor Director are secondary), (b) that it shall be required to advance the full amount of expenses incurred by such Investor Director and shall be liable for the full amount of all expenses, judgments, penalties, fines and amounts paid in settlement by or on behalf of any such Investor Director to the extent legally permitted and as required by the Certificate of Incorporation or Bylaws of the Company (or any agreement betwee…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)IRA · chars 84557–86048
… defined in the Voting Agreement of even date herewith among the Investors, the Company and the other parties named therein) (each, a “Reimbursed Transaction”), the reasonable fees and disbursements, not to exceed $[150,000] in the aggregate, of one counsel for the Investors (“Investor Counsel”), solely in their capacities as stockholders, shall be borne and paid by the Company for each Reimbursed Transaction. 
(B)	Sale of the Company. At the outset of considering a transaction, which if consummated would constitute a Sale of the Company, the Company shall obtain the ability to share with the Investor Counsel (and such counsel’s clients) and shall share the confidential information (including, without limitation, the initial and all subsequent drafts of memoranda of understanding, letters of intent and other transaction documents and related noncompete, employment, consulting and other compensation agreements and plans) pertaining to and memorializing any of the transactions, which, individually or when aggregated with others, would constitute the Sale of the Company. The Company shall be obligated to share (and cause the Company’s counsel and investment bankers to share) such materials when distributed to the Company’s executives and/or any one or more of the other parties to such transaction(s).
(C)	IPO. If in connection with the IPO the underwriters or the Company request the execution of additional agreements pursuant to Section 2.11, the Company shall share (and…

…s obligation to furnish such written statement shall continue notwithstanding the fact that a class of the Company’s stock may be regularly traded on an established securities market or the fact that there is no Preferred Stock then outstanding; provided, such obligation shall terminate as to any particular Investor when the Investor no longer owns shares of Preferred Stock or any shares of Common Stock issued upon conversion of Preferred Stock.][FN id=54]
5.11	[CFIUS and Foreign Person Limitations.[FN id=55]
(A)	Unless otherwise approved by the Board of Directors, the Company will not provide to any Foreign Person any DPA Triggering Rights. No Investor that is a Foreign Person shall be permitted to obtain any DPA Triggering Rights or a voting equity interest in the Company that exceeds 10%[FN id=56] of the Company’s total voting securities pursuant to the Purchase Agreement, Section 4 of this Agreement, or otherwise, including by way of any secondary transaction(s), without the approval of the Board of Directors.
(B)	Each Investor covenants that it will notify the Company in advance of permitting any Foreign Person affiliated with Investor, whether affiliated as a limited partner or otherwise, to obtain through Investor any DPA Triggering Rights.] [FN id=57]
[FN id=58]

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports housekeeping covenants; functions as standing government of the investee.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
The standing-covenant deck.
Economic
Post-closing government by covenant: the company's insurance, tax posture, governance policies, compliance programs, and even its subsidiaries' board mechanics are contract duties to the class; the M&A information covenant is the sharpest - Investor Counsel sees every draft the executives see, so the class is inside the company's exit deliberations by right.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: insurance, compliance, governance hygiene; mutual outbound notices. Cautionary: bracket-density = per-deal government; M&A-drafts covenant; first-resort cost shift.
  • Configuration to watch: Which brackets in; Annex 2 contents; counsel cap.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “it is the indemnitor of first resort”
  • “shall share the confidential information (including, without limitation, the initial and all subsequent drafts of memoranda of understanding, letters …”
  • “No Investor that is a Foreign Person shall be permitted to obtain any DPA Triggering Rights or a voting equity interest in the Company that exceeds 10…”

7. Framework / suite notes

QSBS covenant = SPA 2.x rep's living twin (P8 check 5 pair CONFIRMED: checklist duty here, eligibility rep there, same gross-negligence floor both sides). First-resort indemnification shifts director-protection cost company-ward (named-investor stack).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[D&O + key-person insurance, company as loss payee, PD-holder certification rights]; [QSBS covenant + 20-day checklist duty, gross-negligence-only liability]; successor indemnification; [Investor Counsel expenses to $[150,000] per Reimbursed Transaction + mandatory sharing of ALL M&A drafts/LOIs/comp agreements with Investor Counsel when execs get them + joint-defense machinery]; [indemnitor-of-first-resort (Levy v. HLI)]; [Required Governance Policies per Annex 2]; [FCPA + certification-on-request]; [cybersecurity program]; [CFIUS: no DPA Triggering Rights, Foreign Persons capped at 10% voting]; [Outbound Investment Rules mutual notices]; [subsidiary governance mirror]; termination at IPO/DLE except survival of successor-indemnification[, 5.6(a), first-resort].
Notes
Catechism: purports housekeeping covenants; functions as standing government of the investee. Forensics-light (M&A information covenant).
MG-NVCA-IRA-17SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Miscellaneous: assignment, amendment government, disputes (6.1-6.13)

Government of the rights excludes most of the governed, tiered twice: Requisite Holders hold the general keys, the Major sub-class holds ITS OWN sections' keys (the large amend the large's rights), named investors hold personal vetoes, [the Key Holders hold only a disproportionate-harm shield, bracketed], and new members join without anyone's consent while non-consenters are bound.

Catechism: purports boilerplate; functions as tiered self-government of the rights layer.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…ave been given. Each party to this Agreement agrees to promptly notify the Company of any change in such stockholder’s electronic mail address, and that failure to do so shall not affect the foregoing. 
6.6	Amendments and Waivers
.[FN id=64] 
(A)	Any term of this Agreement may be amended, modified or terminated and the observance of any term of this Agreement may be waived (either generally or in a particular instance, and either retroactively or prospectively) only with the written consent of the Company and the [Requisite Holders]; provided that the Company may in its sole discretion waive compliance with Section 2.12(c) (and the Company’s failure to object promptly in writing after notification of a proposed assignment allegedly in violation of Section 2.12(c) shall be deemed to be a waiver); and provided further that any provision hereof may be waived by any waiving party on such party’s own behalf, without the consent of any other party. 
(B)	Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, 
(I)	this Agreement may not be amended, modified or terminated and the observance of any term hereof may not be waived with respect to any Investor without the written consent of such Investor, unless such amendment, modification, termination, or waiver applies to all Investors in the same fashion [(it being agreed that a waiver of the provisions of Section 4 with respect to a particular transaction shall be deemed to apply to a…

…eding, any claim that it is not subject personally to the jurisdiction of the above-named courts, that its property is exempt or immune from attachment or execution, that the suit, action or proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum, that the venue of the suit, action or proceeding is improper or that this Agreement or the subject matter hereof may not be enforced in or by such court.] 
(B)	Each of the parties to this Agreement consents to personal jurisdiction for any equitable action sought in the U.S. District Court for the District of [_____] or any state court of [state] having subject matter jurisdiction.
(C)	Waiver of Jury Trial: EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, THE OTHER TRANSACTION AGREEMENTS, THE SECURITIES OR THE SUBJECT MATTER HEREOF OR THEREOF. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS, AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS. THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS. EACH PARTY HERETO HEREBY FURTHER WARRANTS AND REPRESENTS THAT SUCH PARTY HAS REVIEWED THIS WAIVER WITH ITS LEGAL COUNSEL, AND THAT SUCH PARTY KNOWINGLY AND VOLUNTARILY WAIVES ITS JURY TRIAL RIG…

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)

Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports boilerplate; functions as tiered self-government of the rights layer.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
The document's own government.
Economic
Government of the rights excludes most of the governed, tiered twice: Requisite Holders hold the general keys, the Major sub-class holds ITS OWN sections' keys (the large amend the large's rights), named investors hold personal vetoes, [the Key Holders hold only a disproportionate-harm shield, bracketed], and new members join without anyone's consent while non-consenters are bound.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: same-fashion rule + Participating-Investor patch are real equal-treatment floors; named-investor veto is honest personal-rights hygiene. Cautionary: tiered self-government; waiver-by-silence; binding non-consenters.
  • Configuration to watch: [(b)(iv)] KH shield in/out - the founder-side bracket to watch; dispute-resolution alternative; fees bracket.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “may be amended, modified, terminated or waived with only (and only with) the written consent of the Company and the holders of [a majority] of the Reg…”
  • “failure to object promptly in writing after notification of a proposed assignment allegedly in violation of Section 2.12(c) shall be deemed to be a wa…”
  • “EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, THE OTHER TRANSACTION…”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 6's richest cell: FOUR consent tiers in one amendment clause (general/Major/named/[KH]) vs the suite's five vocabularies; jury waiver byte-parity check queued (fourth instance); deemed-waiver-by-silence is unique in the suite (drift datum).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Rights assign only to Affiliates/family/[Major-Investor-scale] transferees (+aggregation, attorney-in-fact for small transferees, no Sanctioned assignees); amendment = Company + [Requisite Holders], with: company SOLE-DISCRETION waiver of 2.12(c) incl. DEEMED WAIVER BY SILENCE; same-fashion rule + [Participating-Investor pro-rata patch]; 6.6(b)(ii): Major-Investor sections amendable ONLY by Company + majority of Major-held Registrable Securities; named-investor consent (b)(iii); [(b)(iv) KH disproportionate-adverse-effect consent]; unilateral Schedule A updates; binding on non-consenters; aggregation (6.8); no-consent joinder of new Investors (6.9); entire-agreement incl. Side Letters; [JAMS arbitration]/[courts]; JURY WAIVER (fifth suite instance); [prevailing-party fees]; cumulative remedies.
Notes
Catechism: purports boilerplate; functions as tiered self-government of the rights layer. Forensics: stage 5.

All remedies, whether under this Agreement or by law or otherwise afforded to any party, shall be cumulative and not alternative.

IRA — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Information / KPI stack
Synthesis
Valve complete: 220 waived for all + tiered channel to Majors + wide-audience confidentiality (MRL dependency) + [observer]/[platform]/M&A-drafts - information re-based from ownership to size
MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-02cautionary
Stack
Liquidity / exit / financing stack
Synthesis
Class holds exit timing (demand), exit priority (cutback ladder), financing path (ROFO + 2.10), and both lock-ups' terms
MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-03cautionary
Stack
Governance / operational control stack
Synthesis
Four-tier amendment government; waiver-by-silence; binding non-consenters; covenant deck incl. subsidiary mirror; LP joinder conscription
MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-04cautionary
Stack
Founder labor / equity stack
Synthesis
KH admitted-then-excluded; labor pair (5.2 restraints/lock-lock vs 5.6 fund immunity); SV14-01 durability limb (5.2(b)); assembly at P8
MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-05cautionary
Stack
Named-investor / side-right stack
Synthesis
[Observer]; first-resort; named amendment veto; [named Competitor exception]; Side Letters woven in
MG-NVCA-IRA-SYN-06licit
Stack
Mitigator stack
Synthesis
Corpus's thickest: POS-02 cap; POS-08 crumb; 2.11 parity + [pro-rata waiver]; same-fashion + Participating-Investor patch; deferral + MAC escape; 3.2 carve-outs; sunsets; [KH shield]

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-IRA-A — Wide-class configurationcautionary
Configuration
Major threshold at smallest-VC scale; [KH registration rights + (b)(iv) shield in]; non-compete out; severance cap out; [use-it-or-lose-it] out; observer duty-bound/out
NVCA-IRA-B — Standard-filled roundpotentially extractive
Configuration
High Major threshold (small checks dark post-waiver); KH carve-outs full, shield out; non-competes in; severance cap in; M&A-drafts + platform + waiver-by-silence live. = the v3.15 baseline's reading
NVCA-IRA-C — Shadow-government configurationsevere
Configuration
B + observer + Investor Counsel inside exit deliberations + subsidiary mirror + first-resort; 5.2(b) lock-lock active at suite level (SV14-01 durability limb)

MG-NVCA-VA-02SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Definitions with moral load

Three load-bearing moves: the Qualified Key Holder condition ties founder-side designation power to CONTINUING [full-time] service (stop working, lose the seat's electorate); Requisite Holders sets the class-government threshold (blank); Sale of the Company imports the charter's DLE taxonomy wholesale.

(c)	“Investors” means the persons named on Schedule A hereto, each person who hereafter becomes a party to this Agreement pursuant to Section 7.1(a) and each person to whom the rights of an Investor are assigned pursuant to Section 7.2.

(h)	“Sale of the Company” means either: (a) a transaction or series of related transactions in which a Person, or a group of related Persons, acquires from stockholders of the Company shares representing more than 50% of the outstanding voting power of the Company (a “Stock Sale”); or (b) a transaction that qualifies as a “Deemed Liquidation Event,” as defined in the Restated Certificate.[FN id=7]
(i)	“Sanctioned Party” means any Person: (i) organized under the laws of, ordinarily resident in, or located in a country or territory that is the subject of comprehensive Sanctions (“Restricted Countries”[FN id=8]); (ii) 50% or more owned or controlled by the government of a Restricted Country; or (iii) (A) designated on a sanctioned parties list administered by the United States[, European Union, or United Kingdom], including, without limitation, the U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control’s Spec…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports lexicon; functions as power allocation.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Definitional architecture allocating every later power.
Economic
Three load-bearing moves: the Qualified Key Holder condition ties founder-side designation power to CONTINUING [full-time] service (stop working, lose the seat's electorate); Requisite Holders sets the class-government threshold (blank); Sale of the Company imports the charter's DLE taxonomy wholesale.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: definitional clarity; schedule-based party registers. Cautionary: the QKH service condition is the leaver machinery's voting layer — departure quietly disenfranchises the founder class of its designation power; breadth of 'Shares' conscripts future acquisitions.
  • Configuration to watch: [full-time] bracket scope (consultants?); QKH lapse consequence at configuration (seat falls to 1.2's fallback = combined vote).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “A “Qualified Key Holder” is a Key Holder and (i) if an individual, is providing services to the Company or its subsidiaries as a [full-time] employee”
  • ““Requisite Holders” means the holders of [at least [___]%/a majority] of the then-outstanding shares of Preferred Stock, calculated together as a sing…”
  • “acquires from stockholders of the Company shares representing more than 50% of the outstanding voting power of the Company (a “Stock Sale”)”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 1: 'Requisite Holders' here vs the same term in the charter — same name, independently blanked thresholds (drift candidate #1); 'Sale of the Company' ↔ DLE import (drift candidate #2).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Affiliate (fund-family breadth); Investors/Key Holders (schedule-defined); Qualified Key Holder ([full-time] service condition); Requisite Holders ([at least [___]%/a majority] of preferred, single class as-converted); Sale of the Company (Stock Sale >50% voting power OR the charter's DLE); Sanctioned Party/Sanctions (June-2026 apparatus); Shares (everything votable, however acquired); Side Letters (SPA-defined).
Notes
Catechism: purports lexicon; functions as power allocation. Forensics: stage 5 (QKH).
MG-NVCA-VA-03SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Board-composition covenant (designation seats)

The charter's abstract classes become named persons with conditions: the investor seat survives on share ownership; the founder-side seat survives on EMPLOYMENT (QKH); the CEO seat flips automatically with the office — control follows capital durably, labor conditionally.

Catechism: purports election covenant (societas governance); functions per the seat map.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…e Purchase Agreement.
(n)	“Stockholders” means the Investors, the Key Holders, and each other holder of Common Stock of the Company that becomes party to this Agreement that is not an Investor or Key Holder (which other stockholders shall be set forth on Schedule C to this Agreement). 
(o)	Any reference in this Agreement to “vote” or “voting” or similar language shall include, without limitation, action by written consent of the stockholders.
1.2	Board Composition. Each Stockholder agrees to vote, or cause to be voted, all Shares owned by such Stockholder, or over which such Stockholder has voting control, from time to time and at all times, in whatever manner as shall be necessary to ensure that at each annual or special meeting of stockholders at which an election of directors is held or pursuant to any written consent of the stockholders, subject to Section 5, the following persons shall be elected to the Board: [FN id=11] 
(a)	[As a Preferred Director, [Example 1: one person designated from time to time by [Name of Investor], for so long as [Name of Investor] and its Affiliates (i) continue to beneficially own an aggregate of at least [______] shares of Preferred Stock, which number is subject to appropriate adjustment for any stock splits, stock dividends, combinations, recapitalizations and the like and (ii) are not Sanctioned Parties, which individual as of the date of this Agreement is [_____________];][[Example 2: As a Preferred Director, one person designated from time to time by [Name of Investor], for so long as [Name of Investor] and its A…

… Agreement is [___________];] [Example 2: [name of a Common Director], for so long as such director [remains a [full-time] employee [or consultant (excluding service solely as member of the Board)] of the Company][, except that if such director resigns or is unable to serve, then one individual who is designated by Qualified Key Holders holding a majority of the shares of Common Stock held by Qualified Key Holders for so long as any Qualified Key Holder holds any shares of Common Stock];[FN id=12]]
(c)	[As the [other] Common Director, the Company’s Chief Executive Officer (the “CEO Director”), who as of the date of this Agreement is [_____], provided that if for any reason the CEO Director shall cease to serve as the Chief Executive Officer of the Company, each of the Stockholders shall promptly vote their respective Shares (i) to remove the former Chief Executive Officer from the Board if such person has not resigned from the position of CEO Director; and (ii) to elect the then-current Chief Executive Officer of the Company to serve as the new CEO Director; and]
(d)	[As [each/the] Mutual Director, [one individual who is not otherwise an Affiliate of the Company or of any Investor and who is designated by mutual agreement of the other then-seated members of the Board (the “Mutual Director”), which individual as of the date of this Agreement is [___________]].

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports election covenant (societas governance); functions per the seat map.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Voting covenant hard-wiring board composition to designation rights (DGCL §218 territory).
Economic
The charter's abstract classes become named persons with conditions: the investor seat survives on share ownership; the founder-side seat survives on EMPLOYMENT (QKH); the CEO seat flips automatically with the office — control follows capital durably, labor conditionally.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: designation covenants are the genre's standard board machinery; sanctions conditions (June 2026) are integrity-protective. Cautionary: the ownership-vs-service asymmetry; the [Mutual Director] bracket is the balance seat — its absence tilts the map. Potentially extractive (configuration): investor seat + CEO-flip + QKH lapse = a board that capital cannot lose and labor cannot keep.
  • Configuration to watch: Seat map at configuration (how many of each); Mutual Director in/out; Example 1 vs 2 for the Common seat (named person vs rolling designation).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “in whatever manner as shall be necessary to ensure that at each annual or special meeting of stockholders at which an election of directors is held or…”
  • “provided that if for any reason the CEO Director shall cease to serve as the Chief Executive Officer of the Company, each of the Stockholders shall pr…”

7. Framework / suite notes

The asymmetry is textual: investor designation conditions on OWNERSHIP (survives everything); Key-Holder designation conditions on SERVICE (dies at departure); the CEO seat is an office, not a person. v1.4 founder-voting-loss dimension engaged (configuration).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
All Stockholders must vote at all times to elect: [Preferred Director designated by [Name of Investor] while holding ≥[___] shares/% and not Sanctioned]; [Common Director designated by majority-of-QKH shares, or a named individual while employed]; [CEO Director with automatic remove-and-replace on CEO change]; [Mutual Director: non-Affiliate designated by the other seated directors]. Fallback: inapplicable seats revert to charter voting.
Notes
Catechism: purports election covenant (societas governance); functions per the seat map. Forensics: stage 5 (per-seat).
MG-NVCA-VA-04SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Vacancies, removal, stockholder action

Seats belong to designators, not to elected persons or the electorate: the designator can eject at will and nobody else can — board accountability runs to the appointing constituency alone.
