2. Catechism / title

purports housekeeping; functions as seat ownership.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Removal/vacancy covenants completing the designation architecture.
Economic
Seats belong to designators, not to elected persons or the electorate: the designator can eject at will and nobody else can — board accountability runs to the appointing constituency alone.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: symmetrical for every designated seat. Cautionary: [other than for cause] bracket decides whether ANY seat is removable by the body itself.
  • Configuration to watch: For-cause bracket in/out.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Any vacancies in the Board shall be filled only pursuant to the provisions of Section 1.2.”
  • “shall be promptly removed from office upon the occurrence of any of the following:”

7. Framework / suite notes

Standard designation mechanics; the moral note is structural: a director's tenure depends on the designator's pleasure — stewardship duties point at the company, tenure points at the constituency.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Vacancies filled only per 1.2; removal compelled on designator request (or threshold-holders' request, Mutual-Director majority, eligibility lapse, sanctions); no removal WITHOUT designator consent [other than for cause]; company must call special meetings on a designator's written request.
Notes
Catechism: purports housekeeping; functions as seat ownership.
MG-NVCA-VA-05SEC-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Designator non-liability

Board composition priced at zero accountability for those who compose it; the designee's own fiduciary duties remain, but the power behind the seat is unaccountable.

Catechism: purports comfort clause; functions as accountability thinning.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 12885–14330
…ection 1.2 is no longer in effect pursuant to its terms.
1.5	Stockholder Action. All Stockholders agree to execute any written consents required to perform the obligations of this Section 1, and the Company agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause to be called a special meeting of stockholders for the purpose of electing, removing or replacing directors upon the written request of (i) any Person entitled to designate a director or (ii) the holders of the requisite number of shares of capital stock entitled to approve a director candidate pursuant to Section 1.2.
1.6	No Liability for Election of Designated or Approved Directors. No Stockholder, nor any Affiliate of any Stockholder, shall have any liability as a result of (a) designating a person for election as a director, (b) voting for a person designated for election as a director, or (c) any act or omission by such designated person in such designated person’s capacity as a director of the Company.
2.	Vote to Increase Authorized Common Stock. Each Stockholder agrees to vote or cause to be voted all Shares owned by such Stockholder, or over which such Stockholder has voting control, from time to time and at all times, in whatever manner as shall be necessary to increase the number of authorized shares of Common Stock from time to time to ensure that there will be sufficient shares of Common Stock available for conversion of all of the shares of Preferred S…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports comfort clause; functions as accountability thinning.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Liability disclaimer for designation and voting.
Economic
Board composition priced at zero accountability for those who compose it; the designee's own fiduciary duties remain, but the power behind the seat is unaccountable.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: voting-liability disclaimers are standard; designee duties survive. Cautionary: clause (c) reaches the designee's ACTS — the designator is insulated even from knowing complicity short of separate tort.
  • Configuration to watch: None gating.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “No Stockholder, nor any Affiliate of any Stockholder, shall have any liability as a result of (a) designating a person for election as a director”

7. Framework / suite notes

Pairs with COI-17 (director exculpation) and COI-18 (opportunity waiver): the accountability surface thins at every layer — director, officer, designator.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
No Stockholder or Affiliate has liability for designating, voting for, or the acts/omissions of a designated director.
Notes
Catechism: purports comfort clause; functions as accountability thinning.
MG-NVCA-VA-06SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Vote to increase authorized Common (the COI-03 weld)

Closes Common's dilution gate from the covenant side while the charter's §242(b)(2) override closes it from the statutory side: the increase that dilutes Common is a vote Common has already promised to cast.

No Stockholder, nor any Affiliate of any Stockholder, shall have any liability as a result of (a) designating a person for election as a director, (b) voting for a person designated for election as a director, or (c) any act or omission by such designated person in such designated person’s capacity as a director of the Company.
2.	Vote to Increase Authorized Common Stock. 1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports mechanics covenant; functions as pre-committed dilution capacity.

3. Title breakdown

Valuation controlprimary category
Moral hinge
Valuation terms shape who receives future value and who bears dilution.
Legitimate form
Transparent cap table; broad-based formulas; post-money pool clarity; no hidden founder/common dilution.
Abuse form
Formulaic or side-right mechanisms that transfer upside without comparable risk or contribution.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Pre-commitment covenant on future share authorizations.
Economic
Closes Common's dilution gate from the covenant side while the charter's §242(b)(2) override closes it from the statutory side: the increase that dilutes Common is a vote Common has already promised to cast.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: ensuring conversion capacity honors the priced bargain (the reservation duty's counterpart). Cautionary: the covenant is unbounded ('from time to time and at all times') and reaches increases serving FUTURE financings' conversion needs, not just today's.
  • Configuration to watch: Does the covenant reach increases for future series (as drafted: 'all of the shares of Preferred Stock outstanding at any given time' — yes, ratchets forward)? P8 exhibit.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “in whatever manner as shall be necessary to increase the number of authorized shares of Common Stock from time to time to ensure that there will be su…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Cross-document weld: COI Part A bracket (combined-majority increases) + this covenant = belt and suspenders on dilution capacity — P8 rights-accumulation exhibit A; conversion-reservation duty (COI 4.3.2) is the same promise from the company side (legitimate purpose exists).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Every Stockholder must vote, at all times, to increase authorized Common as needed for full preferred conversion.
Notes
Catechism: purports mechanics covenant; functions as pre-committed dilution capacity. Forensics: stage 5 (with COI-03).
MG-NVCA-VA-07SEC-3cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Drag-along: compelled actions

Exit control: the specified majority moves everyone's shares. The (e) covenant is the sharpest text — the minority promises in advance not to test the sale's fairness in ANY forum: appraisal waived, fiduciary claims waived, class participation waived. Exit power and exit scrutiny concentrated in the same hands.

Catechism: purports coordinated-exit covenant (societas exit discipline); (e) functions as scrutiny renunciation.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · governance_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
drag_waterfall_forced_exit
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports coordinated-exit covenant (societas exit discipline); (e) functions as scrutiny renunciation.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Drag-along covenant with waiver of statutory exit-scrutiny remedies (appraisal; fiduciary actions), post-2020 stockholder-representative machinery.
Economic
Exit control: the specified majority moves everyone's shares. The (e) covenant is the sharpest text — the minority promises in advance not to test the sale's fairness in ANY forum: appraisal waived, fiduciary claims waived, class participation waived. Exit power and exit scrutiny concentrated in the same hands.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: drag with all three approval limbs + 3.2 protections is the market's cleanest exit-coordination form. Cautionary: as-bracketed template. Potentially extractive (configuration): Selling-Investors-only trigger + (e)'s full waiver set — compelled sale with pre-waived scrutiny (the baseline's PE reading, scenario NVCA-VA-B).
  • Configuration to watch: Trigger limbs at configuration (the single largest VA decision); appraisal-waiver enforceability (FN 20's own doubt) — enforceability caveat, not moral comfort; Stockholder Representative fee/escrow scope.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “to refrain from (i) exercising any dissenters’ rights or rights of appraisal under applicable law at any time with respect to such Sale of the Company”
  • “alleging a breach of any fiduciary duty (including, without limitation, aiding and abetting a breach of any fiduciary duty) by the Selling Investors o…”
  • “approve a Sale of the Company (which approval of the Selling Investors [and the holders described in clause (ii)] must be in writing), which approval …”

7. Framework / suite notes

Flag drag_waterfall_forced_exit (weight 1.5) attaches: drag + DLE import + certificate-waterfall allocation = the flagged machinery, present as bracketed template. B2V precedent distinguishes EXIT drag (here) from FINANCING drag (B2V's severe center — absent here). The bracketed trigger limbs ([Common majority], [Board]) are what keep the template out of investor-only drag.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
On approval by [Selling Investors ≥ %][+ QKH-Common majority][+ Board]: every Stockholder must vote yes (and against impediments), sell pro-rata in Stock Sales, execute all transaction documents, avoid voting trusts, refrain from dissenters'/appraisal rights AND from any claim — including fiduciary-breach and aiding-abetting claims — against Selling Investors, directors, or acquirer; accept cash-for-securities substitution; consent to a Stockholder Representative and its escrow/fees.
Notes
Catechism: purports coordinated-exit covenant (societas exit discipline); (e) functions as scrutiny renunciation. Forensics: stage 5 (per (a)-(g)).
MG-NVCA-VA-08SEC-3licitprotective

Drag conditions (the protective set)

The drafted counterweight: whatever the majority compels, the minority cannot be made to warrant more than its own title, bear others' liabilities, forfeit more than it receives, or take a different deal than its class — proceeds-capped several liability is periculum kept proportionate.

Catechism: purports conditions; functions as proportionality restored.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · damnum_emergensStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 20827–22393
…s, and (y) not to assert any claim or commence any suit against the Stockholder Representative or any other Stockholder with respect to any action or inaction taken or failed to be taken by the Stockholder Representative, within the scope of the Stockholder Representative’s authority, in connection with its service as the Stockholder Representative, absent fraud, bad faith, [gross negligence] or willful misconduct.
3.2	Conditions. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, a Stockholder will not be required to comply with Section 3.1 above in connection with any proposed Sale of the Company (the “Proposed Sale”), unless: 
(a)	any representations and warranties to be made by such Stockholder in connection with the Proposed Sale are made severally and not jointly and are limited to representations and warranties related to authority, ownership and the ability to convey title to such Shares, including, but not limited to, representations and warranties that (i) the Stockholder holds all right, title and interest in and to the Shares such Stockholder purports to hold, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances, (ii) the obligations of the Stockholder in connection with the transaction have been duly authorized, if applicable, (iii) the documents to be entered into by the Stockholder have been duly executed by the Stockholder and delivered to the acquirer and are enforceable (subject to customary limitations) against the Stockholder in accordance with their respective terms; and (iv) neither the execution and delivery of docume…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 23740–25217
…representation, warranty or covenant made by any other Person in connection with the Proposed Sale, other than the Company [(except to the extent that funds may be paid out of an escrow established to cover breach of representations, warranties and covenants of the Company as well as breach by any stockholder of any of identical representations, warranties and covenants provided by all stockholders)];
(e)	 liability shall be limited to such Stockholder’s applicable share (determined based on the respective proceeds payable to each Stockholder in connection with such Proposed Sale in accordance with the provisions of the Restated Certificate) of a negotiated aggregate indemnification amount that in no event exceeds the amount of consideration otherwise payable to such Stockholder in connection with such Proposed Sale in such person’s capacity as a stockholder of the Company, except with respect to claims related to fraud by such Stockholder, the liability for which need not be limited as to such Stockholder; [and]
(f)	upon the consummation of the Proposed Sale (i) each holder of each class or series of the capital stock of the Company will receive the same form of consideration for their shares of such class or series as is received by other holders in respect of their shares of such same class or series of stock, (ii) each holder of a series of Preferred Stock will receive the same amount of consideration per share of such series of Preferred Stock as i…

…the Stockholder and delivered to the acquirer and are enforceable (subject to customary limitations) against the Stockholder in accordance with their respective terms; and (iv) neither the execution and delivery of documents to be entered into by the Stockholder in connection with the transaction, nor the performance of the Stockholder’s obligations thereunder, will cause a breach or violation of the terms of any agreement (including the Company’s or such Stockholder’s organizational documents) to which the Stockholder is a party, or any law or judgment, order or decree of any court or governmental agency that applies to the Stockholder;
(b)	such Stockholder is not required to agree (unless such Stockholder is a Company officer, director, or employee) to any restrictive covenant in connection with the Proposed Sale (including, without limitation, any covenant not to compete or covenant not to solicit customers, employees or suppliers of any party to the Proposed Sale) or any release of claims other than a release in customary form of claims arising solely in such Stockholder’s capacity as a stockholder of the Company;

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
licit
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
protective
Positive form
POS-01 — Risk truly shared — Capital stands to lose principal with no insulating stack; profit-share matches exposure.
Materiality M
0.48 (b1.0 operative conditions precedent; s0.6 event-scoped (per Proposed Sale); l0.8 answers the drag's dominant risk (uncapped liability, unequal treatment) directly)
Enforceability test
Operative conditions precedent — drag obligations do not arise unless satisfied; self-executing protection.
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports conditions; functions as proportionality restored.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Damnum emergenssecondary category
Moral hinge
The lender may be restored for actual loss, not enriched because money was lent.
Legitimate form
Actual loss; causal connection; documentation; proportionality; no double-counting; restorative accounting.
Abuse form
Hypothetical, standardized, inflated, or presumed losses converted into universal yield.

4. Functions

Legal
Conditions precedent to drag obligations — the minority-protection schedule.
Economic
The drafted counterweight: whatever the majority compels, the minority cannot be made to warrant more than its own title, bear others' liabilities, forfeit more than it receives, or take a different deal than its class — proceeds-capped several liability is periculum kept proportionate.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: the whole schedule. Cautionary (within the licit row): the (b) carve-out for employees/officers; the [escrow] bracket in (d) reopens capped cross-liability.
  • Configuration to watch: (b) carve-out breadth at configuration — does 'employee' swallow every founder? P8: compare with SPA reps structure.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “any representations and warranties to be made by such Stockholder in connection with the Proposed Sale are made severally and not jointly and are limi…”
  • “of a negotiated aggregate indemnification amount that in no event exceeds the amount of consideration otherwise payable to such Stockholder in connect…”
  • “such Stockholder is not required to agree (unless such Stockholder is a Company officer, director, or employee) to any restrictive covenant in connect…”

7. Framework / suite notes

The suite's clearest protective block on the adversarial side: it exists BECAUSE dragged parties historically bore joint liability and post-closing covenants (calibration memory: b2venture's drag lacked this schedule). The officer/director/employee carve-out in (b) leaves FOUNDERS exposed to non-competes in a drag — the one hole, and it points at labor.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
No drag compliance unless: reps several-not-joint and title/authority-only; no restrictive covenants [unless officer/director/employee] and no releases beyond stockholder capacity; no forced amendment of other relationships; no liability for others' breaches [except capped escrow]; liability capped at such Stockholder's proceeds (fraud excepted); same form and amount of consideration per class, allocated per the certificate waterfall; [equal option if any holder gets a choice of consideration].
Notes
Catechism: purports conditions; functions as proportionality restored. Forensics: stage 5 (condition-by-condition).
MG-NVCA-VA-09SEC-3cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Restrictions on sales of control

Allocation honesty extended to stockholder-level exits (no side-door control sales that skip the waterfall) — and, in the same words, a founder-liquidity lock: no founder can sell control without the preferred coming along at their preference.

Catechism: purports anti-evasion; functions as waterfall lock on private exits.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · surplus_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 27382–28988
…ny single class or series of capital stock, if any holders of any capital stock of the Company are given an option as to the form and amount of consideration to be received as a result of the Proposed Sale, all holders of such capital stock will be given the same option; provided, however, that nothing in this Section 3.2(g) shall entitle any holder to receive any form of consideration that such holder would be ineligible to receive as a result of such holder’s failure to satisfy any condition, requirement or limitation that is generally applicable to the Company’s stockholders.]
3.3	Restrictions on Sales of Control of the Company.[FN id=22] No Stockholder shall be a party to any Stock Sale unless (a) all holders of Preferred Stock are allowed to participate in such transaction(s) and (b) the consideration received pursuant to such transaction is allocated among the parties thereto in the manner specified in the Company’s Restated Certificate in effect immediately prior to the Stock Sale (as if such transaction(s) were a Deemed Liquidation Event), unless the holders of at least the requisite percentage required to waive treatment of the transaction(s) as a Deemed Liquidation Event pursuant to the terms of the Restated Certificate, elect to allocate the consideration differently by written notice given to the Company at least [__] days prior to the effective date of any such transaction or series of related transactions.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports anti-evasion; functions as waterfall lock on private exits.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Transfer restriction conditioning private control sales on participation and waterfall allocation.
Economic
Allocation honesty extended to stockholder-level exits (no side-door control sales that skip the waterfall) — and, in the same words, a founder-liquidity lock: no founder can sell control without the preferred coming along at their preference.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: anti-evasion of the priced waterfall. Cautionary: binds ALL stockholders' exits to preferred participation — founder liquidity conditioned on capital's terms.
  • Configuration to watch: Waiver-notice blank; interaction with ROFR §2 (the dedicated founder-transfer machinery) — P8.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “No Stockholder shall be a party to any Stock Sale unless (a) all holders of Preferred Stock are allowed to participate in such transaction(s) and (b) …”

7. Framework / suite notes

The VA-side twin of COI 2.3.2(a) (waterfall-forcing) — P8 parity check; double-edged like its twin: honesty device and liquidity lock in one text.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
No Stockholder may join a Stock Sale unless all preferred may participate and consideration follows the certificate waterfall (as if a DLE), unless the DLE-waiver threshold elects otherwise on [__] days' notice.
Notes
Catechism: purports anti-evasion; functions as waterfall lock on private exits.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports compliance + mechanics; the waiver functions as scrutiny closure, completing 3.1(e).

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Sanctions-driven disenfranchisement (June-2026 surface); waiver of consent-notice and appraisal-notice statutes.
Economic
The notice waiver completes 3.1(e)'s scrutiny closure: no appraisal, no fiduciary claims, and now no statutory notice that the sale is even happening — the dragged minority may learn of the sale after it closes.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: sanctions compliance is integrity machinery. Cautionary: notice waiver = information closure on the dragged; threshold-disregard shifts class government without anyone acting.
  • Configuration to watch: EU/UK sanctions brackets in/out (a European deployment would want them in).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Each Stockholder hereby waives the right to receive any notices that would otherwise be required to be given to it under Sections 228 and/or 262 of th…”
  • “The Shares held by such Stockholders shall be disregarded for the purpose of calculating any voting threshold set forth in this Agreement.”

7. Framework / suite notes

FN 23 notes the §228 waiver's post-2020 statutory basis (DGCL amendment permitting advance waiver) — new-surface item; threshold-disregard mechanics can silently change who the Requisite Holders ARE (P8 note).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Sanctioned Stockholders lose drag rights/benefits and their Shares are disregarded in ALL voting thresholds; every Stockholder waives DGCL §§228/262 notices in drag sales.
Notes
Catechism: purports compliance + mechanics; the waiver functions as scrutiny closure, completing 3.1(e).
MG-NVCA-VA-11SEC-4cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Company covenants, irrevocable proxy, enforcement

The covenant enforces itself: a Stockholder who hesitates five days — or votes their conscience against the Agreement — is voted BY the Company's own officers and the preferred class's designee. Dissent is not breached; it is overwritten.

Catechism: purports enforcement mechanics; functions as vote substitution.Geometry: control_without_stewardship · governance_asymmetryStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…le of the Company ]pursuant to and in accordance with the terms and provisions of this Agreement or to take any action reasonably necessary to effect this Agreement. The power of attorney granted hereunder shall authorize each Proxyholder to execute and deliver any documentation required by this Agreement on behalf of any party failing to do so within five business days after request by the Company. Each of the proxy and power of attorney granted pursuant to this Section 4.2 is given in consideration of the agreements and covenants of the Company and the parties in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement and, as such, each is coupled with an interest and shall be irrevocable unless and until this Agreement terminates or expires pursuant to Section 6 hereof. Each party hereto hereby revokes any and all previous proxies or powers of attorney with respect to the Shares and shall not hereafter, unless and until this Agreement terminates or expires pursuant to Section 6 hereof, purport to grant any other proxy or power of attorney with respect to any of the Shares, deposit any of the Shares into a voting trust or enter into any agreement (other than this Agreement), arrangement or understanding with any person, directly or indirectly, to vote, grant any proxy or give instructions with respect to the voting of any of the Shares, in each case, with respect to any of the matters set forth herein.[FN i…

… voting trust or enter into any agreement (other than this Agreement), arrangement or understanding with any person, directly or indirectly, to vote, grant any proxy or give instructions with respect to the voting of any of the Shares, in each case, with respect to any of the matters set forth herein.[FN id=25]
4.3	Specific Enforcement. Each party acknowledges and agrees that each party hereto will be irreparably damaged in the event any of the provisions of this Agreement are not performed by the parties in accordance with their specific terms or are otherwise breached. Accordingly, it is agreed that each of the Company and the Stockholders shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to specific enforcement of this Agreement and its terms and provisions in any action instituted in any court of the United States or any state having subject matter jurisdiction; provided that no party that is regulated as a bank holding company under the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956, as amended, shall have the right to enforce against any Stockholder any provisions of this Agreement that (a) requires a Stockholder to vote for or against any matter or (b) restricts or conditions the ability of a Stockholder to transfer its Shares. Each party to this Agreement agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate in seeking and agreeing to an expedited schedule in any litigation seeking an injunction or…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports enforcement mechanics; functions as vote substitution.

3. Title breakdown

Control without stewardshipprimary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Irrevocable proxy coupled with an interest (DGCL §212(e)); self-help enforcement of the voting covenants.
Economic
The covenant enforces itself: a Stockholder who hesitates five days — or votes their conscience against the Agreement — is voted BY the Company's own officers and the preferred class's designee. Dissent is not breached; it is overwritten.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: proxies enforcing agreed covenants are settled Delaware machinery; BHC carve-out honest. Cautionary: the trigger includes ATTEMPTED inconsistent votes (not just defaults); Proxyholder identity asymmetric. Potentially extractive (configuration): with the drag bracket in 4.2, the proxy executes the compelled sale documents too — the whole exit can complete over a dissenting signature.
  • Configuration to watch: Drag-execution brackets in 4.2 in/out; who is the Requisite-Holders designee in practice.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “each is coupled with an interest and shall be irrevocable unless and until this Agreement terminates or expires pursuant to Section 6 hereof”
  • “fails to vote [and take such other actions] within five business days after request by the Company”
  • “shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to specific enforcement of this Agreement and its terms and provisions”

7. Framework / suite notes

Severe signal root: control without stewardship — the proxy detaches the vote from the person who bears the share's risk, mechanically. Standard in the genre precisely because it is effective; the Proxyholder identity (company officers + preferred designee) means the enforcement agent is one side's.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Company must call meetings on demand (incl. [for drag approval]); every party grants the President/Board-Chair AND a Requisite-Holders designee an irrevocable proxy + power of attorney — exercisable on 5-business-day default, sanctions bar, or any inconsistent vote — to cast their Shares per the Agreement and execute documents; all prior proxies revoked, no new ones permitted; specific enforcement with expedited-schedule cooperation (BHC-regulated parties carved out); remedies cumulative.
Notes
Catechism: purports enforcement mechanics; functions as vote substitution. Forensics: stage 5.

…the voting power, which includes the power to vote or to direct the voting of, such security; and/or (2) the investment power, which includes the power to dispose, or to direct the disposition of, such security. 
(b)	The Company hereby represents and warrants to the Investors that no Disqualification Event is applicable to the Company or, to the Company’s knowledge, any Company Covered Person, except for a Disqualification Event as to which Rule 506(d)(2)(ii)-(iv) or (d)(3) is applicable. 
5.3	Covenants. Each Person with the right to designate or participate in the designation of a director pursuant to this Agreement covenants and agrees (i) not to designate or participate in the designation of any director designee who, to such Person’s knowledge, is a Disqualified Designee, (ii) to exercise reasonable care to determine whether any director designee designated by such person is a Disqualified Designee, (iii) that in the event such Person becomes aware that any individual previously designated by any such Person is or has become a Disqualified Designee, such Person shall as promptly as practicable take such actions as are necessary to remove such Disqualified Designee from the Board and designate a replacement designee who is not a Disqualified Designee, and (iv) to notify the Company promptly in writing in the event a Disqualification Event becomes applicable to such Person or any of its Rule 506(d) Related Par…

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.

7. Framework / suite notes

Symmetric duties; the reasonable-care standard and prompt-removal covenant have real content; protects all stockholders' ability to raise under 506.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Rule 506(d) definitions; designators represent no Disqualification Events, covenant not to designate Disqualified Designees, to remove promptly on discovery, and to notify; Company reciprocates for Company Covered Persons.
Notes

MG-NVCA-VA-13SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Term

The voting regime dies at the public exit — like the charter's preference sunset, the control architecture self-dissolves when liquidity and pricing become public.

Catechism: purports term; functions as the regime's sunset.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · periculum_sortisStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

6.	Term. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date hereof and shall continue in effect until and shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of (a) the consummation of the Company’s first underwritten public offering of its Common Stock[FN id=29] (other than a registration statement relating either to the sale of securities to employees of the Company pursuant to its stock option, stock purchase or similar plan or an SEC Rule 145 transaction)[, or Qualified Direct Listing (as defined in the Restated Certificate)]; (b) the consummation of a Sale of the Company and, if applicable, distribution of proceeds to or escrow for the benefit of the Stockholders in accordance with the Restated Certificate[, provided that the provisions of Section 3 hereof will continue after the closing of any Sale of the Company to the extent necessary to enforce the provisions of Section 3 with resp…

High

2. Catechism / title

purports term; functions as the regime's sunset.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Periculum sortissecondary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.

4. Functions

Legal
Termination provisions.
Economic
The voting regime dies at the public exit — like the charter's preference sunset, the control architecture self-dissolves when liquidity and pricing become public.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: self-dissolution at public exit. Cautionary: without the (d) bracket, no time limit — a company that never exits carries the regime forever.
  • Configuration to watch: Sunset bracket in/out.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of (a) the consummation of the Company’s first underwritten public offering of its Common Stock”

7. Framework / suite notes

Mirrors COI-12; the [sunset date] bracket is the only time-bound limit on the covenant absent an exit — rarely used, and its absence means the regime is perpetual until exit.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Terminates at the earliest of: IPO consummation [or QDL]; Sale consummation + distribution (drag provisions surviving for enforcement); 7.8 termination; [or a sunset date].
Notes
Catechism: purports term; functions as the regime's sunset.
MG-NVCA-VA-14SEC-7cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Additional parties, transfers, successors

The dragnet: ownership of the company is inseparable from membership in the voting regime — shares cannot move without carrying the covenants, so exit from the regime by transfer is impossible by construction.

Catechism: purports joinder mechanics; functions as regime perpetuation.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Named-investor / side-right stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 38712–40187
…r escrow for the benefit of the Stockholders in accordance with the Restated Certificate[, provided that the provisions of Section 3 hereof will continue after the closing of any Sale of the Company to the extent necessary to enforce the provisions of Section 3 with respect to such Sale of the Company]; [and] (c) termination of this Agreement in accordance with Section 7.8 below[; and (d) _____ __, 20__]. 
7.	Miscellaneous.
7.1	Additional Parties.
(a)	Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein, if the Company issues additional shares of Preferred Stock after the date hereof, the Person acquiring such shares of Preferred Stock, as a condition to the issuance of such shares by the Company, shall become a party to this Agreement by executing and delivering a counterpart signature page to this Agreement agreeing to be bound by and subject to the terms of this Agreement as an Investor and Stockholder hereunder. Each such Person shall thereafter be deemed an Investor and Stockholder for all purposes under this Agreement. The Company shall amend Schedule A to include such purchaser as an Investor and Stockholder, but failure to update Schedule A shall not negate such Investor’s rights and obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
(b)	In the event that after the date of this Agreement, the Company enters into an agreement with any Person to issue shares of capital stock [or options or warrants to purchase shares of capital stock] to such Person (…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)VA · chars 42000–43539
…or assignee shall agree in writing to be subject to each of the terms of this Agreement by executing and delivering a counterpart signature page in this Agreement, agreeing to be bound by and subject to the terms of this Agreement in the same capacity as the transferor. Upon the execution and delivery of a counterpart signature page to this Agreement by any transferee, such transferee shall be deemed to be a party hereto as if such transferee were the transferor and such transferee’s signature appeared on the signature pages of this Agreement and shall be deemed to be an Investor and Stockholder, or Key Holder and Stockholder, as applicable. The Company shall not permit the transfer of the Shares subject to this Agreement on its books or issue a new certificate representing any such Shares unless and until such transferee shall have complied with the terms of this Section 7.2. Each certificate instrument, or book entry representing the Shares subject to this Agreement if issued on or after the date of this Agreement shall be notated by the Company with the legend set forth in Section 7.12. The Company shall amend the applicable Schedules to include such transferee as an Investor, Key Holder, and/or Stockholder, as applicable, but the Company’s failure to update the Schedules to this Agreement shall not negate such Stockholder’s rights and obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
7.3	Successors and Assigns. The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the respective su…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports joinder mechanics; functions as regime perpetuation.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Joinder architecture and transfer conditioning.
Economic
The dragnet: ownership of the company is inseparable from membership in the voting regime — shares cannot move without carrying the covenants, so exit from the regime by transfer is impossible by construction.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: covenant continuity is what makes the regime real. Cautionary: the Company's [sole discretion] over Key-Holder status (7.1(b)) quietly controls who shares founder-side rights.
  • Configuration to watch: 1% threshold bracket; Key-Holder admission standard.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “as a condition to the issuance of such shares by the Company, shall become a party to this Agreement by executing and delivering a counterpart signatu…”
  • “The Company shall not permit the transfer of the Shares subject to this Agreement on its books or issue a new certificate representing any such Shares…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Standard perpetuation machinery; with 7.13 (recapitalization dragnet) and the 7.12 legend, the regime is self-propagating across every corporate event.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Every new preferred purchaser must join as Investor; every 1%+ equity recipient must join as Stockholder [/Key Holder at Company discretion]; transfers recognized ONLY on joinder (books blocked otherwise); designation rights non-transferable; no third-party beneficiaries.
Notes
Catechism: purports joinder mechanics; functions as regime perpetuation.
MG-NVCA-VA-15SEC-7cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Amendment, modification, waiver (the gradation cell)

Three keys — but of unequal metal: the investor key (Requisite Holders) never falls away; the founder key carries a [fall-away] bracket AND the (b)(v) carve-out whose 'not directly applicable' judgment invites narrow readings; the named-investor brackets entrench specific seats absolutely.

Catechism: purports amendment rules; functions as the constitution of the constitution.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High

…hall be deemed to have been revoked until a new or corrected electronic mail address has been provided, and such attempted electronic notice shall be ineffective and deemed to not have been given. Each Stockholder agrees to promptly notify the Company of any change in its electronic mail address, and that failure to do so shall not affect the foregoing.
7.8	Consent Required to Amend, Modify, Terminate or Waive. 
(a)	This Agreement may be amended, modified or terminated (other than pursuant to Section 6) and the observance of any term hereof may be waived (either generally or in a particular instance and either retroactively or prospectively) only by a written instrument executed by (i) the Company; (ii) the [Qualified] Key Holders holding [specify percentage]% of the Shares then held by the [Qualified] Key Holders [provided that such consent shall not be required if the [Qualified] Key Holders do not then own Shares representing at least [specify percentage]% of the outstanding capital stock of the Company]; and (iii) the Requisite Holders; provided that Shares held by a Sanctioned Party shall be disregarded for the purpose of calculating the percentages set forth in this section (including determination of Requisite Holders). 
(b)	Notwithstanding the foregoing: 
(i)	this Agreement may not be amended, modified or terminated and the observance of any term of this Agreement may not be waived with respect to any Investor or Key Holder without the written…

(vii)	any provision hereof may be waived by the waiving party on such party’s own behalf, without the consent of any other party.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports amendment rules; functions as the constitution of the constitution.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Amendment architecture with per-constituency consents and carve-outs.
Economic
Three keys — but of unequal metal: the investor key (Requisite Holders) never falls away; the founder key carries a [fall-away] bracket AND the (b)(v) carve-out whose 'not directly applicable' judgment invites narrow readings; the named-investor brackets entrench specific seats absolutely.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: multi-key design with same-fashion protection. Cautionary: the fall-away bracket + (b)(v) carve-out make the founder key conditional; Company-only schedule amendment relocates the party registers.
  • Configuration to watch: Fall-away threshold; who adjudicates 'not directly applicable'; named entrenchments at configuration.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “only by a written instrument executed by (i) the Company; (ii) the [Qualified] Key Holders holding [specify percentage]% of the Shares then held by th…”
  • “the consent of the Key Holders shall not be required for any amendment, modification, termination or waiver if such amendment, modification, terminati…”
  • “unless such amendment, modification, termination or waiver applies to all Investors or Key Holders, as the case may be, in the same fashion”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 6 (amendment-consent gradation), NVCA-side exhibit: which matters need which key, and whose key can be dropped — mirror of the NEC-side 42-visibility/44-approval question. The same-fashion rule (b)(i) is the genuine minority protection.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Amendment/termination/waiver requires Company + [Qualified] Key Holders holding [%] of KH Shares [consent falling away if KH holdings drop below [%] of capital] + Requisite Holders; same-fashion protection per group; [named-investor entrenchments on 1.2(a)]; KH consent NOT required where the change is 'not directly applicable' to KH rights or affects them no differently (b)(v); schedules amendable by Company alone; waivers self-only permitted; sanctions-held Shares disregarded.
Notes
Catechism: purports amendment rules; functions as the constitution of the constitution. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-VA-16SEC-7cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Boilerplate with load

Enforcement economics: arbitration-or-court bracket plus jury waiver plus [fee-shifting] set the cost of resisting the regime; the Side-Letters hook (7.11) means the entire agreement includes terms most parties never see; aggregation (7.18) lets fund families reach thresholds jointly.

…ding, any claim that it is not subject personally to the jurisdiction of the above-named courts, that its property is exempt or immune from attachment or execution, that the suit, action or proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum, that the venue of the suit, action or proceeding is improper or that this Agreement or the subject matter hereof may not be enforced in or by such court.] 
(b)	Each of the parties to this Agreement consents to personal jurisdiction for any equitable action sought in the U.S. District Court for the District of [_____] or any state court of [state] having subject matter jurisdiction. 
(c)	Waiver of Jury Trial: EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, THE OTHER TRANSACTION AGREEMENTS, THE SECURITIES OR THE SUBJECT MATTER HEREOF OR THEREOF. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS, AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS. THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS. EACH PARTY HERETO HEREBY FURTHER WARRANTS AND REPRESENTS THAT SUCH PARTY HAS REVIEWED THIS WAIVER WITH…

… PARTIES HERETO AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS. EACH PARTY HERETO HEREBY FURTHER WARRANTS AND REPRESENTS THAT SUCH PARTY HAS REVIEWED THIS WAIVER WITH ITS LEGAL COUNSEL, AND THAT SUCH PARTY KNOWINGLY AND VOLUNTARILY WAIVES ITS JURY TRIAL RIGHTS FOLLOWING CONSULTATION WITH LEGAL COUNSEL.[FN id=38]
7.17	Costs of Enforcement. [Each party will bear its own costs in respect of any disputes arising under this Agreement.] [The prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorney’s fees, costs, and necessary disbursements in addition to any other relief to which such party may be entitled.]
7.18	Aggregation of Stock. All Shares held or acquired by a Stockholder and/or its Affiliates shall be aggregated together for the purpose of determining the availability of any rights under this Agreement, and such Affiliates may apportion such rights as among themselves in any manner they deem appropriate.

2. Catechism / title

purports boilerplate; margins all lean toward the regime's enforcers.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Miscellaneous provisions with distributional margins.
Economic
Enforcement economics: arbitration-or-court bracket plus jury waiver plus [fee-shifting] set the cost of resisting the regime; the Side-Letters hook (7.11) means the entire agreement includes terms most parties never see; aggregation (7.18) lets fund families reach thresholds jointly.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: standard forms. Cautionary: jury waiver + [fees] price out small-holder resistance; Side-Letter incorporation is an information asymmetry between signers and the rest.
  • Configuration to watch: Arbitration vs court alternative; fee bracket; are Side Letters disclosed to all VA parties at configuration?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT”
  • “All Shares held or acquired by a Stockholder and/or its Affiliates shall be aggregated together for the purpose of determining the availability of any…”
  • “The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision hereof shall in no way affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.”

7. Framework / suite notes

Severability polarity: fail-open (one line), matching the COI — suite signature confirmed on document two. Side-Letters hook: the shadow layer crosses into the SPA's defined term (P8 check 3/5). Aggregation vs COI 5A aggregation: two definitions of the same concept (P8 check 1).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Delaware law; counterparts/e-signature; one-line fail-open severability; entire-agreement incorporating Side Letters; share legend + recapitalization dragnet; manner-of-voting latitude; further assurances; [JAMS arbitration with limited discovery]/[court jurisdiction] + all-caps jury-trial waiver + [prevailing-party fees]; affiliate aggregation for all rights thresholds.
Notes
Catechism: purports boilerplate; margins all lean toward the regime's enforcers.

VA — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Governance / operational control stack
Synthesis
Designated-board government: definitions->seats->removal->proxy->amendment keys
MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-02cautionary
Stack
Liquidity / exit / financing stack
Synthesis
Exit machine: drag + waterfall lock + DLE import + term; scrutiny-closure stack (3.1(e)+3.5+7.16)
MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-03cautionary
Stack
Founder labor / equity stack
Synthesis
Labor hooks: QKH service condition, board-only exclusion, 08.b carve-out, CEO flip; suite assembly at P8
MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-04cautionary
Stack
Named-investor / side-right stack
Synthesis
Seat entrenchments, Side-Letters hook, affiliate aggregation, KH admission discretion
MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-05cautionary
Stack
Information / KPI stack
Synthesis
Notice waiver closes info to the dragged; 506(d) opens info about designators - asymmetric flow
MG-NVCA-VA-SYN-06licit
Stack
Mitigator stack
Synthesis
3.2 schedule, same-fashion rule, sanctions integrity, sunset, BHC carve-out, charter fallback

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-VA-A — Balanced four-corner board, protected dragcautionary
Configuration
Three-limb trigger; full 3.2; (e)(ii) struck; Mutual Director; for-cause removal; no fall-away; jury waiver out
NVCA-VA-B — Standard-filled investor roundpotentially extractive
Configuration
Selling-Investors-only trigger; full scrutiny-closure; proxy w/ drag execution; strict QKH; fall-away in. = the v3.15 baseline's reading
NVCA-VA-C — Exit-forcing configurationsevere
Configuration
B + low trigger %, named entrenchments, drag non-competes via 08.b, notice waiver exercised

NVCA Model ROFR & Co-Sale Agreement (Updated April 2026)

Risk: cautionary (configuration-pending; PE at standard fill)Sim (disclosed): 1.0275Mode-2 D: 0.000014 scored rows

The suite's purest one-way instrument: the founder-exit gauntlet, the co-sale waterfall import, and the frictionless at-cost buyback door (SV14-01 buyback limb). First corpus D-zero.

MG-NVCA-ROFR-01HEAD, RECITALSlicitnot applicable (exemplarity)

The inducement recital states the bargain plainly: founder transfer freedom is part of the price of the round.

  • Configuration to watch: None.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “WHEREAS, each Key Holder is the beneficial owner of shares of Capital Stock, or of options to purchase Capital Stock”

7. Framework / suite notes

Recital-with-operative-twins (5th use).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Title/parties; recitals: Key Holders own Capital Stock; [Prior Agreement restatement]; Key Holders and Company 'desire to induce' the Investors to purchase by granting these rights.
Notes
Grouped meta-at-licit.

Definitions: the one-way architecture

The asymmetry is definitional before it is operative: restrictions attach to Transfer Stock — Key Holder COMMON only — while investor preferred and its conversion shares are expressly outside the machinery. Investors take rights over founder exits; founder rights over investor exits do not exist in this document.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports lexicon; functions as one-way scoping.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Definitional architecture.
Economic
The asymmetry is definitional before it is operative: restrictions attach to Transfer Stock — Key Holder COMMON only — while investor preferred and its conversion shares are expressly outside the machinery. Investors take rights over founder exits; founder rights over investor exits do not exist in this document.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: clear definitions. Cautionary: the one-way scope; the Competitor definition's [named exception] bracket lets a specific investor's portfolio escape the prohibition that binds founders' buyers.
  • Configuration to watch: Competitor [description] breadth at configuration; named exception; fall-away number.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “but does not include any shares of Preferred Stock or of Common Stock that are issued or issuable upon conversion of Preferred Stock”
  • ““Qualified Key Holder” means a Key Holder and (i) if an individual, is providing services to the Company or its subsidiaries as a [full-time] employee”
  • “For purposes of the number of shares of Capital Stock held by an Investor or Key Holder (or any other calculation based thereon), all shares of Prefer…”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 1: QKH wording variance vs VA ('means' vs 'is' — substance identical, drift documented); Transfer-Stock exclusion IS the document's thesis; Investor fall-away threshold parallels VA/IRA thresholds (drift candidates).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Capital Stock (as-converted, everything); Transfer Stock = Key Holder shares EXCLUDING preferred and conversion shares; QKH (same service condition as the VA, 'means' vs 'is' wording variance); Investor fall-away threshold [FN 6]; Competitor ([description of business] + 20% fund carve-out + [named exception]); Change of Control (50% voting power); DPA/Foreign Person apparatus; Side Letters hook.
Notes
Catechism: purports lexicon; functions as one-way scoping. Forensics: stage 5.

Right of First Refusal machinery (2.1)

Founder liquidity runs a 45-to-75-day gauntlet in which the buyer's identity and price are disclosed to the company and every investor before any sale — the chilling effect on prospective buyers is the mechanism, not a side effect; the board values non-cash consideration.

(a)	Grant. Subject to the terms of Section 2.5 below, each Key Holder[FN id=14] hereby unconditionally and irrevocably grants to the Company a Right of First Refusal to purchase all or any portion of Transfer Stock that such Key Holder may propose to include in a Proposed Key Holder Transfer, at the same price and on the same terms and conditions as those offered to the Prospective Transferee.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
rofr_transfer_restriction
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports matched-terms option (emptio venditio pre-emption); functions as a liquidity gauntlet.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
First-refusal architecture over founder transfers.
Economic
Founder liquidity runs a 45-to-75-day gauntlet in which the buyer's identity and price are disclosed to the company and every investor before any sale — the chilling effect on prospective buyers is the mechanism, not a side effect; the board values non-cash consideration.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: company-first refusal at matched terms is the genre's standard anti-stranger machinery. Cautionary: the timeline + disclosure gauntlet; board-only valuation. Potentially extractive (configuration): forfeiture bracket OUT + slow-walked notices = de facto transfer prohibition.
  • Configuration to watch: Timeline blanks; forfeiture bracket in/out; Bylaws-conflict bracket (which instrument controls).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “hereby unconditionally and irrevocably grants to the Company a Right of First Refusal to purchase all or any portion of Transfer Stock”
  • “must deliver a Proposed Transfer Notice to the Company and each Investor not later than [45] days prior to the consummation”
  • “the selling Key Holder shall be free to sell all, but not less than all, of the Transfer Stock to the Prospective Transferee on terms and conditions s…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Flag rofr_transfer_restriction (1.05) attaches. The [forfeiture] bracket is the drafted mitigator (without it, partial exercise can strand the founder mid-sale); note its all-but-not-less-than-all condition cuts back.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Unconditional, irrevocable ROFR grant to the Company at the transferee's price/terms; 45-day pre-notice with full terms and buyer identity; Company 15 days, Investors' Secondary Refusal 10 more, undersubscription 10 more, closing by [45] days; [all-or-forfeit rule: partial exercise forfeits, but sale must be all-and-no-better within 45 days or the machinery resets]; board-valued non-cash consideration with cash substitution.
Notes
Catechism: purports matched-terms option (emptio venditio pre-emption); functions as a liquidity gauntlet. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-04SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Right of Co-Sale (2.2)

The founder's exit becomes a shared exit: investors ride pro rata, a control-shifting sale pays the PREFERENCE out of the founder's private transaction, and 2.2(e) converts a buyer's refusal into the founder's personal purchase obligation — the seller finances the riders or does not sell.

Catechism: purports shared exit; 2.2(d)(ii)+(e) function as preference enforcement against private sales.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · surplus_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High

(ii)	In the event that the Proposed Key Holder Transfer constitutes a Change of Control, the terms of the Purchase and Sale Agreement shall provide that the aggregate consideration from such transfer shall be allocated to the Participating Investors and the selling Key Holder in accordance with Section 2 of Article Fourth, Part B of the Restated Certificate as if (A) such transfer were a Deemed Liquidation Event, and (B) the capital stock sold in accordance with the Purchase and Sale Agreement were the only capital stock outstanding.[FN id=17]

…ng Key Holder in accordance with Section 2 of Article Fourth, Part B of the Restated Certificate as if (A) such transfer were a Deemed Liquidation Event, and (B) the capital stock sold in accordance with the Purchase and Sale Agreement were the only capital stock outstanding.[FN id=17] 
(e)	Purchase by Selling Key Holder; Deliveries. Notwithstanding Section 2.2(c) above, if any Prospective Transferee(s) refuse(s) to purchase securities subject to the Right of Co-Sale from any Participating Investor or Investors or upon the failure to negotiate in good faith a Purchase and Sale Agreement reasonably satisfactory to the Participating Investors, no Key Holder may sell any Transfer Stock to such Prospective Transferee(s) unless and until, simultaneously with such sale, such Key Holder purchases all securities subject to the Right of Co-Sale from such Participating Investor or Investors on the same terms and conditions (including the proposed purchase price) as set forth in the Proposed Transfer Notice and as provided in Section 2.2(d)(i); provided, however, if such sale constitutes a Change of Control, the portion of the aggregate consideration paid by the selling Key Holder to such Participating Investor or Investors shall be made in accordance with Section 2.2(d)(ii). In connection with such purchase by the selling Key Holder, such Participating Investor or Investors shall deliver to the selling Key Holder any stock certificate or certificates, properly endorsed for transfer, representing the capital stock being purchased by the selling Key Holder (or r…

…greed to purchase pursuant to the Secondary Refusal Right)] and the denominator of which is the total number of shares of Capital Stock owned, in the aggregate, by all Participating Investors immediately prior to the consummation of the Proposed Key Holder Transfer [(including any shares that all Participating Investors have collectively agreed to purchase pursuant to the Secondary Refusal Right)], plus the number of shares of Transfer Stock held by the [selling Key Holder][Key Holders]. The Participating Investors and the selling Key Holder agree that the terms and conditions of any Proposed Key Holder Transfer in accordance with this Section 2.2 will be memorialized in, and governed by, a written purchase and sale agreement with the Prospective Transferee (the “Purchase and Sale Agreement”) with customary terms and provisions for such a transaction, and the Participating Investors and the selling Key Holder further covenant and agree to enter into such Purchase and Sale Agreement as a condition precedent to any sale or other transfer in accordance with this Section 2.2.
