1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
tagalong_cosale_structure
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports shared exit; 2.2(d)(ii)+(e) function as preference enforcement against private sales.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Surplus asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Surplus should correspond to real contribution, loss, risk, labor, or stewardship.
Legitimate form
Waterfall transparency; caps; pro rata sharing; loss-sharing; no hidden participation or double counting.
Abuse form
Surplus capture disconnected from contribution, vulnerability, or mission responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Co-sale (tag-along) rights over founder transfers.
Economic
The founder's exit becomes a shared exit: investors ride pro rata, a control-shifting sale pays the PREFERENCE out of the founder's private transaction, and 2.2(e) converts a buyer's refusal into the founder's personal purchase obligation — the seller finances the riders or does not sell.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: co-sale as anti-abandonment (investors not left behind in a founder cash-out) has a real protective rationale. Cautionary: the correspondingly-reduced bracket and waterfall import. Potentially extractive (configuration): 2.2(e) — the personal-purchase obligation is a poena conventionalis-shaped deterrent priced in the founder's own balance sheet.
  • Configuration to watch: Reduction bracket in/out; 2.2(e) scope at configuration; waterfall-import awareness (founders rarely price it).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the aggregate consideration from such transfer shall be allocated to the Participating Investors and the selling Key Holder in accordance with Section…”
  • “no Key Holder may sell any Transfer Stock to such Prospective Transferee(s) unless and until, simultaneously with such sale, such Key Holder purchases…”
  • “the number of shares of Transfer Stock that the selling Key Holder may sell in the Proposed Key Holder Transfer shall be correspondingly reduced”

7. Framework / suite notes

Flag tagalong_cosale_structure (1.0). 2.2(d)(ii) imports the waterfall into PRIVATE founder sales — the preference reaches transactions the company is not even party to; P8: third waterfall-forcing site (COI 2.3.2(a), VA 3.3, here).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Unsold Transfer Stock triggers investor co-sale: pro-rata participation [reducing the founder's own sellable shares correspondingly]; Purchase-and-Sale-Agreement condition; Change-of-Control sales re-allocate ALL consideration per the certificate waterfall as if a DLE (2.2(d)(ii)); if the buyer refuses investor shares — or a satisfactory PSA fails — the founder must personally buy the investors' co-sale shares before selling any of their own (2.2(e)).
Notes
Catechism: purports shared exit; 2.2(d)(ii)+(e) function as preference enforcement against private sales. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-05SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Effect of failure to comply (2.3)

Self-executing enforcement: the books move without the seller's signature; the co-sale put converts violation into the founder's personal repurchase duty plus the investors' legal fees — remedies that run one direction.

Catechism: purports remedies; functions as self-executing enforcement.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Governance / operational control stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 25514–26942
…articipation in such sale as provided in this Section 2.2(e). 
(f)	Additional Compliance. If any Proposed Key Holder Transfer is not consummated within 60 days after receipt of the Proposed Transfer Notice by the Company, the Key Holders proposing the Proposed Key Holder Transfer may not sell any Transfer Stock unless they first comply in full with each provision of this Section 2. The exercise or election not to exercise any right by any Investor hereunder shall not adversely affect its right to participate in any other sales of Transfer Stock subject to this Section 2.2.
2.3	Effect of Failure to Comply.
(a)	Transfer Void; Equitable Relief. Any Proposed Key Holder Transfer not made in compliance with the requirements of this Agreement shall be null and void ab initio, shall not be recorded on the books of the Company or its transfer agent and shall not be recognized by the Company. Each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that any breach of this Agreement would result in substantial harm to the other parties hereto for which monetary damages alone could not adequately compensate. Therefore, the parties hereto unconditionally and irrevocably agree that any non-breaching party hereto shall be entitled to seek protective orders, injunctive relief and other remedies available at law or in equity (including, without limitation, seeking specific performance or the rescission of purchases, sales and other tran…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 26710–28345
…hereto shall be entitled to seek protective orders, injunctive relief and other remedies available at law or in equity (including, without limitation, seeking specific performance or the rescission of purchases, sales and other transfers of Transfer Stock not made in strict compliance with this Agreement).
(b)	Violation of First Refusal Right. If any Key Holder becomes obligated to sell any Transfer Stock to the Company or any Investor under this Agreement and fails to deliver such Transfer Stock in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, the Company and/or such Investor may, at its option, in addition to all other remedies it may have, send to such Key Holder the purchase price for such Transfer Stock as is herein specified and transfer to the name of the Company or such Investor (or request that the Company effect such transfer in the name of an Investor) on the Company’s books any certificates, instruments, or book entry representing the Transfer Stock to be sold.
(c)	Violation of Co-Sale Right. If any Key Holder purports to sell any Transfer Stock in contravention of the Right of Co-Sale (a “Prohibited Transfer”), each Participating Investor who desires to exercise its Right of Co-Sale under Section 2.2 may, in addition to such remedies as may be available by law, in equity or hereunder, require such Key Holder to purchase from such Participating Investor the type and number of shares of capital stock that such Participating Investor would have been entitled to sell to the Prospective Transferee had the Prohibited Transfer been effected in compliance with the terms of Section 2.2. The sale will be m…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports remedies; functions as self-executing enforcement.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Remedies architecture for the transfer machinery.
Economic
Self-executing enforcement: the books move without the seller's signature; the co-sale put converts violation into the founder's personal repurchase duty plus the investors' legal fees — remedies that run one direction.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: void-ab-initio against secret transfers protects everyone's bargain. Cautionary: the self-help book-entry transfer (2.3(b)) executes without any tribunal; fee-shifting one-way in 2.3(c).
  • Configuration to watch: None gating; enforcement asymmetry noted for synthesis.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Any Proposed Key Holder Transfer not made in compliance with the requirements of this Agreement shall be null and void ab initio”
  • “send to such Key Holder the purchase price for such Transfer Stock as is herein specified and transfer to the name of the Company or such Investor (or…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Third void-ab-initio site in the suite (COI 3.3 bracket, ROFR here; the VA proxy achieves the same by substitution) — the suite's remedies harden wherever founder action is the subject; P8 pattern note.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Non-compliant transfers null and void ab initio, unrecordable, unrecognized; injunctions/specific performance/rescission; on refusal to deliver, the Company/Investor may simply SEND the price and transfer the shares on the books (2.3(b)); prohibited co-sale transfers give investors a 90-day put against the Key Holder personally, with fee-shifting (2.3(c)).
Notes
Catechism: purports remedies; functions as self-executing enforcement. Forensics: with 03/04.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-06SEC-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

[Foreign-person cap]; entity transfers (2.4-2.5)

Regulatory perimeter (DPA/FIRRMA era) plus closure of the entity-transfer loophole — both anti-avoidance in character.

Catechism: purports compliance; functions as compliance.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Regulatory / fund approval stackConfidence: Medium-high
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 28602–30033
… except that the sale (including, without limitation, the delivery of the purchase price) must be made within 90 days after the Participating Investor learns of the Prohibited Transfer, as opposed to the timeframe proscribed in Section 2.2. Such Key Holder shall also reimburse each Participating Investor for any and all reasonable and documented out-of-pocket fees and expenses, including reasonable legal fees and expenses, incurred pursuant to the exercise or the attempted exercise of the Participating Investor’s rights under Section 2.2.
2.4	[Limitation on Foreign Person Investors
. Notwithstanding the covenants set forth in this Section 2, no Investor that is a Foreign Person shall be permitted to obtain greater than 9.9% of the outstanding voting shares of the Company.][FN id=18]
2.5	 [Entity Transfers. No Key Holder that is a trust, corporation, partnership, limited liability company or other entity shall engage or permit any Entity Transfer without complying with this Agreement mutatis mutandis as if such Entity Transfer were a direct Transfer of Transfer Stock by the Key Holder.]
3.	Exempt Transfers.[FN id=19]
3.1	Exempt Transfers
. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary herein, the provisions of Sections 2.1 and 2.2 shall not apply (a) in the case of a Key Holder that is an entity, upon a Transfer by such Key Holder to its stockholders, members, partners or other equity holders; (b)…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
Medium-high

2. Catechism / title

purports compliance; functions as compliance.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
CFIUS-driven ownership cap; anti-avoidance for entity Key Holders.
Economic
Regulatory perimeter (DPA/FIRRMA era) plus closure of the entity-transfer loophole — both anti-avoidance in character.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: compliance perimeter; anti-avoidance symmetry (2.5). Cautionary: bracket-dependent.
  • Configuration to watch: Brackets at configuration (US-nexus deals).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “no Investor that is a Foreign Person shall be permitted to obtain greater than 9.9% of the outstanding voting shares of the Company”

7. Framework / suite notes

New-surface vs 2020 baseline (DPA apparatus); the 9.9% cap binds INVESTORS — one of two places the document restricts the capital side (with 3.3's prohibited-transferee list binding founders instead).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[No Foreign Person investor may exceed 9.9% of voting shares]; Key-Holder entities cannot launder transfers through entity-level ownership changes (2.5 mutatis mutandis).
Notes
Catechism: purports compliance; functions as compliance.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-07SEC-3cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Exempt transfers, exempted offerings, prohibited transferees (3.1-3.3)

Two moral centers: 3.1(b) exempts the LEAVER BUYBACK — repurchase at original cost (not value) flows freely through machinery that gates every other founder transfer; and 3.3 closes the founder's buyer universe (competitors, key customers, sanctioned parties) — the chilling list.

Catechism: purports exemptions; 3.1(b) functions as the buyback channel.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · stipendium_laborisStack: Founder labor / equity stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 29384–30996
…][FN id=18]
2.5	 [Entity Transfers. No Key Holder that is a trust, corporation, partnership, limited liability company or other entity shall engage or permit any Entity Transfer without complying with this Agreement mutatis mutandis as if such Entity Transfer were a direct Transfer of Transfer Stock by the Key Holder.]
3.	Exempt Transfers.[FN id=19]
3.1	Exempt Transfers
. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary herein, the provisions of Sections 2.1 and 2.2 shall not apply (a) in the case of a Key Holder that is an entity, upon a Transfer by such Key Holder to its stockholders, members, partners or other equity holders; (b) to a repurchase of Transfer Stock from a Key Holder by the Company at a price no greater than that originally paid by such Key Holder for such Transfer Stock and pursuant to an agreement containing vesting and/or repurchase provisions approved by a majority of the disinterested members of the Board of Directors; [or]  (c) in the case of a Key Holder that is a natural person, upon a Transfer of Transfer Stock by such Key Holder made for bona fide estate planning purposes, either during such person’s lifetime or on death by will or intestacy to such person’s spouse, including any life partner or similar statutorily-recognized domestic partner, child (natural or adopted), or any other direct lineal descendant of such Key Holder (or such person’s spouse, including any life partner or similar statutorily-recognized domestic partner) (all of the foregoing collectively referred to as “family members”), or any other Person approved by a majority of the dis…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 32569–33927
…ant to Section 2; and provided further in the case of any transfer pursuant to clause (a) or (c) above, that such transfer is made pursuant to a transaction in which there is no consideration actually paid for such transfer.
3.2	Exempted Offerings
. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary herein, the provisions of Section 2 shall not apply to the sale of any Transfer Stock (a) to the public in an offering pursuant to an effective registration statement under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (a “Public Offering”); or (b) pursuant to a Deemed Liquidation Event.
3.3	Prohibited Transferees
. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no Key Holder shall transfer any Transfer Stock to any (a) Competitor; (b) customer, distributor or supplier of the Company, if the majority of the disinterested members of the Board of Directors should determine that such transfer would result in such customer, distributor or supplier receiving information that would place the Company at a competitive disadvantage with respect to such customer, distributor or supplier; (c) Sanctioned Party. [(d) Foreign Person that pursuant to any such transfer would acquire any DPA Triggering Rights, unless otherwise approved by the majority of the disinterested members of the] Board of Directors][FN id=20].

2. Catechism / title

purports exemptions; 3.1(b) functions as the buyback channel.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Stipendium laborissecondary category
Moral hinge
Labor may be paid; capital should not hide yield inside labor language.
Legitimate form
Actual labor or administration; reasonable cost; transparency; no surplus extraction; mission-serving stewardship.
Abuse form
Management, monitoring, facility, compliance, or advisory fees exceeding plausible labor or administrative cost.

4. Functions

Legal
Exemption schedule and buyer prohibitions.
Economic
Two moral centers: 3.1(b) exempts the LEAVER BUYBACK — repurchase at original cost (not value) flows freely through machinery that gates every other founder transfer; and 3.3 closes the founder's buyer universe (competitors, key customers, sanctioned parties) — the chilling list.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: estate-planning and entity exemptions with joinder; anti-competitor rationale is real. Cautionary: the at-cost repurchase exemption's asymmetry (cost out easy, value out hard); board-judgment buyer vetoes. Potentially extractive (configuration): carve-out (e) omitted + broad Competitor definition = no lawful buyer but the cap table.
  • Configuration to watch: Carve-out (e) % and inclusion; Competitor [description]; disinterested-majority reality.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “to a repurchase of Transfer Stock from a Key Holder by the Company at a price no greater than that originally paid by such Key Holder for such Transfe…”
  • “no Key Holder shall transfer any Transfer Stock to any (a)”

7. Framework / suite notes

3.1(b) is the suite's leaver-machinery lubricant #2 (with COI-19's §500 waiver): at-cost buybacks of vested labor equity pass exempt — SV14-01's buyback limb, present and FRICTIONLESS while every value-realizing transfer is gated. The [__]% lifetime carve-out (e) is the drafted founder-relief valve.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Exemptions: entity distributions, estate planning to family/trusts, [pledges with joinder], [a [__]% lifetime carve-out], and — 3.1(b) — Company repurchases AT ORIGINAL COST under board-approved vesting/repurchase agreements; public offerings and DLEs exempt; prohibited transferees: Competitors, information-risk customers/suppliers (board judgment), Sanctioned Parties, [DPA-triggering Foreign Persons].
Notes
Catechism: purports exemptions; 3.1(b) functions as the buyback channel. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-08SEC-4cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Legend and transfer-agent enforcement

The machinery follows the paper: every share announces its own restrictions and the agent enforces them mechanically.

Catechism: purports notice; functions as perfection.Geometry: liquidity_pressure · information_asymmetryStack: Liquidity / exit / financing stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 33483–34914
…istributor or supplier receiving information that would place the Company at a competitive disadvantage with respect to such customer, distributor or supplier; (c) Sanctioned Party. [(d) Foreign Person that pursuant to any such transfer would acquire any DPA Triggering Rights, unless otherwise approved by the majority of the disinterested members of the] Board of Directors][FN id=20].
4.	Legend
. Each certificate, instrument, or book entry representing shares of Transfer Stock held by the Key Holders or issued to any permitted transferee in connection with a transfer permitted by Section 3.1 hereof shall be notated with the following legend:
THE SALE, PLEDGE, HYPOTHECATION, OR TRANSFER OF THE SECURITIES REPRESENTED HEREBY IS SUBJECT TO, AND IN CERTAIN CASES PROHIBITED BY, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF A CERTAIN RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL AND CO-SALE AGREEMENT BY AND AMONG THE STOCKHOLDER, THE CORPORATION AND CERTAIN OTHER HOLDERS OF STOCK OF THE CORPORATION. COPIES OF SUCH AGREEMENT MAY BE OBTAINED UPON WRITTEN REQUEST TO THE SECRETARY OF THE CORPORATION.
Each Key Holder agrees that the Company may instruct its transfer agent to impose transfer restrictions on the shares notated with the legend referred to in this Section 4 to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, and the Company agrees to promptly do so. The legend shall be removed upon termination of this Agreement at the request of the holder.

MG-NVCA-ROFR-09SEC-5cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Lock-up (5.1-5.3)

Founder liquidity stays locked through the exit event itself — and this clause alone outlives the document, so the last thing the machinery does is persist past its own death.

… provisions of this Agreement, and the Company agrees to promptly do so. The legend shall be removed upon termination of this Agreement at the request of the holder.
5.	Lock-Up.
5.1	Agreement to Lock-Up
. Each Key Holder hereby agrees that it will not, without the prior written consent of the managing underwriter, during the period commencing on the date of the final prospectus relating to a Public Offering, the registration by the Company of shares of its Common Stock or any other equity securities on a registration statement (other than a Form S-8), and ending on the date specified by the managing underwriter or the Company, as applicable (such period not to exceed 180 days, or such other period as may be requested by the Company or an underwriter to accommodate regulatory restrictions on (1) the publication or other distribution of research reports and (2) analyst recommendations and opinions, including, but not limited to, the restrictions contained in applicable FINRA rules, or any successor provisions or amendments thereto), (a) lend; offer; pledge; sell; contract to sell; sell any option or contract to purchase; purchase any option or contract to sell; grant any option, right, or warrant to purchase; or otherwise transfer or dispose of, directly or indirectly, any shares of Common Stock (or other equity se…

…tains terms that are more restrictive to the Key Holder, the Key Holder agrees that the Key Holder shall be subject to the more restrictive terms and compliance therewith shall be deemed compliance herewith.[FN id=21]
5.2	Stop Transfer Instructions
. In order to enforce the foregoing covenant, the Company may impose stop-transfer instructions with respect to the shares of capital stock of each Key Holder (and transferees and assignees thereof) until the end of such restricted period.
5.3	Survival
. Unless and only to the extent otherwise superseded by an underwriting agreement entered into in connection with the underwritten Public Offering, the obligations of the Key Holders under this Section 5 shall survive the termination of this Agreement or any provision(s) of this Agreement. 
6.	Miscellaneous.
6.1	Term
. This Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the earlier of (a) immediately prior to the consummation of the Company’s initial Public Offering (“IPO”) [or Qualified Direct Listing, as applicable], and (b) the consummation of a Deemed Liquidation Event [in which the consideration received by the Investors in such Deemed Liquidation Event is in the form of cash and/or publicly traded securities, or if the Investors receive substantially similar rights].

The Company’s underwriters are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Section 5 and shall have the right, power and authority to enforce the provisions hereof as though they were a party hereto. Each Key Holder further agrees to execute such agreements as may be reasonably requested by the underwriters that are consistent with this Section 5 or that are necessary to give further effect thereto. In the event a Key Holder is or becomes party to a lock-up or market standoff agreement with the Company or any third party beneficiary of this Section 5.1 that contains terms that are more restrictive to the Key Holder, the Key Holder agrees that the Key Holder shall be subject to the more restrictive terms and compliance therewith shall be deemed compliance herewith.[FN id=21]

rofr_cosale_lockup_combination
Exemplarity (Mode 2)

Confidence

2. Catechism / title

purports market hygiene; functions as terminal liquidity lock.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Carentia pecuniaesecondary category
Moral hinge
Mere lack of money is not enough; absence must not collapse into universal time-value entitlement.
Legitimate form
Only under narrowly justified, verified, non-universal conditions if accepted by a reviewed moral regime.
Abuse form
Every loan becomes interest-bearing by nature; restorative compensation collapses into usurious yield.

4. Functions

Legal
IPO market-standoff covenant.
Economic
Founder liquidity stays locked through the exit event itself — and this clause alone outlives the document, so the last thing the machinery does is persist past its own death.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: market standoffs are underwriting infrastructure; investor lock-ups live in the IRA (2.11 territory) — parity question for P8. Cautionary: survival + ratchet + underwriter third-party enforcement.
  • Configuration to watch: P8: does the IRA impose the same standoff on INVESTORS (parity check)?

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “such period not to exceed 180 days”
  • “the obligations of the Key Holders under this Section 5 shall survive the termination of this Agreement”
  • “the Key Holder agrees that the Key Holder shall be subject to the more restrictive terms and compliance therewith shall be deemed compliance herewith”

7. Framework / suite notes

Flag rofr_cosale_lockup_combination (1.0) attaches here as the completing member of the combination the flag names. Most-restrictive-terms rule: the covenant automatically ratchets to the harshest instrument the holder ever signs — a one-way term escalator.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
180-day post-offering lock (extendable for regulatory windows), covering every economic-transfer form incl. swaps; 10b5-1 plans carved; underwriters are third-party beneficiaries; stop-transfer teeth; a more-restrictive later lock-up supersedes; the Section SURVIVES termination of the Agreement.
Notes
Catechism: purports market hygiene; functions as terminal liquidity lock. Forensics-light: stage 5 note.

2. Catechism / title

purports term; functions as sunset-with-conditions.

3. Title breakdown

Liquidity pressureprimary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.
Periculum sortissecondary category
Moral hinge
Capital may share surplus where it truly shares downside.
Legitimate form
Principal can actually be lost; no guarantee, seniority, collateral, redemption, or control stack that empties risk; return proportionate to risk.
Abuse form
Risk language used while capital is insulated through seniority, redemption, guarantees, control rights, or downside shifting.

4. Functions

Legal
Termination and housekeeping.
Economic
The machinery dies at the public exit — with the DLE limb bracket-conditioned on investors' consideration quality (the machinery persists through a private-stock DLE unless investors are cashed out).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: sunset. Cautionary: the bracketed persistence condition.
  • Configuration to watch: Bracket in/out.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “This Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the earlier of”
  • “immediately prior to the consummation of the Company’s initial Public Offering”

7. Framework / suite notes

Sunset parallel to COI-12/VA-13; the bracketed DLE condition is investor-protective persistence.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Auto-termination immediately before IPO [/QDL] or at a DLE [if investors receive cash/public securities or similar rights]; share-count adjustments; sole-ownership representation (community-property acknowledgment).
Notes
Catechism: purports term; functions as sunset-with-conditions.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-11SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Amendment and waiver (6.8)

The government of the founder-binding machinery excludes the bound: investors can waive the ROFR/co-sale for any specific transfer without any founder's consent — the rights over founder exits are the investors' to trade.

2. Catechism / title

purports amendment rules; functions as one-sided waiver power.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Amendment architecture.
Economic
The government of the founder-binding machinery excludes the bound: investors can waive the ROFR/co-sale for any specific transfer without any founder's consent — the rights over founder exits are the investors' to trade.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: multi-key form; the proportionality proviso. Cautionary: per-transfer waivers without the founder; fall-away.
  • Configuration to watch: Fall-away %; proportionality proviso in/out.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “no consent of any Key Holder shall be required for a waiver of the applicability of the rights provided in Section 2 of this Agreement as to any speci…”
  • “provided that the Company may in its sole discretion waive compliance with any provision of this Agreement if observance of the terms would cause the …”

7. Framework / suite notes

P8 check 6 (gradation), ROFR cell: same three-key shape as VA 7.8 with the same unequal metal (fall-away + carve-outs) — the pattern is now suite-wide; the (c)(i) ROFR-Investor proportionality proviso is a genuine intra-investor fairness device.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Three keys (Company / [Q]KH % of Transfer Stock [fall-away if KH below [__]% of capital] / Investor %); binding on non-consenters; [same-fashion protection with the ROFR-Investor proportionality proviso]; NO Key Holder consent for per-transfer waivers of Section 2 rights (6.8(c)(ii)) nor for changes not applying to them; Company-only Schedule A updates; Company sole-discretion sanctions waiver.
Notes
Catechism: purports amendment rules; functions as one-sided waiver power. Forensics: stage 5.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-12SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Assignment, additional investors, additional key holders (6.9, 6.11, 6.17)

The regime propagates asymmetrically: investor rights travel freely to affiliates and large transferees; founder burdens attach automatically to every significant employee — the VA-14 dragnet's ROFR twin, pointed at labor.

Catechism: purports joinder; functions as burden propagation.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Founder labor / equity stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 56726–58174
…	Additional Key Holders
. In the event that after the date of this Agreement, the Company issues shares of Common Stock, or options to purchase Common Stock, to any employee or consultant, which shares or options would collectively constitute with respect to such employee or consultant (taking into account all shares of Common Stock, options and other purchase rights held by such employee or consultant) 1% or more of the Company’s then outstanding Common Stock (treating for this purpose all shares of Common Stock issuable upon exercise of or conversion of outstanding options, warrants or convertible securities, as if exercised or converted), the Company shall, as a condition to such issuance, cause such employee or consultant to execute a counterpart signature page hereto as a Key Holder, and such person shall thereby be bound by, and subject to, all the terms and provisions of this Agreement applicable to a Key Holder. 
6.18	Sanctions
. For the avoidance of doubt, at any time that a Person is or becomes a Sanctioned Party, all rights granted to the Person under this Agreement, including, without limitation, any right to purchase any Transfer Stock, will be immediately suspended for so long as such Person is a Sanctioned Party or until authorization is issued by a relevant government authority as required by applicable Sanctions.
6.19	Costs of Enforcement
. [Each party will bear its own costs in respect of any disputes arisi…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports joinder; functions as burden propagation.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Party-perpetuation architecture.
Economic
The regime propagates asymmetrically: investor rights travel freely to affiliates and large transferees; founder burdens attach automatically to every significant employee — the VA-14 dragnet's ROFR twin, pointed at labor.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: continuity mechanics. Cautionary: the conscription asymmetry.
  • Configuration to watch: 1% threshold parity across VA/ROFR (P8).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “the Company shall, as a condition to such issuance, cause such employee or consultant to execute a counterpart signature page hereto as a Key Holder”

7. Framework / suite notes

6.17 + VA 7.1(b): the suite conscripts labor at 1% ownership into BOTH regimes; P8 rights-accumulation exhibit (who accumulates obligations, mirror-image of who accumulates rights).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
KH successors/assignees join as condition of transfer; Investor rights assignable to Affiliates or [threshold] transferees (Company consent not-unreasonably-withheld otherwise); Company assignment only to an acquirer; new preferred purchasers join as Investors; every 1%+ employee/consultant equity recipient is CONSCRIPTED as a Key Holder (6.17).
Notes
Catechism: purports joinder; functions as burden propagation.
MG-NVCA-ROFR-13SEC-6cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Boilerplate with load (6.4-6.7, 6.10, 6.12-6.16, 6.18-6.19)

Same enforcement-economics package as the VA (jury waiver, [fees], arbitration option), the Side-Letters hook again, aggregation again — and 6.16 grants SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE expressly to Investors only.

Catechism: purports boilerplate; margins to the enforcers.Geometry: governance_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Named-investor / side-right stackConfidence: Medium-high

…eding, any claim that it is not subject personally to the jurisdiction of the above-named courts, that its property is exempt or immune from attachment or execution, that the suit, action or proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum, that the venue of the suit, action or proceeding is improper or that this Agreement or the subject matter hereof may not be enforced in or by such court.] 
(b)	Each of the parties to this Agreement consents to personal jurisdiction for any equitable action sought in the U.S. District Court for the District of [_____] or any state court of [state] having subject matter jurisdiction.
(c)	Waiver of Jury Trial: EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, THE OTHER TRANSACTION AGREEMENTS, THE SECURITIES OR THE SUBJECT MATTER HEREOF OR THEREOF. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS, AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS. THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS. EACH PARTY HERETO HEREBY FURTHER WARRANTS AND REPRESENTS THAT SUCH PARTY HAS REVIEWED THIS WAIVER WITH…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 55826–57260
… Act of 2000, e.g., www.docusign.com) or other transmission method and any counterpart so delivered shall be deemed to have been duly and validly delivered and be valid and effective for all purposes. 
6.15	Aggregation of Stock
. All shares of Capital Stock held or acquired by Affiliates shall be aggregated together for the purpose of determining the availability of any rights under this Agreement and such Affiliates may apportion such rights as among themselves in any manner they deem appropriate. 
6.16	Specific Performance
. In addition to any and all other remedies that may be available at law in the event of any breach of this Agreement, each Investor shall be entitled to specific performance of the agreements and obligations of the Company and the Key Holders hereunder and to such other injunction or other equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
6.17	Additional Key Holders
. In the event that after the date of this Agreement, the Company issues shares of Common Stock, or options to purchase Common Stock, to any employee or consultant, which shares or options would collectively constitute with respect to such employee or consultant (taking into account all shares of Common Stock, options and other purchase rights held by such employee or consultant) 1% or more of the Company’s then outstanding Common Stock (treating for this purpose all shares of Common Stock issuable upon e…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)ROFR · chars 55406–56876
…or convenience only and are not to be considered in construing or interpreting this Agreement.
6.14	Counterparts
. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Counterparts may be delivered via electronic mail (including pdf or any electronic signature complying with the U.S. federal ESIGN Act of 2000, e.g., www.docusign.com) or other transmission method and any counterpart so delivered shall be deemed to have been duly and validly delivered and be valid and effective for all purposes. 
6.15	Aggregation of Stock
. All shares of Capital Stock held or acquired by Affiliates shall be aggregated together for the purpose of determining the availability of any rights under this Agreement and such Affiliates may apportion such rights as among themselves in any manner they deem appropriate. 
6.16	Specific Performance
. In addition to any and all other remedies that may be available at law in the event of any breach of this Agreement, each Investor shall be entitled to specific performance of the agreements and obligations of the Company and the Key Holders hereunder and to such other injunction or other equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Miscellaneous with distributional margins.
Economic
Same enforcement-economics package as the VA (jury waiver, [fees], arbitration option), the Side-Letters hook again, aggregation again — and 6.16 grants SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE expressly to Investors only.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: standard forms. Cautionary: the margins lean one way, again.
  • Configuration to watch: Arbitration/court; fees bracket.

  • “EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT”

Severability fail-open (third document); jury-waiver text verbatim-identical to the VA's (P8 wording-parity datum); 6.16's one-way specific-performance grant completes the remedies asymmetry of 2.3.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[JAMS arbitration]/[court] + all-caps jury waiver + [prevailing-party fees]; notices; entire agreement incl. Side Letters; fail-open severability; Delaware law; affiliate aggregation; investor-side specific performance (6.16); sanctions suspension of rights (6.18).

ROFR — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-ROFR-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Liquidity / exit / financing stack
Synthesis
Gauntlet chain: notice->options->co-sale->waterfall-import->lock-up; lock-up survives the document
MG-NVCA-ROFR-SYN-02cautionary
Stack
Founder labor / equity stack
Synthesis
Labor cell: at-cost buyback exemption + 1% conscription + QKH condition + buyer-universe closure; SV14-01 buyback limb present, assembly at P8
MG-NVCA-ROFR-SYN-03cautionary
Stack
Governance / operational control stack
Synthesis
Government excludes the governed: per-transfer waivers, self-help enforcement, one-way specific performance
MG-NVCA-ROFR-SYN-04cautionary
Stack
Named-investor / side-right stack
Synthesis
[Named exception] in Competitor def; Side-Letters hook; aggregation; investor fall-away
MG-NVCA-ROFR-SYN-05licit
Stack
Mitigator stack
Synthesis
Estate-planning exemptions, [lifetime carve-out], [forfeiture rule], proportionality proviso, IPO sunset

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-ROFR-A — Relief-valve configurationcautionary
Configuration
Carve-out (e) in at meaningful %; forfeiture in; narrow Competitor; proportionality in
NVCA-ROFR-B — Standard-filled roundpotentially extractive
Configuration
Carve-out out; broad Competitor; per-transfer waivers exercised; 2.2(e) enforced. = v3.15 baseline's reading
NVCA-ROFR-C — Full lock configurationsevere
Configuration
B + waterfall import on CoC sale w/ multiple >1x + personal-purchase enforced + at-cost buyback active (SV14-01 both limbs, suite level)

NVCA Model Management Rights Letter (July 2020 text)

Risk: cautionary (configuration-pending; PE at standard fill)Sim (disclosed): 0.8529Mode-2 D: 0.00006 scored rows

The information-valve keystone: board-grade knowledge without board duties — rights the VCOC rules require to be exercised.

MG-NVCA-MRL-01HEAD, PRELIM-NOTES, LETTER-OPEN, CLOSINGlicitnot applicable (exemplarity)

Preliminary notes, letterhead, execution (the VCOC frame)

The frame is the finding: the letter exists so the FUND can satisfy its own regulatory exemption, and the exemption requires the rights to be USED — monitoring-as-influence is not optional decoration but a regulatory duty of the holder.

Catechism: Grouped boilerplate — no operative function to catechize.Geometry: not applicable (grouped boilerplate)Stack: Confidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)MRL · chars 1141–2616
…n venture capital investments.  An investment in a portfolio company qualifies as a “venture capital investment” if the fund obtains certain management rights with respect to the portfolio company.  “Management rights,” in turn, are defined as contractual rights running directly from the portfolio company to the fund that give the fund the right to participate substantially in, or substantially influence the conduct of, the management of the portfolio company.  In addition to obtaining management rights, the fund is also required to actually exercise its management rights with respect to one (1) or more of its portfolio companies every year.
In order to build a case for an exemption from the ERISA Plan asset rules, a venture fund will generally ask each of its portfolio companies to sign a management rights letter in connection with the fund’s initial investment.  An example of such a letter follows.


[PORTFOLIO COMPANY LETTERHEAD]
[______], 20[__]
[Investor Name]
[Street Address]
[City], [State] [Zip]
	Re:	Management Rights
Ladies and Gentlemen:
This letter will confirm our agreement that pursuant to and effective as of your purchase of [________] shares of Series [_] Preferred Stock of [_____________________] (the “Company”),  [Investor Name] (the “Investor”) shall be entitled to the following contractual management rights, in addition to any rights to non-public financial information, inspection rights, and other rights specifically provided to al…
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)MRL · chars 1001–2439
…enture capital operating company (a “VCOC”).  To qualify as a VCOC, the fund must have at least fifty percent (50%) of its assets invested in venture capital investments.  An investment in a portfolio company qualifies as a “venture capital investment” if the fund obtains certain management rights with respect to the portfolio company.  “Management rights,” in turn, are defined as contractual rights running directly from the portfolio company to the fund that give the fund the right to participate substantially in, or substantially influence the conduct of, the management of the portfolio company.  In addition to obtaining management rights, the fund is also required to actually exercise its management rights with respect to one (1) or more of its portfolio companies every year.
In order to build a case for an exemption from the ERISA Plan asset rules, a venture fund will generally ask each of its portfolio companies to sign a management rights letter in connection with the fund’s initial investment.  An example of such a letter follows.


[PORTFOLIO COMPANY LETTERHEAD]
[______], 20[__]
[Investor Name]
[Street Address]
[City], [State] [Zip]
	Re:	Management Rights
Ladies and Gentlemen:
This letter will confirm our agreement that pursuant to and effective as of your purchase of [________] shares of Series [_] Preferred Stock of [_____________________] (the “Company”),  [Investor Name] (the “Investor”) shall be entitl…

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
ERISA/VCOC preliminary notes: a fund avoiding plan-asset status must obtain contractual management rights AND 'actually exercise' them annually; letterhead/address block; signature blocks.

MG-NVCA-MRL-02PARA-1cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Consultation and advice right

A standing seat at management's table without a board seat's duties: advice flows in, accountability does not attach — the structural form of influence-without-office.

Catechism: purports advice channel (locatio conductio of attention); functions as influence-without-office.Geometry: information_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High

…ANY LETTERHEAD]
[______], 20[__]
[Investor Name]
[Street Address]
[City], [State] [Zip]
	Re:	Management Rights
Ladies and Gentlemen:
This letter will confirm our agreement that pursuant to and effective as of your purchase of [________] shares of Series [_] Preferred Stock of [_____________________] (the “Company”),  [Investor Name] (the “Investor”) shall be entitled to the following contractual management rights, in addition to any rights to non-public financial information, inspection rights, and other rights specifically provided to all investors in the current financing:
1.	[If Investor is not represented on Company’s Board of Directors, Investor shall be entitled to consult with and advise management of the Company on significant business issues, including management’s proposed annual operating plans, and management will meet with Investor regularly during each year at the Company’s facilities at mutually agreeable times for such consultation and advice and to review progress in achieving said plans.][FN id=1]
2.	[Investor may examine the books and records of the Company and inspect its facilities and may request information at reasonable times and intervals concerning the general status of the Company’s financial condition and operations, provided that access to highly confidential proprietary information and facilities need not be provided.][FN id=2]
3.	[If Investor is not represented on the Company’s Board of Directors, the Company sh…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports advice channel (locatio conductio of attention); functions as influence-without-office.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Contractual consultation right (VCOC management right #1).
Economic
A standing seat at management's table without a board seat's duties: advice flows in, accountability does not attach — the structural form of influence-without-office.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: advice is not control; conditioned on non-representation (no doubling). Cautionary: 'significant business issues' + annual-plan review + regular cadence = shadow-governance surface; no reciprocal duty.
  • Configuration to watch: Cadence in practice; interaction with any Preferred Director seat (condition drops the right).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Investor shall be entitled to consult with and advise management of the Company on significant business issues, including management’s proposed annual…”

7. Framework / suite notes

Paired with the VCOC duty to exercise: the fund MUST use this channel somewhere every year — consultation pressure is regulatorily load-bearing (prelim notes).

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[If not board-represented:] Investor entitled to consult with and advise management on significant business issues incl. proposed annual operating plans; regular meetings at Company facilities to review progress.
Notes
Catechism: purports advice channel (locatio conductio of attention); functions as influence-without-office.
MG-NVCA-MRL-03PARA-2cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Inspection and information right

Direct examination beyond stockholder statutory rights (DGCL 220 needs proper purpose; this needs none) — with the carve-out as the company's one shield.

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports inspection; functions as unconditioned examination.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Governance asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.

4. Functions

Legal
Inspection right (VCOC management right #2).
Economic
Direct examination beyond stockholder statutory rights (DGCL 220 needs proper purpose; this needs none) — with the carve-out as the company's one shield.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: reasonable-times limits + confidentiality carve-out. Cautionary: purpose-free inspection; cumulative with IRA rights.
  • Configuration to watch: P8: count the parallel inspection channels per holder class.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Investor may examine the books and records of the Company and inspect its facilities and may request information at reasonable times and intervals”
  • “provided that access to highly confidential proprietary information and facilities need not be provided”

7. Framework / suite notes

Overlaps the IRA's inspection right (3.2 territory) — P8 rights-accumulation: the same holder may hold BOTH channels plus board materials; the MRL grants it WITHOUT the IRA's Major-Investor threshold.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Investor may examine books and records, inspect facilities, request information at reasonable times on financial condition and operations; highly confidential proprietary information and facilities carved out.
Notes
Catechism: purports inspection; functions as unconditioned examination.
MG-NVCA-MRL-04PARA-3cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Board-materials mirror and address-the-board right

The board's information stream duplicated to a non-director: everything directors see, when they see it, minus fiduciary duties, minus deliberation votes — observer-grade access without observer designation (and expressly avoiding the DPA observer trigger, per MRL-05).

Catechism: purports information courtesy; functions as board-mirror without office. The suite's information-valve keystonGeometry: information_asymmetry · control_without_stewardshipStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Analyzer flag
information_mrl_shadow_governance
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports information courtesy; functions as board-mirror without office. The suite's information-valve keystone.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Control without stewardshipsecondary category
Moral hinge
Dominion must be exercised as stewardship, not extraction.
Legitimate form
Narrow impairment protections; mission constraints; fiduciary-like accountability; independent governance; ordinary-course carveouts.
Abuse form
Operational vetoes, board capture, information capture, or exit rights used without long-term stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
Board-information mirror + speaking right (VCOC management right #3).
Economic
The board's information stream duplicated to a non-director: everything directors see, when they see it, minus fiduciary duties, minus deliberation votes — observer-grade access without observer designation (and expressly avoiding the DPA observer trigger, per MRL-05).

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: exclusion machinery (privilege, confidential info) is real and counsel-gated. Cautionary→PE-leaning: the full-mirror default; combined with COI-18 and IRA rights the one-way information valve completes (P8 cell).
  • Configuration to watch: Exclusion practice; representative identity (same person as IRA observer?).

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “give a representative of Investor copies of all notices, minutes, consents and other material that the Company provides to its directors”
  • “such representative may address the Board with respect to Investor’s concerns regarding significant business issues facing the Company”

7. Framework / suite notes

Flag information_mrl_shadow_governance (1.45) attaches — the flag NAMES this document. Severe signal root: information without accountability, in its cleanest suite form: the COI-18 opportunity waiver means what is learned here may be used elsewhere freely.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
[If not board-represented:] Investor's representative receives ALL board materials concurrently with directors (privilege/confidentiality exclusions on counsel's advice); may address the Board on significant issues at scheduled meetings.
Notes
Catechism: purports information courtesy; functions as board-mirror without office. The suite's information-valve keystone.
MG-NVCA-MRL-05PARA-4licitneutral (exemplarity)

DPA negative covenant

A ceiling written for regulatory geometry: the letter grants exactly as much influence as VCOC requires while disclaiming exactly what would trigger CFIUS jurisdiction — the rights are calibrated to sit between two regulators.

Catechism: purports rights ceiling; functions as regulatory calibration. No exemplarity credit (regulatory compliance is Geometry: governance_asymmetry · information_asymmetryStack: Regulatory / fund approval stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)MRL · chars 3860–5287
…ctors determines in good faith, upon advice of counsel, that such exclusion is reasonably necessary to preserve the attorney-client privilege, to protect highly confidential proprietary information, or for other similar reasons.  Upon reasonable notice and at a scheduled meeting of the Board or such other time, if any, as the Board may determine in its sole discretion, such representative may address the Board with respect to Investor’s concerns regarding significant business issues facing the Company.][FN id=3]
4.	[Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this letter agreement, solely by reason of becoming party to this letter agreement, Investor will not obtain with respect to the Company, and the Company will not provide to Investor, any of the following rights, as defined in Section 721 of the Defense Production Act, as amended, including its implementing regulations: (a) “control” of the Company, including the power to determine, direct or decide any important matters affecting the Company; (b) membership or observer rights on the Board of Directors or equivalent body of the Company, or the right to nominate an individual to a position on the Board of Directors or equivalent body of the Company; (c) access to any “material nonpublic technical information” in the possession of the Company [(provided, however, that such prohibited information shall not include financial information regarding the pe…

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
licit
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports rights ceiling; functions as regulatory calibration. No exemplarity credit (regulatory compliance is owed, not supererogatory — G3 discipline).

3. Title breakdown

Governance asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Control must answer to stewardship and the good of the enterprise.
Legitimate form
Protective rights limited to real impairment; objective thresholds; ordinary-course carveouts; accountability to mission and all stakeholders.
Abuse form
Control without matching downside, labor, fiduciary accountability, or mission duty.
Information asymmetrysecondary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.

4. Functions

Legal
CFIUS-avoidance negative covenant (defines the letter's rights as non-DPA-triggering).
Economic
A ceiling written for regulatory geometry: the letter grants exactly as much influence as VCOC requires while disclaiming exactly what would trigger CFIUS jurisdiction — the rights are calibrated to sit between two regulators.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: a genuine mutual ceiling; the financial-information carve-back is honest. Cautionary: none of substance — but note the ceiling exists for the investor's regulatory convenience as much as the company's.
  • Configuration to watch: Foreign-investor deals only; carve-back scope.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “Investor will not obtain with respect to the Company, and the Company will not provide to Investor, any of the following rights”

7. Framework / suite notes

New surface vs 2020-era MRL usage (FIRRMA-era drafting); the covenant binds BOTH sides and protects the company from inadvertent foreign-investment exposure.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Notwithstanding the letter, Investor obtains NO DPA-defined rights: no control, no board membership/observer/nomination rights, no material nonpublic technical information [financial info carve-back], no substantive decision-making involvement in sensitive data/critical tech/critical infrastructure.
Notes
Catechism: purports rights ceiling; functions as regulatory calibration. No exemplarity credit (regulatory compliance is owed, not supererogatory — G3 discipline).
MG-NVCA-MRL-06PARA-4cautionaryneutral (exemplarity)

Confidentiality tie-in and termination

The letter's information flows into the IRA's confidentiality regime (one regime, two channels — P8 coherence point), and the rights die at exit like the rest of the suite — with the bracketed merger conditions protecting the investor's rights through reorganizations.

Catechism: purports housekeeping; functions as regime tie-in + sunset.Geometry: information_asymmetry · liquidity_pressureStack: Information / KPI stackConfidence: High
Source text (deterministic extraction · evidence window)MRL · chars 5250–6760
…inancial information regarding the performance of the Company, and provided further that Investor may confer with the Company about such financial information)]; and (d) any “involvement” (other than through voting of shares) in “substantive decision making” of the Company regarding (i) the use, development, acquisition, safekeeping, or release of “sensitive personal data” of U.S. citizens maintained or collected by the Company, (ii) the use, development, acquisition, or release of “critical technologies,” or (iii) the management, operation, manufacture, or supply of “covered investment critical infrastructure.”[FN id=4]
Investor agrees that any confidential information provided to or learned by it in connection with its rights under this letter shall be subject to the confidentiality provisions set forth in that certain Investors’ Rights Agreement of even date herewith by and among the Company, the Investor and other investors.[FN id=5]  
The rights described herein shall terminate and be of no further force or effect upon (a) such time as no shares of the Company’s stock are held by the Investor or its affiliates; (b) the consummation of the sale of the Company’s securities pursuant to a registration statement filed by the Company under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, in connection with the firm commitment underwritten offering of its securities to the general public; or (c) the consummation of a merger or consolidation of the Company [(x)] that is effected (i) for independen…
