…ment, acquisition, safekeeping, or release of “sensitive personal data” of U.S. citizens maintained or collected by the Company, (ii) the use, development, acquisition, or release of “critical technologies,” or (iii) the management, operation, manufacture, or supply of “covered investment critical infrastructure.”[FN id=4]
Investor agrees that any confidential information provided to or learned by it in connection with its rights under this letter shall be subject to the confidentiality provisions set forth in that certain Investors’ Rights Agreement of even date herewith by and among the Company, the Investor and other investors.[FN id=5]  
The rights described herein shall terminate and be of no further force or effect upon (a) such time as no shares of the Company’s stock are held by the Investor or its affiliates; (b) the consummation of the sale of the Company’s securities pursuant to a registration statement filed by the Company under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, in connection with the firm commitment underwritten offering of its securities to the general public; or (c) the consummation of a merger or consolidation of the Company [(x)] that is effected (i) for independent business reasons unrelated to extinguishing such rights; and (ii) for purposes other than (A) the reincorporation of the Company in a different state; or (B) the formation of a …

1. Moral verdict (two axes)

Risk (Mode 1, controlling)
cautionary
Exemplarity (Mode 2)
neutral (exemplarity)
Confidence
High

2. Catechism / title

purports housekeeping; functions as regime tie-in + sunset.

3. Title breakdown

Information asymmetryprimary category
Moral hinge
Information should enable accountability, not unilateral command.
Legitimate form
Stage-appropriate reporting; confidentiality; reciprocal accountability; no investor-defined metric capture.
Abuse form
Information without accountability; metrics used to govern without bearing stewardship responsibility.
Liquidity pressuresecondary category
Moral hinge
Liquidity is not neutral when it determines who can wait and who must yield.
Legitimate form
Reciprocal timing protections; short and objective exclusivity; fair sale terms; hardship and fiduciary carveouts.
Abuse form
Liquidity without reciprocity; exit power used to force sale, recapitalization, or concession.

4. Functions

Legal
Confidentiality incorporation + termination provisions.
Economic
The letter's information flows into the IRA's confidentiality regime (one regime, two channels — P8 coherence point), and the rights die at exit like the rest of the suite — with the bracketed merger conditions protecting the investor's rights through reorganizations.

5. Features

  • Reading: Licit: sunset + survival structure standard. Cautionary: the bracketed merger conditions (rights persist unless cashed out — investor-protective persistence, as at ROFR 6.1).
  • Configuration to watch: Bracket configuration; IRA-confidentiality dependency noted.

6. Evidence (verified spans)

  • “any confidential information provided to or learned by it in connection with its rights under this letter shall be subject to the confidentiality prov…”
  • “The rights described herein shall terminate and be of no further force or effect upon”

7. Framework / suite notes

The IRA tie-in is a live cross-reference (P8 check 3): the MRL's confidentiality has no content of its own — if the IRA's provisions change, this letter's regime changes silently.

8. Mechanism

Mechanism
Confidential information under this letter is governed by the IRA's confidentiality provisions; rights terminate at zero-holdings, IPO, or a merger [effected for independent business reasons and] [unless comparable rights or cash/listed-securities consideration]; confidentiality survives.
Notes
Catechism: purports housekeeping; functions as regime tie-in + sunset.

MRL — stacks & scenarios (stage-6 synthesis)

MG-NVCA-MRL-SYN-01cautionary
Stack
Information / KPI stack
Synthesis
Three channels compose into board-grade monitoring without duties; with COI-18 and the IRA the suite's one-way valve completes
MG-NVCA-MRL-SYN-02licit
Stack
Regulatory / fund approval stack
Synthesis
The VCOC/DPA double calibration — the letter as regulatory geometry

Configuration scenarios

NVCA-MRL-A — Light-exercise configurationcautionary
Configuration
Rights held, exercised lightly; exclusion machinery used
NVCA-MRL-B — Exercise-duty concentratedpotentially extractive
Configuration
The annual VCOC exercise duty discharged here: regular plan consultations + full mirror + IRA stack on the same holder

References / Authorities

collapsed by default — click to expand

The canonical workbook's Authorities Library (20 rows: 13 Verified, 7 Draft). Verified quotations are shown only where the workbook marks the row Verified and supplies a quotation; Draft authorities are rendered as attributed paraphrases only. Rows whose IDs appear verbatim in this dossier's clause cards are tagged; shorthand citations in clause prose (e.g. section-specific CST references) are not auto-matched to library rows.

AUTH-AQU-ST78 Verifiedcited in this dossier

Thomas Aquinas, Summa Theologiae · II-II q.78, especially a.1-a.2 (corpus-confirmed; operative replies a.2 ad 1, ad 5, ad 7) — verified: ST II-II q.78 a.1 corpus; a.2 ad 5 (English Dominican trans. 1920, newadvent.org/summa/3078.htm, fetched 2026-07-03) · Scholastic theology

Usury concerns receiving surplus from a mutuum as such; compensation may be distinct where tied to actual loss, partnership risk, or other external grounds.

[a.1 corpus] “To take usury for money lent is unjust in itself, because this is to sell what does not exist, and this evidently leads to inequality which is contrary to justice.” || [a.2 ad 5] “he that entrusts his money to a merchant or craftsman so as to form a kind of society, does not transfer the ownership of his money to them, for it remains his, so that at his risk the merchant speculates with it”

Applies to: usury; mutuum; damnum emergens; periculum sortis; delayed payment

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-AQU-ST61 Verified

Thomas Aquinas, Summa Theologiae · II-II q.61 (corpus-confirmed; restitution strictly at q.62) — verified: ST II-II q.61 a.1-a.2 corpora (English Dominican trans. 1920, newadvent.org/summa/3061.htm, fetched 2026-07-03) · Scholastic theology

Commutative justice concerns equality in exchanges and restitution between parties.

[a.1 corpus] “This order is directed by commutative justice, which is concerned about the mutual dealings between two persons.” || [a.2 corpus] “it is necessary to equalize thing with thing, so that the one person should pay back to the other just so much as he has become richer out of that which belonged to the other”

Applies to: commutative justice; restorative compensation; damnum emergens

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-AQU-ST77 Verified

Thomas Aquinas, Summa Theologiae · II-II q.77 (corpus-confirmed; a.1 explicitly at Ch.20.4 p.391) — verified: ST II-II q.77 a.1 + a.3 corpora (English Dominican trans. 1920, newadvent.org/summa/3077.htm, fetched 2026-07-03) · Scholastic theology

Buying and selling are morally evaluated by justice in exchange, defects, disclosure, and proportionate gain.

[a.1 corpus] “to sell a thing for more than its worth, or to buy it for less than its worth, is in itself unjust and unlawful.” || [a.3 corpus] “Wherefore if such like defects be hidden, and the seller does not make them known, the sale will be illicit and fraudulent”

Applies to: just price; disclosure; information asymmetry; valuation control

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-ROM-SOCIETAS-LEONINA Draftcited in this dossier

Roman law, Digest · Digest 17.2.29.2 · Roman law / partnership doctrine

A leonine partnership is the paradigm of a partnership-like arrangement in which one party takes gain without sharing loss.

Applies to: upside without downside; surplus asymmetry; societas leonina

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-OLIVI-CAPITALE Draft

Peter John Olivi · Tractatus de contractibus; exact locus to be supplied · Franciscan scholastic theology

Olivi is used as a candidate authority for productive, risk-bearing capital and the distinction between idle money and capital ordered toward commerce.

Applies to: risk-bearing capital; lucrum cessans; periculum sortis; productive capital

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-VIENNE-USURY Draft

Council of Vienne · Usury decrees; exact canon to be supplied · Conciliar / canon law

Candidate authority for the ecclesial seriousness of usury and the need to preserve the distinction between licit compensation and usurious gain.

Applies to: usury; anti-abuse boundary

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-LATERAN-INTER-MULTIPLICES Verified

Fifth Lateran Council · Inter multiplices; montes pietatis (council approval corpus-confirmed; bull name not in corpus) — verified: Lateran V, Session 10, 4 May 1515, definition on the Montes (Tanner trans. via papalencyclicals.net, fetched 2026-07-03; incipit name not printed in fetched source) · Conciliar / magisterial

Candidate authority for the permissibility of charges tied to the labor and expense needed to sustain charitable lending institutions.

[Session 10 definition] “we declare and define, with the approval of the sacred council, that the above-mentioned credit organisations, established by states and hitherto approved and confirmed by the authority of the apostolic see, do not introduce any kind of evil or provide any incentive to sin if they receive, in addition to the capital, a moderate sum for their expenses and by way of compensation, provided it is intended exclusively to defray the expenses of those employed and of other things pertaining (as mentioned) to the upkeep of the organisations, and provided that no profit is made therefrom.”

Applies to: stipendium laboris; expense reimbursement; Monti di Pietà

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-BEN-XIV-VIX-PERVENIT Verified

Benedict XIV · Vix Pervenit (1745; corpus-confirmed) — verified: Vix Pervenit (1745) §3, opinions I and III (papalencyclicals.net English, fetched 2026-07-03) · Papal / magisterial

Candidate authority for maintaining that gain from the loan itself remains usury while genuine extrinsic titles may still be considered.

[§3.I] “The nature of the sin called usury has its proper place and origin in a loan contract. This financial contract between consenting parties demands, by its very nature, that one return to another only as much as he has received.” || [§3.III] “By these remarks, however, We do not deny that at times together with the loan contract certain other titles-which are not at all intrinsic to the contract-may run parallel with it. From these other titles, entirely just and legitimate reasons arise to demand something over and above the amount due on the contract.”

Applies to: usury; extrinsic titles; carentia pecuniae warning

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-LEO-XIII-RN Verified

Leo XIII · Rerum Novarum; exact section to be supplied — verified: Rerum Novarum (1891) §3 (official English, vatican.va, fetched 2026-07-03) · Papal social teaching

Candidate authority for condemning rapacious usury and situating finance within justice for workers and the vulnerable.

[§3] “The mischief has been increased by rapacious usury, which, although more than once condemned by the Church, is nevertheless, under a different guise, but with like injustice, still practiced by covetous and grasping men.”

Applies to: rapacious usury; labor; social justice

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-PIUS-XI-QA Verified

Pius XI · Quadragesimo Anno; exact section to be supplied — verified: Quadragesimo Anno (1931) §§45-49 (official English, vatican.va, fetched 2026-07-03) · Papal social teaching

Candidate authority for ownership as socially conditioned and accountable to the common good.

[§49] “It follows from what We have termed the individual and at the same time social character of ownership, that men must consider in this matter not only their own advantage but also the common good.” || [§45] “neither Leo nor those theologians who have taught under the guidance and authority of the Church have ever denied or questioned the twofold character of ownership, called usually individual or social according as it regards either separate persons or the common good”

Applies to: ownership; social function; governance stewardship

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-JPII-CA Verified

John Paul II · Centesimus Annus; exact section to be supplied — verified: Centesimus Annus (1991) §35 and §43 (official English, vatican.va, fetched 2026-07-03) · Papal social teaching

Candidate authority for the firm as a community of persons rather than a mere capital asset.

[§35] “the purpose of a business firm is not simply to make a profit, but is to be found in its very existence as a community of persons who in various ways are endeavouring to satisfy their basic needs, and who form a particular group at the service of the whole of society.” || [§43] “A business cannot be considered only as a "society of capital goods"; it is also a "society of persons" in which people participate in different ways and with specific responsibilities”

Applies to: firm purpose; mission; governance; labor dignity

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-FRANCIS-LS Verified

Francis · Laudato Si’; exact section to be supplied — verified: Laudato Si’ (2015) §67 (official English, vatican.va, fetched 2026-07-03) · Papal social teaching

Candidate authority for dominion as stewardship, care, and responsibility rather than extractive control.

[§67] “we must forcefully reject the notion that our being created in God's image and given dominion over the earth justifies absolute domination over other creatures.” || [§67] “This implies a relationship of mutual responsibility between human beings and nature.”

Applies to: stewardship; mission lock; commons; control without stewardship

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-OPQ Verified

Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith / Dicastery text · Oeconomicae et pecuniariae quaestiones; exact section to be supplied — verified: Oeconomicae et pecuniariae quaestiones (2018) §21; cf. §26 (official English, vatican.va, fetched 2026-07-03) · Magisterial ethics of finance

Candidate authority for the ethical evaluation of financial instruments, transparency, and the social consequences of finance.

[§21] “The regulations must favor a complete transparency regarding whatever is traded in order to eliminate every form of injustice and inequality, thus assuring the greatest possible equity in the exchange.”

Applies to: financial instruments; transparency; governance; information asymmetry

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-ARIST-POL Verified

Aristotle · Politics I; exact chapter / Bekker locus to be supplied — verified: Politics, Book I, Part X in Jowett trans. (classics.mit.edu, fetched 2026-07-03); Bekker number not printed in fetched source · Classical philosophy

Candidate authority for the distinction between household economy ordered to life and chrematistics ordered to accumulation.

[Politics I, Part X (Jowett)] “The most hated sort, and with the greatest reason, is usury, which makes a gain out of money itself, and not from the natural object of it. For money was intended to be used in exchange, but not to increase at interest.”

Applies to: chrematistics; capital that serves life; accumulation without limit

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-ARIST-NE Verified

Aristotle · Nicomachean Ethics V; exact locus to be supplied — verified: Nicomachean Ethics, Book V, section 5 in Ross trans. (classics.mit.edu, fetched 2026-07-03); Bekker number not printed in fetched source · Classical philosophy

Candidate authority for justice in exchange and proportional or corrective justice.

[NE V.5 (Ross)] “But in associations for exchange this sort of justice does hold men together-reciprocity in accordance with a proportion and not on the basis of precisely equal return.”

Applies to: commutative justice; exchange; proportionality

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

AUTH-CONTRACTUM-TRINUS Draft

Scholastic doctrine of contractum trinus · Exact loci to be supplied · Scholastic contract doctrine

Structural analogy for a stack of formally separate rights that can reassemble equity-like form into debt-like economics or protected yield.

Applies to: clause stacking; participating preference; redemption; cumulative dividend; disguised mutuum

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-ALEXANDER-PERICULUM Draft

Alexander of Alexandria / Alexander Lombard · Tractatus de Usuris, 1307; exact locus to be supplied (title and date corpus-confirmed) · Scholastic theology

Candidate authority for early named treatment of periculum sortis as risk of principal.

Applies to: periculum sortis; risk of capital

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-LUGO-LUCRUM Draft

Juan de Lugo · De iustitia et iure; exact locus to be supplied · Late scholastic theology

Candidate authority for conditions and synthesis around lost profit, risk, and detriment.

Applies to: lucrum cessans; damnum emergens; periculum sortis

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-LESSIUS-CARENTIA Draft

Leonardus Lessius · De iustitia et iure; exact locus to be supplied · Late scholastic theology

Candidate authority for carentia pecuniae and the danger of treating absence of money as a generalized title.

Applies to: carentia pecuniae; liquidity premium; time-value warning

Draft authority paraphrase only. Locus pending verification where applicable.

AUTH-MONTI-PIETA Verified

Monti di Pietà tradition · Fifteenth to sixteenth-century debates; exact loci to be supplied — verified: Primary attestation: Lateran V Session 10 (4 May 1515) recounting and resolving the Monti debate (Tanner trans., fetched 2026-07-03) · Institutional moral finance

Practical institutional model for charitable lending that permits cost recovery without collapsing into profit extraction.

[Session 10 (debate + resolution)] “such a type of lending is meritorious and should be praised and approved. It certainly should not be considered as usurious”

Applies to: stipendium laboris; expense reimbursement; mission-locked finance

Primary-text verified; verification detail lives in the companion Run Verification Report.

Moral-economic analysis. Not legal advice.
